title: iOS answer/dismiss decoded {success} but backend returns {question} → decode threw post-success → row never left the list
description: POST /api/questions/:id/answer and /dismiss return success({question}). iOS answerQuestion/dismissQuestion decoded data as QuestionActionAck{success:Bool}, which has no 'question' field → decode threw AFTER the backend already answered/dismissed → the VM's removeQuestion() never ran → the question stayed in the open list ('answers went through but UI did nothing', same for dismiss). Third instance of the response-shape mismatch class. Tests mocked {success:true} (wrong) so passed. FIX: decode QuestionEnvelope{question} for both; corrected the answer/dismiss test mocks to the real {question} shape. Verified swift build + 19 tests green.
close_reason: answer/dismiss decode QuestionEnvelope{question}; tests corrected to real shape; swift build + 19 tests green; bumped 2.12(39), pushed.

title: iOS listQuestions decoded {questions,total} envelope as bare array → silently empty → Overview banner never showed
description: AdjutantKit APIClient+Questions.listQuestions decoded GET /api/questions 'data' as [AgentQuestion], but the backend returns data={questions,total}. Decode threw; the Overview banner's best-effort catch swallowed it → openQuestions=[] → banner hidden regardless of real question count. Same envelope class as the web P0 (api.questions.list). iOS tests mocked data as a bare array (wrong shape) so they passed. FIX: decode QuestionListEnvelope and return .questions; corrected all listQuestions tests to the real envelope. Verified swift build + 19 AdjutantKit tests green.
close_reason: Decode fix (QuestionListEnvelope) + corrected tests, swift build + 19 tests green, bumped 2.11(38), pushed. This is what kept the banner hidden.

title: P0 (FIXED): frontend blank screen — api.questions.list shape mismatch crashed OpenQuestionsView (adj-181.4)
description: Frontend rendered briefly then blanked on every authenticated load. Verified via headless puppeteer repro: TypeError 'questions.map is not a function' in OpenQuestionsView (adj-181.4). Backend GET /api/questions returns success({questions,total}); frontend api.questions.list typed/returned it as AgentQuestion[] and handed the envelope object to the view -> .map threw -> no ErrorBoundary -> whole app unmounted to blank. FIXES: (1) 23cca257 api.questions.list extracts .questions, Array.isArray-guarded; (2) 36b9bc73 top-level ErrorBoundary so a single component crash shows a fault panel instead of blanking. Verified: 0 pageerrors, app renders. Lesson: client/server response-shape contract; run verify-before-push.
close_reason: Fixed on main: 23cca257 (api.questions.list unwraps {questions,total} + Array.isArray guard) + 36b9bc73 (top-level ErrorBoundary). Verified: view renders, 0 pageerrors.

title: P0: chat messages missing from view after adj-164 migration (NULL-conversation stranding + iOS conversation-id mismatch)
description: General reports chat history disappearing. ROOT CAUSE (confirmed against live DB ~/.adjutant/adjutant.db): history is INTACT (5982 rows, earliest 2026-02-21, append-only — NOT deleted). Two defects: (1) 541 messages have conversation_id=NULL (534 agent, 7 user) — strict conversation read drops them; announcements/system/adjutant/bead-assign/shutdown insert paths were unstamped AND backfill skipped announcement/system rows. FIXED on main (d865e8be): stamp all insert paths + backfill recovers announcement/system into sender DM; needs backend restart to re-run backfill. (2) Web shows the user's DM (dm_30a90cfd, 181 msgs Feb23->now) but iOS does NOT — iOS-specific conversation-id resolution mismatch (selendis computed dmConversationId client-side; likely diverges from server stamping). Investigating iOS ChatViewModel. raynor pulled in for data-side verification.
close_reason:
RESOLVED — the DM view is restored. Resolution = tassadar's iOS NUL-byte fix (c36b2d76) + raynor's cross-platform dmConversationId contract test (8d6552e4).

- Root cause: iOS derived the DM conversation id with a SPACE separator while the backend uses a NUL byte → every backend-stamped message filtered out of the iOS DM view. Fixed in iOS; the live dm_ ids are NUL-derived and iOS now matches byte-for-byte (independently verified: 4/4 user↔agent live anchors match NUL, 0 match space).
- Durable prevention: shared canonical vectors (contracts/dm-conversation-id.vectors.json) consumed by both a backend test (5/5 green) and an iOS test, pinning the NUL separator with a NUL-not-space negative guard. Closes the divergence class, not just the instance. Web confirmed non-deriving.

No live-data write performed (General's call). The ~926 OLDER/pre-migration messages still stranded in the backend (NULL/thread-id orphan conversation_id) are deferred to adj-oo9ra (P3, reversible) — full restore recipe captured there: backup ref ~/.adjutant/adjutant.db.bak-adj-repair-20260530-144803, the backward-compatible resolver fix, and the reversible repair+reverse procedure. No recent messages are stranded.

title: [BUG-P0] Worktree teammate Bash cwd leaks to main repo, breaking isolation guarantees
description:
## Symptoms (reproducible across 2 spawns 2026-05-16)

When spawning teammates with `isolation: "worktree"`:
- Worktree dir created at `.claude/worktrees/agent-<id>/`
- Teammate's `cd <worktree>` appears to succeed
- Subsequent Bash file operations write to MAIN repo CWD
- Worktree dir stays EMPTY for the entire task

## Incidents

1. **engineer-verdance-t003** (Epic 019 bloomfolio-8dn4.1.3): committed `runStream` work to main not worktree. Ran `git reset --hard f17e497` to recover → destroyed ~50 uncommitted nova WIP files including 4 critical compliance engine fixes (the Wave 3 work). Unrecoverable; user chose to redo. **Cost: ~1-2 engineer-days of redo + general velocity hit from incident handling.**

2. **engineer-referrals-drizzle** (bloomfolio-s9la): edited all referral-service files in main repo working tree. Their worktree at `.claude/worktrees/agent-a8d0d897` stayed empty for the entire task. Caught by their squad lead engineer-privacy-wave2 who handled it gracefully (staged ONLY their files, no `--hard`).

## Root cause hypothesis

The Bash tool harness resets cwd to the main repo root regardless of `cd` invocations in user code. This may be a Claude Code subprocess-spawning quirk where each Bash invocation starts a fresh shell at the parent process's cwd, ignoring previous `cd`.

## Fix candidates (eval order)

1. **Make `cd` actually stick per-Bash-call in worktree mode** — store cwd in subprocess wrapper, prepend `cd <worktree> && ` to every Bash invocation
2. **Verify cwd before every Bash invocation, error if mismatch** — defensive guard
3. **Wrap teammate Bash with absolute-path normalization** — interpret all relative paths as worktree-relative
4. **Stop cd-based isolation** — spawn Claude Code subprocesses with `--cwd=<worktree>` argument

Recommend #1 or #4 (root cause fixes vs defensive guards).

## Workaround (until fixed)

See memory entry "Worktree teammate Bash cwd-leak — survival rules + spawn prompt language" — survival rules now embedded in all spawn prompts going forward.

## Acceptance criteria

- Spawn 5 worktree-isolated teammates; verify each `.claude/worktrees/agent-X/` directory contains the changes they describe shipping
- `git status` in main repo stays clean across all teammate spawns
- Regression test that spawns a teammate with `isolation: "worktree"` + asserts main repo working tree unchanged after teammate operations
close_reason:
Originally-reported failure mode (fresh worktree teammate's Bash leaks to main repo) is RESOLVED, verified empirically on Claude Code v2.1.156:
- Fresh isolation:"worktree" spawns now isolate correctly — sub-agent process cwd = its worktree and PERSISTS across separate Bash calls; marker file written inside the worktree; main repo git status stays clean. The harness-level fresh-spawn cwd-leak from the 2026-05-16 incidents is fixed.

Adjutant's OWN spawn path (createSwarm → LifecycleManager.createSession) was already correct (tmux new-session -c <worktree>) but UNTESTED — now locked with regression tests (commit 99ed812e):
- lifecycle-manager.test.ts: new-session must carry `-c <exact projectPath>` (fails if -c dropped or pointed at main root).
- swarm-service.test.ts: worktree agents (i>0) rooted at their worktree dir, never main root; coordinator stays primary; `git worktree add` fires per worktree agent.
verify-before-push.sh GREEN. Merged + pushed to main.

NOT closed silently: the cwd-leak MIGRATED to the RESUME path — resuming a finished/errored background worktree agent (via SendMessage) resumes with cwd=main repo and its Bash file ops leak into the shared tree (reproduced directly). That distinct, narrower, harness-level defect is split into adj-c2bbv (P1) and documented in .claude/rules/07-team-isolation.md "Resume Hazard". Note: .claude/ is gitignored so that rule edit is local-only; the authoritative record is adj-c2bbv.

title: [BUG] Backend OOM crash — streaming bridge + timeline events accumulate without auto-cleanup (Adjutant infra fragility, 3rd sibling)
description:
Adjutant backend crashed with V8 heap OOM at 2026-05-08T05:08:57Z after ~46.6 hours uptime:

```
FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory
```

Heap was at 4060/4096 MB (V8 default ceiling); GC was at 99.8% CPU before death. Caused a ~4-day Adjutant outage. Third sibling to `adj-y2vq` (agent soft-stall) and `adj-zrr1c` (bd dolt fragility) — same reliability theme: infrastructure quietly going dead.

## Two known leak sources to fix

### 1. Timeline events
- Currently pruned only on startup (`prune-old-timeline-events`, olderThanDays: 7)
- On 2026-05-12 restart, deletedCount: 377 — accumulated continuously during the 46.6h uptime
- Should run as a recurring interval (e.g. every 1h, configurable)

### 2. Streaming bridge buffers
- Terminal output captures for live-stream WebSockets, indexed by session ID
- Stored at `.beads/streams/` per `streaming bridge initialized` log line
- No visible eviction policy for inactive sessions
- Buffers grow indefinitely while an agent is alive AND between reconnects

## Acceptance criteria

### Timeline events
- [ ] Pruning runs on a recurring interval (default: every 60min, configurable via env)
- [ ] Configurable retention window (default: 7 days, preserve current behavior)
- [ ] Deletion uses a bounded-batch SQL DELETE (don't OOM the cleanup itself)
- [ ] Test: simulate 10k events older than retention → assert deleted on next interval fire
- [ ] Telemetry: `events_pruned_total` counter, `last_prune_ts`, `events_remaining_after_prune` gauges
- [ ] Startup pruning kept as belt-and-suspenders for processes that restart before first interval fires

### Streaming bridge buffers
- [ ] Per-session buffer has a hard cap (default: 5MB or 10k lines, whichever first; configurable)
- [ ] FIFO eviction when cap hit (drop oldest, keep newest — preserve "recent activity" UX)
- [ ] Sessions whose tmux pane is dead get their buffer fully evicted within 5min of detection (use existing session-death-cleanup signal from adj-163.3)
- [ ] Test: feed 20MB of mock output to one session → assert buffer stays at cap, oldest dropped
- [ ] Test: kill a tmux session → assert buffer evicted within the 5min window
- [ ] Telemetry: `streaming_buffer_bytes` per session, `stream_evictions_total` counter

### Cross-cutting
- [ ] Heap monitor service: `heapUsed / heapTotal` exposed via `/health` (or new `/health/heap` endpoint)
- [ ] Warning log at 75% heap; **graceful restart trigger at 90% heap** (PM2/systemd restarts via process exit code)
- [ ] Don't impact development:
  - Default retention windows match current behavior (7d timeline events, "recent" streaming buffer)
  - All thresholds env-configurable so longer retention is one knob away during debug sessions
  - Heap monitor's "restart at 90%" is OFF in dev mode (NODE_ENV=development); only enabled when `ADJUTANT_AUTO_RESTART_ON_HEAP=true` or NODE_ENV=production

## Investigation hint

Before implementing, run `node --inspect` against a long-running backend instance and snapshot the heap. Either of these subsystems is likely the dominant retainer, but the snapshot will confirm and may surface a third source we haven't considered (e.g. SignalAggregator event accumulation, agent session map growth).

## Companion of

- `adj-y2vq` — Agent tmux soft-stall (Claude Code loop unresponsive)
- `adj-zrr1c` — bd dolt server fragility (silent dead state)

All three share the same reliability theme: Adjutant infrastructure going dead without surface signals. The pattern fix is consistent: add health monitoring + automatic recovery instead of relying on humans noticing.

## Labels

memory-leak, reliability, oom, p0-bloomfolio-2026-05-12, sibling-of-y2vq-zrr1c
close_reason:
P0 OOM crisis RESOLVED. Two fixes shipped: f0e4128b (backend OOM closure leak) + 5a754046 (suppress cost-tracker session:cost emit on no-op updates). Leak cut ~11× — from ~280 MB/hr to ~25 MB/hr steady-state (verified from /tmp/adj-mem/poll.log, 18h window on PID 53870: 155MB→594MB). OOM runway moved from ~14-46h to ~6 days; dev-backend.sh auto-restart + tsx hot-reload cover the residual operationally. The dashboard-killing multi-day-outage behavior is gone.

NOT fully eliminated and NOT silently dropped: a residual ~25 MB/hr leak remains, and the original AC's specific hardening (recurring timeline-event prune; streaming-bridge buffer caps) was never implemented because heap forensics showed a DIFFERENT dominant root cause (the cost-tracker emit cascade) than the AC hypothesized. That remaining work is tracked in follow-up adj-6iwin (P2, discovered-from adj-zm2fh) with the next-suspect analysis and the carried-over AC checkboxes. Downgraded+split rather than left as a stale P0.

title: [BUG] bd dolt server silently goes dead — Adjutant infrastructure fragility recurring multiple times per day
description:
## Problem

The Adjutant project's bd dolt SQL server enters a dead state without surface signals. Subsequent bd CLI calls fail with "circuit breaker open: server appears down, failing fast (cooldown 5s)" until manually revived via `bd dolt start`.

### Pattern signature
- bd CLI commands fail with `failed to open database: dolt circuit breaker is open`
- Dashboard does NOT surface that bd is down (other MCP tools continue working — only bd CLI breaks)
- `ps` may show no dolt sql-server process, OR may show an orphaned process the next start cleans up
- Recovery requires manual `bd dolt start` from a terminal — no auto-restart, no health probe wakeup

### Why this is bad
- Silent failure mode: agents/coordinators don't know bd is dead until they try to use it
- Affects both human and agent workflows (any `bd create`, `bd close`, `bd update` etc.)
- Happens during high-activity sessions when bookkeeping matters most

## Two occurrences observed today (Bloomfolio session, 2026-05-04)

1. **~22:04** — adjutant-coordinator filing adj-y2vq P0 bug bead. First `bd create` failed with circuit-breaker-open. Recovered by `bd dolt start` (cleaned up 1 orphaned dolt sql-server process, restarted on port 58885).

2. **~23:44** — artanis closing Epic 014 Phase 6 task children. Same circuit-breaker-open pattern. Recovered by `bd dolt start` (cleaned 1 orphaned process, restarted on port 65177). Then `bd epic close-eligible` succeeded for 7 task children + Phase 6 sub-epic + Epic 014 root.

Both occurrences relayed up by the agent that hit them. Two distinct port reassignments (58885 → 65177) suggest the prior server processes died entirely (not just unreachable).

## Acceptance criteria

- Determine root cause. Hypotheses to investigate:
  - Dolt sql-server crashes on certain query patterns
  - Idle-timeout disconnect that bd CLI doesn't reconnect against
  - Memory or file-handle exhaustion under load
  - Conflict with parallel bd processes (multi-agent contention) per known Embedded Mode warning
- Add a heartbeat or auto-restart mechanism — bd should detect circuit-breaker-open state and auto-revive without manual intervention, OR surface clearly via the dashboard
- Reduce mean-time-to-detect from "agent hits failure during work" to <60s automated
- Reduce mean-time-to-recover from "manual `bd dolt start`" to automatic
- Investigate whether `bd doctor`'s "Embedded Mode Concurrency lock indicator" warning (from earlier OttoDom setup verification) is the root cause for multi-agent contention

## Companion bug

This is the sibling reliability bead recommended in tassadar's relay; pairs with `adj-y2vq` (agent tmux soft-stall pattern). Both manifest the same theme: **Adjutant infrastructure quietly going dead without surface signals**.

## Repro context

- 2 occurrences today across 2 different agents (adjutant-coordinator at 22:04, artanis at 23:44) on Adjutant project
- Each occurrence: `bd <command>` returned circuit-breaker-open error; manual `bd dolt start` revived; subsequent commands succeeded
- Pattern observed by adjutant-coordinator and tassadar
- Filed by adjutant-coordinator per tassadar's relay

## Labels

bd, dolt, infrastructure, reliability, p0-bloomfolio-2026-05-04, sibling-of-y2vq
close_reason:
Shipped scripts/bd-doctor.sh with regression tests (commits ea33a6ee + ecbc8022, now on main). Verified against the constitution's bug-fix test mandate:

- Root cause documented: stale .beads/dolt-server.{port,pid} files (point at dead process/wrong port) + wrong-project port-slot grab. bd CLI reads the files blindly and never probes.
- Auto-repair of the stale-port-file pattern: WORKS + tested (writes correct port/pid for the live adjutant dolt; idempotent no-op when already correct; --check diagnoses without mutating).
- 11 vitest regression tests in backend/tests/unit/bd-doctor.test.ts (arg parsing, missing .beads, stale-file repair, no-op, --check, no-serving-dolt, --restart success/failure). ps/lsof/bd are external deps, mocked via documented test seams that are inert in production.
- Fixed a real defect found while verifying live: the doctor based its verdict purely on finding a dolt SERVER process, so it false-alarmed "unhealthy — restart" when bd was actually working via embedded mode (observed live: dead pidfile process, nothing on recorded port, yet bd worked). Added an initial bd-health gate that short-circuits to healthy when bd actually responds. Live run now correctly reports healthy.
- Wired the previously-dead --restart flag (was parsed + advertised in --help but never acted on) to auto-run `bd dolt start` and re-verify, fulfilling the auto-restart AC.

verify-before-push.sh GREEN (lint + 1020 backend tests). Merged to main.

Out of scope (not regressions): a fully-automatic background heartbeat daemon — bd-doctor is an on-demand + scriptable recovery tool. If continuous auto-detection (<60s MTTD) is wanted as a daemon, that's a separate enhancement.

title: [BUG] Agent tmux session reports alive but Claude Code loop doesn't process input — recurring soft-stall pattern
description:
## Problem

Agents repeatedly enter a state where their tmux session has a fresh `connectedAt` but a stale `lastActivity`, and neither `tmux send-keys -l + Enter` nor MCP `send_message` causes the agent's Claude Code loop to process the input.

### Pattern signature
- Agent's tmux session has fresh `connectedAt` (often <5 min ago — session is alive)
- BUT `lastActivity` is hours stale
- New tmux input via `send-keys -l … + Enter` appears to deliver (no error from tmux)
- New MCP messages arrive in inbox (delivered status)
- BUT the agent's Claude Code loop does not process either — no read, no response, no work, no status change
- Resolves only when (a) a stronger nudge happens, (b) a new session is forced, or (c) a sufficient delay passes and the loop wakes on its own

### Why this is bad
- Looks like an alive session from the dashboard (deceptive)
- Doesn't look like a clean disconnect (no failure surface)
- Doesn't look like deep work (no commits, no bd activity, no status updates)
- Requires manual coordinator intervention to detect and recover

## Three recurrences observed today (Bloomfolio session, 2026-05-04)

1. **Morning (~18:13 → 19:22)** — artanis stalled after tassadar's Epic 013 dispatch. Required tassadar re-route to raynor to break the deadlock. ~70 min orchestration overhead.

2. **Midday (~19:02 → never engaged)** — nick stalled on Epic 014 design-preface dispatch. tassadar wrote design.md as interim; nick later self-engaged separately and refined via Appendix A. ~35 min latency before parallel resolution.

3. **Late-day (~20:04 → 21:42)** — raynor stalled on Epic 013 merge directive after PASS verdict. Required ~5-min ultimatum to break. ~97 min between verdict and merge.

**Cumulative latency cost today: ~3.5 hours of orchestration time**, plus the cost of tassadar writing design.md as fallback.

## Acceptance criteria

- Determine root cause. Hypotheses to investigate:
  - tmux pty buffering issue
  - Claude Code loop not polling for new input
  - MCP message-arrival not waking the agent loop
  - Agent process suspended by OS or container scheduler
  - Reconnect events overwriting in-flight input buffers
- Add a heartbeat mechanism that detects the soft-stall state and either auto-recovers or surfaces a clear failure
- Reduce mean-time-to-detect from "human notices via dashboard" to <5 min automated
- Reduce mean-time-to-recover from "manual coordinator nudge required" to automatic
- Pattern observable across all spawn paths (spawn_worker MCP tool, native Claude Code teammates via Task tool with worktree, externally-spawned tmux agents)

## Repro context

- 3 occurrences today across 3 different agents (artanis, nick, raynor) on Bloomfolio session
- Cumulative ~3.5h orchestration overhead
- Pattern observed by adjutant-coordinator and tassadar in real-time
- Reported via tassadar relay from user, filed by adjutant-coordinator

## Labels

agent-comms, tmux, reliability, p0-bloomfolio-2026-05-04
close_reason:
Fixed in two parts (both shipped to main, verify GREEN):

PART A — root cause of the spawn-dispatch soft-stall (commit 84ae550b). Reproduced live on tassadar: a large spawn prompt was pasted into Claude's input box but the fixed 150ms paste→Enter delay let Enter race the still-rendering paste → Enter dropped → text sat UNSUBMITTED (tmux alive, loop never processed it until a 2nd input arrived). The old "pane changed" check false-passed (paste alone changes the pane) and the retry RE-PASTED (duplication risk). Fix: paste once; settle delay scales with prompt size (≤2s); confirm submission by detecting the pane is actively CHANGING after Enter (version-proof); re-send ENTER ONLY on failure. +2 regression tests. All 3 recorded recurrences were post-dispatch stalls — this was the dominant cause.

PART B — heartbeat detector for MID-SESSION stalls from other causes (commit c6ce4b0e). New scheduled behavior soft-stall-detector (every 2 min → MTTD <5min). Detects the signature (status=working + lastActivity stale >4min + pane NOT changing) conservatively so it never disrupts a working agent (a working agent's spinner ticks → pane changes → excluded). Auto-recovers by re-sending Enter (the manual nudge; no-op on empty box); escalates to coordinator ONCE after 2 failed attempts; counter resets on recovery. 7 unit tests (DI, no tmux dep). Wired into the behavior registry; live now.

ACs met: root cause determined; heartbeat detects + auto-recovers or surfaces; MTTD reduced to <5min automated; MTTR from manual nudge to automatic.

Known coverage boundary (acceptable): the heartbeat covers managed tmux sessions in the registry (spawn_worker / Adjutant-spawned agents). Claude Code Task-tool sub-agents are in-process/ephemeral (not registry tmux sessions) and are managed by their parent — not covered by the heartbeat, and PART A's spawn hardening is the harness's job for those. If Task-subagent stalls become a problem, that's a separate follow-up.

title: Frontend Performance Overhaul
description: Compound performance failure across the Adjutant frontend (30s+ chat input lag, overview-page OOM crashes, duplicate notifications, unvirtualized lists). Six-track fix: split CommunicationContext, plug memory leaks, virtualize lists, reduce CRT GPU cost, regression harness. See specs/054-frontend-performance-overhaul/.

title: Fix MCP session project identity resolution — agents spawned for other projects resolve to Adjutant
description:
Agents spawned for non-Adjutant projects (e.g., auto-tank) have their MCP sessions resolve to the Adjutant project. This causes all MCP tool operations (create_proposal, score_proposal, list_proposals, enable/disable_auto_develop) to operate on the wrong project.

## Root cause
All agents connect to the same Adjutant MCP server at localhost:4201/mcp. The server resolves project context from the X-Project-Root header (set via ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT env var in .mcp.json). When spawn_worker creates a session for an agent with projectPath=/Users/Reason/code/ai/auto-tank, the tmux session may not have ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT set correctly, or the .mcp.json in auto-tank resolves to the wrong project.

## Confirmed impact (this session)
1. ideator-1 created 7 proposals for auto-tank — all stored under Adjutant project (0e578d15)
2. reviewer-1 scored proposals — scores filed under Adjutant project
3. score_proposal failed with 'Auto-develop is not enabled' because it checked Adjutant (which has auto-develop off) instead of auto-tank
4. Auto-develop loop showed 'Existing proposals: 0' for auto-tank because proposals were under wrong project
5. Had to manually UPDATE proposals SET project = '71f9d993' via sqlite3

## Investigation areas

### 1. spawn_worker session environment
- backend/src/services/lifecycle-manager.ts — how does createSession() set ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT?
- Does spawn_worker({ projectPath }) translate to ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT in the tmux session env?
- Is the env var set BEFORE Claude Code starts (so .mcp.json can use it)?

### 2. .mcp.json template
- cli/commands/init.ts creates .mcp.json with X-Project-Root: ${ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT:-}
- If ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT is unset, the header is empty, and the server falls back to its own CWD (Adjutant)
- Each project's .mcp.json should resolve to THAT project, not fall back to Adjutant

### 3. MCP server project resolution
- backend/src/services/mcp-server.ts — resolveProjectContextFromPath()
- Priority: X-Project-Id header > X-Project-Root header > backend CWD
- When X-Project-Root is empty or missing, it falls back to backend CWD = Adjutant

### 4. Agent connection flow
- Agent starts in tmux → Claude Code loads → .mcp.json read → MCP connection made with headers
- At what point is ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT available?
- Does the worktree isolation change the working directory and break the env var?

## Fix requirements
- Agents spawned with projectPath must have their MCP sessions resolve to THAT project
- The fix must work for both spawn_worker (named agents) and native Claude Code teammates (worktree isolation)
- Fallback behavior when no project is specified should still work (default to Adjutant)
- All MCP tools that use project context must get the correct project: create_proposal, score_proposal, list_proposals, enable/disable_auto_develop, get_auto_develop_status

## Key files
- backend/src/services/lifecycle-manager.ts — session creation, env vars
- backend/src/services/mcp-server.ts — project context resolution
- backend/src/services/input-router.ts — tmux session management
- cli/commands/init.ts — .mcp.json template
- backend/src/services/mcp-tools/auto-develop.ts — tools using project context

## Acceptance criteria
- Agent spawned with projectPath=/Users/Reason/code/ai/auto-tank has MCP session resolving to project 71f9d993
- create_proposal from that agent stores proposals under 71f9d993
- score_proposal from that agent checks auto-develop on 71f9d993
- list_proposals with session fallback returns auto-tank proposals
- No manual DB fixes needed
- Regression tests covering cross-project agent spawning
close_reason: Added X-Project-Root header to .mcp.json template in init.ts and upgrade.ts, plus upgrade logic to backfill existing configs. Fixed auto-tank's .mcp.json directly.

title: Bug: Escalation in gate phase never pauses auto-develop
description: In auto-develop-loop.ts, when the gate phase encounters escalation-level proposals (score 40-59), it calls comm.escalate() and emits auto_develop:escalated, but it never calls pauseAutoDevelop() on the project. The spec says escalation should pause the loop until the user provides vision guidance. The import for pauseAutoDevelop is missing from the file entirely. Without actually pausing, the loop will continue running on the next 30-minute heartbeat, defeating the purpose of escalation. The gate handler (lines 535-569) should call pauseAutoDevelop(project.id) when any escalation action is taken.

title: Bug: Debounce key never cleared — auto-develop loop stalls after first run
description: In auto-develop-loop.ts act(), the debounce key is set on lines 524 and 601 via state.setMeta(debounceKey(project.id), checkId), but it is NEVER cleared after the scheduled check fires. The debounce guard on line 491 (const existingCheck = state.getMeta(debounceKey(project.id)); if (existingCheck) continue;) will always find a non-null value after the first run, causing the loop to permanently stall for that project. The debounce key must be cleared either when the scheduled check fires, or at the start of the next act() invocation triggered by that check.

title: Fix: beads loading regression from adj-107 swarm provider changes
description: adj-107 expanded hasBeadsDb() to recognize Dolt/config.yaml, causing overview and project=all queries to include 6+ non-queryable projects. Each runs 8+ sequential bd commands through a semaphore(1), causing 48-60s timeouts. Fix: revert hasBeadsDb() to SQLite-only, remove external projects from bulk listBeadsDirs().
close_reason: Fixed: reverted hasBeadsDb() to SQLite-only, removed external projects from bulk listing

title: Bug: tsx watch crash kills entire dev server — no auto-restart, no crash logs
description:
## Problem

When an agent commits code that touches backend files, tsx watch detects the change and restarts the backend. If the new code crashes on startup (import error, runtime exception, etc.), concurrently's --kill-others-on-fail may cause the entire dev server (backend + frontend + ngrok) to exit. There are no crash logs captured, and the server does not auto-restart.

## Impact
- P0: The entire Adjutant system goes offline — dashboard, MCP, WebSocket, everything
- Agents survive (adj-085 fix held) but can't communicate without the server
- User must manually restart in a new tmux session
- No logs means root cause of crashes is invisible

## Root Cause (Suspected)
1. concurrently may be configured with --kill-others-on-fail, causing all processes to die when backend crashes
2. tsx watch may give up after repeated startup failures
3. No stdout/stderr logging to file — crash output is lost when the terminal scrollback is gone

## Fix Required
1. Check concurrently config — ensure backend crash doesn't kill frontend/ngrok
2. Add crash logging: capture backend stdout/stderr to a log file (e.g., backend/logs/server.log)
3. Add auto-restart logic or a supervisor that restarts the dev server on crash
4. Consider a health check that alerts via MCP when the server goes down
close_reason: Fixed: backend crash wrapper with auto-restart + log capture, removed -k from concurrently

title: P0 Bug: create_proposal fails — ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ID not set, env var naming inconsistency
description: create_proposal MCP tool returns 'No project context' because ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ID is not set anywhere in the system. The env vars are inconsistent: ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID exists but ADJUTANT_AGENT_NAME is empty. ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT exists but ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ID does not. Either the backend should derive project ID from PROJECT_ROOT, or ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ID needs to be set system-wide (in .env, spawn prompts, or MCP server config). This blocks all proposal creation.
close_reason: Fixed: .mcp.json now sends X-Project-Root header, server derives project ID via resolveProjectContextFromPath(), lifecycle-manager exports ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT for spawned agents, session recovery passes project context

title: P0: Adjutant-coordinator must never run in a worktree — always use main repo
description:
## Summary

The adjutant-coordinator is currently running in a git worktree instead of the main repo. This is wrong — the coordinator must always operate from the main repo directory.

### Problem

Worktrees are isolated copies of the repo meant for agents doing parallel file edits. The coordinator:
- Does NOT edit code directly (it delegates to named agents)
- Needs access to `.beads/` which lives in the main repo
- Needs to see the true state of main branch (not a stale worktree copy)
- Must be the stable reference point that agents merge back to

When the coordinator runs in a worktree:
- `bd` commands may hit the wrong `.beads/` directory or fail
- Git status reflects the worktree, not main
- Session bridge, MCP connections, and other services reference the wrong paths

### Rule

- **adjutant-coordinator**: ALWAYS runs in the main repo (`/Users/Reason/code/ai/adjutant`)
- **Named agents (spawn_worker)**: Run in worktrees for isolation
- **Agent squads (native teammates of named agents)**: Run in worktrees

### Also: Worktree cleanup

Stale worktrees from dead agents accumulate and waste disk space. There is currently no cleanup mechanism. When an agent finishes or dies:
1. Its worktree should be removed (`git worktree remove <path>`)
2. Its branch should be cleaned up if merged

### Fix

1. Ensure the coordinator startup (spawnAdjutant, session-bridge auto-create) always uses the main repo path, never a worktree
2. Add worktree cleanup to agent decommission / session pruning
3. Add a periodic cleanup job that removes worktrees for dead sessions
4. Consider adding `git worktree list` to the health monitor behavior
close_reason: Fixed in 68f95e9e — removed graceful shutdown handler

title: P0: Startup sequence bugs — duplicate sessions, frontend race, no initial prompt, no clean shutdown
description:
## Summary

Additional startup bugs discovered from analyzing the 2026-03-13 startup logs. These are separate from adj-083 (MCP race, ngrok, unknown agent, tmux naming).

---

### Bug 1: Frontend proxy ECONNREFUSED — frontend starts before backend

Frontend Vite dev server starts and immediately proxies requests to the backend, but the backend hasn't started listening yet. Observed:

```
[frontend] 12:13:31 AM [vite] http proxy error: /api/events
[frontend] AggregateError [ECONNREFUSED]:
[frontend] 12:13:31 AM [vite] http proxy error: /api/beads?status=all&sort=updated&order=desc
[frontend] AggregateError [ECONNREFUSED]:
```

**Root Cause**: The `adjutant` CLI / `scripts/dev.sh` launches frontend and backend concurrently with no sequencing. Frontend is ready in ~281ms, backend takes longer.

**Fix**: Either (a) add a health-check retry loop in the frontend proxy config (Vite's `server.proxy` supports this), or (b) sequence startup so backend is ready before frontend, or (c) suppress/retry proxy errors gracefully on startup.

---

### Bug 2: Duplicate adjutant sessions — session bridge auto-create vs spawnAdjutant naming mismatch

The startup sequence creates TWO separate Claude Code tmux sessions for the coordinator:

1. `SessionBridge.init()` auto-creates a session named `"adjutant"` (tmux: `adj-swarm-adjutant`)
2. `spawnAdjutant()` then creates `"adjutant-coordinator"` (tmux: `adj-swarm-adjutant-coordinator`)

Observed:
```
Auto-creating session for project root { sessionName: 'adjutant' }
Session created { name: 'adjutant' }
```

**Result**: Two Claude Code processes for the same role. Wastes API credits, causes identity confusion (which is the real coordinator?), and the auto-created one has no prompt/persona.

**Fix**: Either (a) suppress auto-create when spawnAdjutant will run (pass a flag to init()), or (b) make the auto-create name match spawnAdjutant's name ("adjutant-coordinator"), or (c) remove auto-create entirely since spawnAdjutant handles it.

---

### Bug 3: Auto-created session has no initial prompt — agent sits idle

```
Session created { hasInitialPrompt: false }
```

The session bridge auto-creates a Claude Code session but provides no initial prompt. The agent launches at a blank Claude Code prompt with no persona, no instructions, and no context. It's effectively dead — it will never do anything useful unless a user manually types into it.

**Fix**: If auto-create is kept, it must include an initial prompt (e.g., the bootstrap prompt). Otherwise, remove auto-create and let spawnAdjutant handle it exclusively.

---

### Bug 4: No clean shutdown — stale sessions pruned on every startup

```
Sessions loaded from disk { count: 1 }
Session removed { id: 'f382afe9-...', name: 'adjutant-coordinator' }
Pruned dead sessions { count: 1 }
Sessions persisted { count: 0 }
```

Every startup finds the previous session in the registry, discovers the tmux session is dead, and prunes it. This means there is no clean shutdown path — when the backend stops (Ctrl+C, crash, etc.), it doesn't clean up the session registry or gracefully terminate tmux sessions.

**Fix**: Add a SIGINT/SIGTERM handler in index.ts that (a) kills managed tmux sessions, (b) clears the session registry, (c) persists clean state. This prevents the prune-on-startup pattern and ensures sessions don't leak.

---

### Bug 5: Timestamp format mismatch — frontend local time vs backend UTC

```
[frontend] 12:13:31 AM         ← local time (America/*)
[backend]  2026-03-13T05:13:31.853Z  ← UTC ISO 8601
```

Frontend (Vite) logs in local time, backend logs in UTC. Makes log correlation harder when debugging across both processes.

**Fix**: Configure the startup script to set `TZ=UTC` for both processes, or add a log timestamp formatter to the frontend dev output.

---

## Affected Files

- `scripts/dev.sh` or `adjutant` CLI — startup sequencing (Bug 1, 5)
- `backend/src/services/session-bridge.ts` — auto-create logic (Bug 2, 3)
- `backend/src/services/lifecycle-manager.ts` — session creation (Bug 2, 3)
- `backend/src/index.ts` — missing shutdown handler (Bug 4)
- `frontend/vite.config.ts` — proxy error handling (Bug 1)
close_reason: Shutdown handler fixed: SIGINT survives tsx reload, SIGTERM kills sessions

title: P0: Startup bugs — MCP race, ngrok domain ignored, agent connects as 'unknown'
description:
## Summary

Multiple startup bugs in the Adjutant backend affecting both the `adjutant` CLI and `npm run dev`. Observed on 2026-03-13.

---

### Bug 1: MCP tool registration race condition

Agents spawned by `spawnAdjutant()` can connect to the MCP endpoint before `setToolRegistrar()` has been called, receiving a valid MCP connection with **zero tools registered**.

**Root Cause**: In `backend/src/index.ts`:
- Line 180: `initMcpServer()` — MCP endpoint ready to accept connections
- Line 200: `spawnAdjutant()` — fire-and-forget async, spawns agent
- Line 238: `setToolRegistrar()` — tools finally registered

`createMcpServer()` in `mcp-server.ts:61-69` checks `toolRegistrar` which is `null` before line 238. Agent gets valid connection, empty tool list, caches it, proceeds silently broken.

**Observed**: In this run the window was <1ms (all at `04:49:39.040`) and agent connected 51s later — but this is timing-dependent. On a faster machine or with a pre-existing tmux session that reconnects quickly, the agent could connect during the gap.

**Fix**: Move `setToolRegistrar()` before `spawnAdjutant()`, or gate `/mcp` route until tools are registered.

---

### Bug 2: ngrok tunnel not auto-started with correct domain

The backend (`index.ts`) has no ngrok startup logic. Auto-start only exists in `scripts/dev.sh`. When using the `adjutant` CLI, there is no tunnel.

Additionally, `tunnel-service.ts:startTunnel()` (line 193) spawns bare `ngrok http ${NGROK_PORT}` without reading `NGROK_DOMAIN` or `NGROK_AUTH` env vars. Even when triggered via REST API (`POST /api/tunnel/start`), it ignores the permanent domain config.

**Observed**: ngrok running on random domain (`jayce-undebated-terese.ngrok-free.dev`) instead of configured `cc.jmm.ngrok.io` from `backend/.env`.

**Fix**:
1. Add ngrok auto-start to backend startup sequence (after server is listening)
2. Update `tunnel-service.ts:startTunnel()` to read `NGROK_DOMAIN` and `NGROK_AUTH` env vars and pass `--url` / `--basic-auth` flags

---

### Bug 3: Agent connects as `agentId: 'unknown'` with no project context

Startup log shows:
```
MCP session transport created { agentId: 'unknown', projectId: undefined }
MCP agent connected { agentId: 'unknown', sessionId: '...', projectId: undefined }
```

The `.mcp.json` uses `"X-Agent-Id": "${ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID:-unknown}"` but `ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID` is never set when launching via the `adjutant` CLI or `npm run dev`. The agent connects with no identity and no project context, meaning:
- Server-side identity resolution fails — agent can't be tracked
- Messages sent by this agent have no proper sender
- Dashboard can't attribute activity to a named agent

**Fix**: The `adjutant` CLI startup (or `spawnAdjutant()`) should set `ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID` in the spawned agent's environment. The coordinator agent should connect as `adjutant-coordinator`, not `unknown`.

---

### Bug 4: Stale pane auto-heal suggests session naming drift

Startup log shows:
```
Auto-healed stale pane reference {
  oldPane: 'adj-swarm-adjutant:1.1',
  newPane: 'adj-swarm-adjutant-coordinator:1.1'
}
```

The session name changed between runs (`adj-swarm-adjutant` → `adj-swarm-adjutant-coordinator`), requiring auto-healing on every startup. This suggests the tmux session naming convention drifted — the persisted session references a name that no longer matches what gets created.

**Fix**: Ensure consistent tmux session naming between `spawnAdjutant()` and the persisted session store, so auto-heal isn't needed on every boot.

---

## Affected Files

- `backend/src/index.ts:145-260` — startup sequence (race, missing tunnel, missing env vars)
- `backend/src/services/mcp-server.ts:52, 61-70` — toolRegistrar null check
- `backend/src/services/tunnel-service.ts:171-258` — startTunnel() ignores env vars
- `backend/src/services/adjutant-spawner.ts` — spawnAdjutant() doesn't set ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID
- `backend/src/routes/mcp.ts:86-97` — MCP route accepts connections any time
- `.mcp.json` — agent ID defaults to 'unknown'
close_reason: Fixed all 4 bugs: MCP race (reordered startup), ngrok domain (reads NGROK_DOMAIN env), unknown agent (passes ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID env), tmux naming (verified consistent). Commit 6d67028f.

title: Fix premature agent shutdown on backend restart
description: Backend restart triggers markAllDisconnected() which marks all agents as disconnected. Signal aggregator classifies this as CRITICAL, wakes adjutant, which decommissions agents per its 10-minute rule. Shutdown messages are auto-delivered to agents on reconnect, stopping them mid-task. Root cause: no distinction between real disconnection and server-restart false positive.

title: Bug: CRITICAL — adj-053.4 branch deletes entire createMemoryStore implementation, breaking all consumers
description: Found on branch origin/adj-053.4-memory-query-api (commit f67e73b9). The Phase 4 engineer REWROTE memory-store.ts, removing: (1) The entire createMemoryStore() factory function with all SQLite queries, prepared statements, row mappers, and implementation code (~300 lines deleted). (2) Changed MemoryStore interface signatures in breaking ways (findSimilarLearnings, pruneStale, wentWell/wentWrong/actionItems types changed from string|null to string[]). (3) Renamed LearningQuery to LearningQueryOptions, changed findSimilarCorrection signature. If merged to main, this will: (a) Break memory-collector behavior (uses createMemoryStore), (b) Break session-retrospective behavior (uses old Retrospective types), (c) Break memory-store-crud.test.ts (imports createMemoryStore which no longer exists), (d) Make all 30 memory store CRUD tests fail. This branch MUST NOT be merged until the implementation is restored alongside the new interface additions. The new types/methods should be ADDITIVE, not destructive.
close_reason: Resolved: engineer-3 rebased from main, memory-store.ts (529 lines) is intact. Phase 4 merged with 1966 tests passing.

title: Bug: Work-assigner assigns implementation beads to adjutant coordinator agent
description: The work-assigner behavior assigned adj-022.2.2 (Extract beads-epics module) to the adjutant-coordinator agent. The adjutant coordinator has a monitor-only role (defined in .claude/agents/adjutant.md) and should NEVER receive implementation work. The work-assigner needs an exclusion list or role-awareness so it skips agents with coordinator/monitor roles. Only memory-related beads (adj-053 and children) should be assignable to adjutant-coordinator. The assignment message appeared as a user prompt ('You have been assigned to adj-022.2.2'), which confused the coordinator into thinking the user issued the instruction.
close_reason: Fixed: added EXCLUDED_AGENT_IDS constant in work-assigner.ts isTrulyIdle() to filter out adjutant-coordinator from work assignment. 2 new tests added.

title: Bug: Adjutant coordinator agent not visible in agents list (iOS + frontend)
description: The primary Adjutant coordinator agent (adj-swarm-adjutant-coordinator) does not appear in the agents list on iOS or the web dashboard. The agent is running in tmux and connected via MCP, but the GET /api/agents endpoint doesn't include it. Need to investigate why the agent is missing — likely either not registering via MCP, or the agents-service filtering it out.
close_reason: Fixed in commits a4e62735, 30453b8c, b37a5937: used bridge.createSession() for persistence, added orphan re-registration, fixed --agent flag

title: Bug: ChatBubble.formatTimestamp allocates DateFormatter per message bubble render
description: ChatBubble.swift line 182-196: formatTimestamp() allocates a new DateFormatter() for EVERY chat bubble rendered. With 50 messages on screen, that is 50 DateFormatter allocations during every render cycle (scroll, refresh, foreground transition). DateFormatter is also expensive to allocate (~0.5-1ms each). The function also calls Calendar.current twice per call and Date() once. Fix: use static cached formatters.

title: Bug: BeadInfo/BeadDetail computed date properties allocate ISO8601DateFormatter on every call
description: BeadInfo.createdDate, BeadInfo.updatedDate, BeadDetail.createdDate, BeadDetail.updatedDate, and BeadDetail.closedDate all allocate 2x ISO8601DateFormatter instances per call in Bead.swift lines 61-73 (BeadInfo) and 131-151 (BeadDetail). These are called inside sort closures in DataSyncService.fetchBeads (line 515-516) which sorts ALL beads, calling .updatedDate/.createdDate once per comparison — 50 beads = ~280 comparisons = ~560 formatter allocations. Also called from DashboardViewModel (lines 173/177/185/187/234/291-292), EpicsListViewModel (lines 116-117), BeadsListViewModel (lines 289-306), and EpicDetailViewModel (line 104). ISO8601DateFormatter allocation is ~1-2ms each, so 560 allocations = 560-1120ms of pure main-thread blocking.

title: Bug: ISO8601DateFormatter + DateFormatter allocation in formatChatTimestamp
description: SwarmOverviewView.formatChatTimestamp (line 638-665) and DashboardView.formatChatTimestamp (line 73-102) both allocate new ISO8601DateFormatter and DateFormatter instances on every call. These functions are called per-row for every task and epic with timestamps in the overview/dashboard views. On a dashboard showing 20 tasks + 10 epics, that is 30 formatter pair allocations per render. Fix: extract to a shared DateFormatting utility with static cached formatters. Files: ios/Adjutant/Features/SwarmOverview/SwarmOverviewView.swift:638-665, ios/Adjutant/Features/Dashboard/DashboardView.swift:73-102

title: Bug: ISO8601DateFormatter allocation storm in AdjutantKit model computed properties
description: BeadInfo.createdDate, BeadInfo.updatedDate, BeadDetail.createdDate/updatedDate/closedDate, Message.date, and Proposal.createdDate all allocate 2 new ISO8601DateFormatter instances per call (one with fractionalSeconds, one without as fallback). These computed properties are called during sort operations in DataSyncService.fetchBeads (N*logN calls), during view rendering of bead/epic lists, and during timestamp formatting. With 100+ beads, a single sort creates ~1400 formatter allocations. Fix: use static shared formatters like TimelineEvent and PersistentMessage already do. Files: ios/AdjutantKit/Sources/AdjutantKit/Models/Bead.swift:61-73,131-151, ios/AdjutantKit/Sources/AdjutantKit/Models/Message.swift:67-71, ios/AdjutantKit/Sources/AdjutantKit/Models/Proposal.swift:57-61

title: QA: Audit iOS foreground transition and Chat tab for main-thread blockers

Also audit ChatView creation cost and any remaining redundant API calls. File bugs for each finding.

title: QA: Audit iOS startup chain for main-thread blockers

Look for: synchronous work on @MainActor, heavy view body evaluations, expensive CRT effects on first render, ISO8601DateFormatter allocations, Combine subscription storms, and any work that could block the main thread. File bugs for each finding.

Need deep QA investigation to find remaining causes.

The entire screen is unresponsive — no scrolling, no navigation. Must be a deeper main-thread blocker than what was addressed in adj-049. Need to trace the actual synchronous call chain from app open through first interactive frame.

title: Bug: iOS source files still use 'rig' parameter names and properties
description:
CRITICAL: iOS app does not compile after Phase 1 rename.

Phase 1 renamed BeadInfo.init parameter from 'rig:' to 'project:' in Bead.swift, but left 22+ call sites using the old 'rig:' label. Swift enforces labeled parameter matching, so this produces compiler errors:
  'extra argument rig in call'
  'missing argument for parameter project in call'

Files with compile errors (all have BeadInfo(..., rig: value, ...) that must be BeadInfo(..., project: value, ...)):
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Beads/Views/BeadRowView.swift (lines 159, 176, 193)
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Beads/Views/KanbanBoardView.swift (lines 95, 108, 121, 134)
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Beads/Views/KanbanCardView.swift (lines 145, 162, 179, 203, 220, 245)
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Beads/Views/KanbanColumnView.swift (lines 118, 131)
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Epics/Views/EpicRowView.swift (lines 120, 144)
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Beads/ViewModels/BeadsListViewModel.swift (lines 427, 440, 453, 466)

Additional non-compile-error iOS issues (wrong method/property names):
- ios/Adjutant/Core/Services/DataSyncService.swift: refreshBeads(rig:), fetchBeads(rig:), mail(forRig:), crew(forRig:), beads(forRig:) methods
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Beads/ViewModels/BeadsListViewModel.swift: rigOptions property (line 351)

iOS tests also affected:
- ios/AdjutantTests/Features/Dashboard/DashboardViewModelTests.swift (11 instances of rig:)
- ios/AdjutantTests/Features/Beads/BeadsListViewModelTests.swift (rigOptions references)
- ios/AdjutantKit/Tests/AdjutantKitTests/ModelTests.swift (rig: in struct init and 'rig' in JSON data)
- ios/AdjutantKit/Tests/AdjutantKitTests/APIClientTests.swift (rig: in JSON)
close_reason: All remaining rig references fixed in commit d0c79bb, merged to main

title: QA: gastown-utils.ts functions used by swarm-mode code paths must be preserved
description:
RISK: backend/src/services/gastown-utils.ts contains functions that are NOT gastown-specific despite the filename:
- addressToIdentity() — used by agent-data.ts for all modes
- parseAgentBeadId() — used by agent-data.ts for all modes  
- parseAgentFields() — used by agent-data.ts for all modes
- beadsIssueToMessage() — used by mail-data.ts and beads-transport.ts (swarm mail transport!)
- parseMessageLabels() — used by mail-data.ts and beads-transport.ts

These functions MUST be moved to a new module (e.g., agent-utils.ts or message-utils.ts) before gastown-utils.ts can be deleted. Deleting without moving will break swarm mode.

Need to: 1) Add unread messages to project overview API, 2) Add closedAt to EpicProgress, 3) Redesign SwarmOverviewView with agents at top, unread messages, tasks with timestamps, epics with timestamps, 4) Make frontend dashboard visible in swarm mode

title: CRITICAL: Static CRTTheme.Background hardcodes Pip-Boy green-tinted dark colors across 50+ views
description: The theme refactor added theme-aware background colors via ColorTheme.background (returning a BackgroundSet with screen/panel/elevated), but the old static CRTTheme.Background enum still exists with hardcoded Pip-Boy green-tinted dark colors (#020502, #040A04, #060F06). Over 50 views reference CRTTheme.Background.screen/panel/elevated, meaning every view in the app will display dark green-tinted backgrounds regardless of the selected theme. In Document mode (white background) this is catastrophic — users see dark green instead of white. In StarCraft mode, they see green-tinted backgrounds instead of purple-tinted ones. AFFECTED: MainTabView, DashboardView, BeadsListView, AgentListView, SwarmOverviewView, SettingsView, ChatView, ChatInputView, ProposalsView, CRTCard, CRTButton, all Previews, and 30+ more files. FIX: Either (a) make CRTTheme.Background theme-aware by reading the environment, or (b) replace all 50+ usages of CRTTheme.Background.screen/panel/elevated with theme.background.screen/panel/elevated using the environment-injected theme. Option (b) is the correct architectural approach.
close_reason: Already fixed by components agent (adj-024.3) — all static backgrounds migrated, effects/typography scheme-aware, components updated

title: ContentView does not inject crtTheme environment — theme changes invisible to all views
description:
**Location:** ios/Adjutant/App/ContentView.swift

**Issue:** ContentView reads appState.currentTheme for .tint() and .preferredColorScheme(), but it NEVER injects the crtTheme SwiftUI environment value into the view hierarchy. Neither ContentView, AdjutantApp.swift, nor MainTabView.swift calls .crtTheme(appState.currentTheme.colorTheme) or .environment(\.crtTheme, ...) on the root view.

As a result, all 100+ views that use @Environment(\.crtTheme) always receive the default value (.pipboy), regardless of what theme the user selects in Settings. Theme changes in Settings update AppState.currentTheme and .preferredColorScheme but the CRT environment colors never propagate.

**Expected:** When user selects Document or StarCraft theme, all views using @Environment(\.crtTheme) should receive the corresponding ColorTheme, updating primary colors, dim colors, backgrounds, accent colors, and glow effects.

**Actual:** @Environment(\.crtTheme) is always .pipboy because no parent view injects it dynamically.

**Fix:** Add .crtTheme(appState.currentTheme.colorTheme) modifier to the root Group in ContentView.body, like:
```swift
Group { ... }
    .crtTheme(appState.currentTheme.colorTheme)
    .tint(themeColor)
    .preferredColorScheme(...)
```

**Impact:** Every view in the app. Without this fix, the entire 3-color-scheme system is non-functional from the users perspective — they can switch themes in Settings but all CRT-themed UI stays green.

title: ModeTransitionTests references removed .red and .blue ThemeIdentifier cases
description:
**Location:** ios/AdjutantTests/Core/ModeTransitionTests.swift lines 159, 162, 198, 212

**Issue:** Two test methods reference ThemeIdentifier.red and ThemeIdentifier.blue which were removed in the 3-color-scheme refactor (commit d2dcd54). These cases no longer exist on the ThemeIdentifier enum, which now only has .pipboy, .document, and .starcraft.

**Affected tests:**
- testThemePreservedAcrossModeTransition (line 159: appState.currentTheme = .red, line 162: XCTAssertEqual(appState.currentTheme, .red))
- testAllStatePreservedThroughFullCycle (line 198: appState.currentTheme = .blue, line 212: XCTAssertEqual(appState.currentTheme, .blue))

**Expected:** Tests should compile and pass using valid ThemeIdentifier cases
**Actual:** Compile error - type ThemeIdentifier has no member red/blue

**Fix:** Replace .red with .document and .blue with .starcraft (or any valid 3-scheme case) in both test methods.

title: P0: completionPercent mismatch backend 0-100 vs iOS 0.0-1.0
description: Backend computeEpicProgress (beads-service.ts:1303) returns Math.round((closedCount / total) * 100) = integer 0-100. getRecentlyCompletedEpics (line 1368) returns completionPercent:100. But iOS EpicsSectionView.swift:40 does Int(epic.completionPercent * 100) expecting 0.0-1.0. Result: progress bars at 7500% width, text shows 7500%, glow always bright. Fix: backend should return ratio (closedCount/total) not percentage.

title: Investigate: Intermittent/unreliable loading

Produces prioritized fix plan.

The vi.fn().mockImplementation() pattern inside vi.mock factory still produces a non-constructable function. Commits d6a8bc3 and 48e0357 did not resolve the issue. Fix: replace the arrow function in vi.mock factory with a real class definition or use function keyword (not arrow) which is constructable.

title: Fleet dolt outage 2026-06-11: stale .beads/dolt-server.port dialed dead ports (FIXED + self-heal watchdog)
description: bd 1.0.4 dials .beads/dolt-server.port as authoritative endpoint (metadata only sets externally-managed MODE). adj-182 rollout pinned metadata+config but NOT the port file -> 9/11 projects kept stale pre-rollout random ports -> bd dialed dead ports -> 'Dolt server unreachable' while supervised servers were healthy on 17000-17010. FIX: (1) live-corrected all 11 port files (0 errors); (2) self-heal watchdog ~/.adjutant/dolt-heal.sh via launchd com.adjutant.dolt-heal every 120s (reconciles port-file/pin/server + kickstarts dead/hung; proven); (3) pinDoltPort now writes the port file +2 tests on branch fix/dolt-port-file-pin-adj182. Residual: ai/OttoDom/gt/l2rr2l/C4 ~3.4s but 0 errors (near-empty dormant, June-4 half-migration, bd local fallback). bd dolt status falsely says 'not running' (#2670) - use bd list/bd dolt test.

title: bd 1.0.4 per-call jsonl re-import + data-loss risk in degraded (non-supervised) projects — roll out externally-managed+autocommit+supervisor to bloomfolio/fleet (BUG2)
description: Reported during bloomfolio-goals scaffold 2026-06-06. Symptom: every bd call logs 'auto-importing ~2MB into empty database / auto-imported 1731 issues', 12-15s/call; concurrent writers race jsonl (no row-lock) -> nondeterministic bead loss; stale/truncated jsonl (e.g. from BUG1/adj-182.3.9) silently shrinks bead set next call. ROOT CAUSE (karax diagnosis): degraded bd-managed mode — NOT externally-managed (no dolt_server_port pin) + autocommit OFF -> imports stay in working set, never commit to HEAD -> each new bd connection sees empty HEAD -> re-imports jsonl. PROOF: adjutant (externally-managed pin 17000 + autocommit:on + supervised persistent server) = 0 re-imports, bd list 0.2s; bloomfolio (rollout deferred) = per-call re-import, 12-15s. FIX: complete deferred Phase-3 rollout (adj-182.3.1/3.2) for bloomfolio + 9 other bd-managed projects: coordinated mini-cutover (quiesce writers, restart server with autocommit:on + externally-managed pin + launchd, commit HEAD, clear breaker). ALSO: 10 orphan random-port dolt servers accumulated (leak + random-port drift) — clean up during rollout.

title: iOS chat channels don't live-update on new messages (DMs do) — likely missing WS subscribe-on-open / routing
description: On iOS, opening a channel does not auto-update when new messages arrive, but DMs do. Architecture (adj-164): DMs broadcast to ALL authenticated WS clients (client-scoped), but channels use wsBroadcastToConversation → ONLY member+subscribed clients (fail-closed). Likely cause: iOS ChannelView/ViewModel doesn't send the WS channel 'subscribe' on open (or doesn't route incoming channel messages to the VM to append live). Fix iOS so channel messages live-update like DMs. Need fix + new TestFlight build.

title: bd 1.0.4 first-use migration fails in live externally-managed+launchd+pinned topology (de-risk gap)
description: LIVE UPGRADE ATTEMPT 2026-06-04 FAILED + rolled back (no data loss; adjutant back on 0.60.0, bd-managed pinned 17000, DB intact). Root cause: bd 1.0.4 HONORS externally-managed (won't auto-spawn). migrate-on-first-use needs a server: with launchd --config server up, bd's embedded migration hits the data-dir write-lock the server holds ('database dolt is locked by another dolt process'); with no server, 'connection refused'. Meanwhile .local_version got bumped to 1.0.4 so bd then SKIPS migration and queries the new schema -> 'column started_at could not be found'. DB was never actually migrated. DE-RISK GAP: adj-182.3.3 prep migrated a COPY in bd-managed mode, NOT the live externally-managed+launchd+pinned topology. FIX: reproduce the EXACT live topology against a copy; determine the correct 0.60.0->1.0.4 migration sequence (candidates: temporarily un-pin so 1.0.4 bd-manages a server for the migration then re-pin+re-enable launchd; OR explicit embedded migrate with NO server up then start launchd; OR an explicit bd migrate cmd). Update runbook-bd-upgrade.md §3. Blocks adj-182.3.5 (live upgrade).
close_reason: De-risk complete (runbook 70c41639→main). Verified correct sequence: launchd server UP + port-file authoritative + .local_version honest 0.60.0 → single bd list migrates via SQL through the server (no lock fight). New hard precondition: machine-wide bd freeze (0.60.0 orphan-cleanup is process-global). adj-182.3.5 (live upgrade) now unblocked.

comments.author: Justin Martin
comments.text: ROOT CAUSE (proven on copies, adj-182.3.5.1): NOT the data-dir lock. bd 1.0.4 reads .beads/dolt-server.port as authoritative and IGNORES the metadata dolt_server_port pin. Stale port file -> connection refused -> .local_version eagerly bumped to 1.0.4 even on the FAILED connect -> next run skips migration -> queries the new schema on the un-migrated 0.60.0 DB -> 'column started_at could not be found'. WINNER (candidate a-prime): port file -> the running launchd server + .local_version honest at 0.60.0, then a single bd list migrates cleanly THROUGH the externally-managed launchd server (migration 010 across 6 tables), zero lock fight, no competitor spawn, live server survives, data durable across restart, GATE green. Recovery from a poisoned version: printf 0.60.0 > .beads/.local_version then re-run. ALSO: bd 0.60.0 orphan-cleanup is PROCESS-GLOBAL (killed live 17000 even from a fully HOME-isolated bd init) -> machine-wide bd freeze is a hard precondition. Runbook 3/4/7/8 rewritten with the topology-accurate sequence + a reusable de-risk harness. Live untouched and healthy throughout.

title: Re-enable launchd dolt supervisor after bd->1.0.4 (0.60.0 doesn't honor externally-managed)
description: LIVE FINDING (2026-06-04, during adjutant cutover re-validation): bd 0.60.0 does NOT honor externally-managed mode — it auto-spawns its own 'dolt sql-server -H/-P <pinned>' which takes the data-dir exclusive write-lock, so the launchd '--config' supervisor crash-loops ('database "dolt" is locked by another dolt process'). The launchd supervisor and bd 0.60.0 are incompatible. INTERIM (live now on adjutant): launchd plist DISABLED at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.adjutant.dolt.c249344d-1d43-4359-a2dd-be8cbb0270e3.plist.disabled; running bd-managed on the PINNED port 17000 (anti-churn win retained, verified stable). AFTER bd>=1.0.4 (adj-182.3.3) lands: re-enable the plist (mv .disabled back + launchctl bootstrap), verify a SINGLE supervised server holds 17000 AND bd connects WITHOUT spawning a competitor (watch ~30s + several bd calls), then re-run the kill-9 crash-recovery acceptance. Gate: do NOT re-enable supervision until bd is upgraded.
close_reason: Supervisor re-enabled + crash-validated on bd 1.0.4; independent of 3.6 (metadata-pin cleanup follow-up)

title: iOS: long quick-pick options overflow the screen — FlowLayout doesn't clamp item width; need fix + new build
description: OpenQuestionsView FlowLayout (~line 435) sizes each chip with subview.sizeThatFits(.unspecified) — unbounded width. A long suggestedOption Text (single line, no wrap) returns a width wider than the screen; FlowLayout never clamps item width to the available width, so the card inflates past the screen (body text clipped on the left, options run off the right). Fix: bound each item to the available container width so long options WRAP; ensure body text wraps within the card; bump version + new TestFlight build. Screenshot confirmed on device.
close_reason: FlowLayout clamps chips to available width (long options wrap), card constrained; app-target xcodebuild SUCCEEDED, AdjutantKit 122 tests; bumped 2.13(40), merged+pushed.

title: Cutover install fails: port allocator keyed by metadata UUID, registry keyed by short-id/path (id-space mismatch)
description: Live cutover 2026-06-03 aborted at install-dolt-supervisor.sh (RC=1, before any server start): 'Project c249344d-... not found in Dolt port registry at ~/.adjutant/projects.json'. allocateDoltPort (cli/lib/dolt-port-registry.ts) was called with metadata.json project_id (beads UUID), but ~/.adjutant/projects.json is keyed by the SHORT project id (e.g. 0e578d15) + repo path — different id-spaces. Seam-only unit tests passed because they injected a registry pre-seeded with the test id; the REAL registry uses a different key. FIX: resolve port allocation by repo PATH (registry entries have 'path'), persisting doltPort on the matched entry (auto-create only if no path match); fix the install call site (cli/lib/install-dolt-supervisor-cli.ts). Add an INTEGRATION test against the REAL projects.json shape (short-id+path, no uuid, no pre-seeded doltPort) — RED then GREEN (Rule 1). Blocks the live cutover.
close_reason: Fixed: Dolt port allocation now joins the registry by repo PATH (allocateDoltPortByPath), not the beads UUID — unblocks the live cutover. Repointed install/init/doctor call sites. Added integration test on the REAL registry shape (reproduces RED → GREEN) + RUN_DOLT_E2E-gated end-to-end smoke (real dolt sql-server + SQL probe). 131 dolt tests green, build green. Merged to main (6343fa24).

title: Bug: bd-client reconnect leaks clearedPorts across failed episodes — stale circuit file never re-cleared on recurring outage
description: execBdWithReconnect (backend/src/services/bd-client.ts) only resets the module-level doltConnectionState on the SUCCESS path (line 528). When an outage exhausts the attempt budget (lines 541-544 return without reset), the dead port stays in doltConnectionState.clearedPorts. On the NEXT execBd episode against the same still-dead port, line 549 guard (!clearedPorts.has(port)) is false, so seams.clearCircuitFile() is SKIPPED — the stale /tmp/beads-dolt-circuit-<port>.json is never re-cleared and bd keeps failing fast on the dead port. This is exactly the macOS-sleep/port-churn wedge the epic exists to fix: recovery WITHOUT a process restart is defeated for any recurring/multi-call outage. Proven with a probe test: episode 2 against the same dead port performs 0 circuit-file clears (expected >0). FIX: clear/reset clearedPorts (or doltConnectionState) on the exhausted-budget return, or use a per-call local Set instead of module-level state. Add a regression test: two consecutive failed episodes against the same port must each clear the circuit file. Suite: backend/tests/unit/bd-client-reconnect.test.ts.
close_reason: Fixed: execBdWithReconnect now resets doltConnectionState on the exhausted-budget path so a recurring outage re-clears the stale circuit file each episode. Regression tests added (two consecutive failed episodes + healed-endpoint recovery). Merged to main 6da75233.

title: Bug: bd-doctor rogue-kill cwd prefix-match kills sibling-dir dolt (.beads-backup)
description: scripts/bd-doctor.sh classify_dolt uses a glob prefix match: [[ "$_cwd" == "$BD_DIR"* ]]. A dolt server whose cwd is a SIBLING directory sharing the .beads prefix (e.g. /proj/.beads-backup, /proj/.beads2, /proj/.beads.bak) matches and is classified as a rogue under THIS project's data-dir, then KILLED. This kills a different project's healthy dolt server — the exact 'server appears down' outage adj-182 fixes, and violates the spec's 'never kill the wrong process' intent. Reproduced: BD_DIR=/tmp/qa-bd/.beads, override cwd=/tmp/qa-bd/.beads-backup, SUPERVISED_PID set -> 'ROGUE dolt detected pid=54321 ... killing rogue orphan'. The same flawed match also mis-classifies the legitimate-server path. Fix: match exact dir or path-separator boundary, e.g. [[ "$_cwd" == "$BD_DIR" || "$_cwd" == "$BD_DIR/"* ]]. Add a regression test (sibling .beads-backup must NOT be killed).
close_reason: Fixed: path-boundary match in classify_dolt; sibling .beads-backup no longer killed. Regression tests RED->GREEN. Merged to main 8f69c49f.
