title: QA: WS sync replay leaks room-scoped channel messages to non-members (FR-007 bypass)
description:
EPIC adj-164. CRITICAL room-isolation bypass on the WS recovery path.

ROOT CAUSE: wsBroadcastToConversation() (backend/src/services/ws-server.ts:778-800) fans out room-scoped (member AND subscribed) for LIVE delivery — correct. BUT it also pushes the room message into the GLOBAL replayBuffer via addToReplay(msg) at ws-server.ts:793. handleSync() (ws-server.ts:344-356) returns EVERY replay entry with seq > lastSeqSeen to ANY authenticated client with NO membership and NO subscription check. A non-member that sends {type:'sync', lastSeqSeen:0} receives all channels' messages (id + body) in sync_response.missed.

This defeats FR-007 (WS delivery MUST be room-scoped via membership) and the success criterion 'zero cross-conversation bleed' on the recovery path. The live-fan-out gate is fully bypassed.

REPRO (confirmed by QA probe, shared-checkout backend):
1. setConversationStore with chan-secret members = [raynor, kerrigan] (user NOT a member)
2. wsBroadcastToConversation('chan-secret', {type:'chat_message', id:'secret-msg', body:'members-only intel'})
3. connect+auth a client as identity 'user', send {type:'sync', lastSeqSeen:0}
EXPECTED: sync_response.missed contains NO entry with id 'secret-msg'
ACTUAL: sync_response.missed = [{type:'chat_message', id:'secret-msg', body:'members-only intel', seq:1}]

FIX OPTIONS:
- handleSync MUST filter missed entries by the requesting client's membership+subscription (resolve via conversationStore.getMembers and client.subscriptions for any entry that carries a conversationId; entries with a conversationId the client cannot access must be dropped).
- Preferred: tag replay entries with their conversationId (room messages already carry msg.conversationId) and exclude any room-scoped entry whose conversationId the client is not a member of. Non-room (global) messages remain replayable.

TEST GAP: tests/unit/ws-room-fanout.test.ts:250 only asserts replay buffering assigns a seq; no test sends sync from a non-member and asserts exclusion. Add a regression test mirroring the probe above.

FILES: backend/src/services/ws-server.ts:344-356 (handleSync), :778-800 (wsBroadcastToConversation), :793 (addToReplay).
close_reason: Duplicate of adj-2jy4u (same WS sync/replay non-member leak) — already in_progress with karass. Independent QA confirmation noted.

title: Review: channel posts leak to non-members via WS sync/replay buffer
description:
adj-164 code review (mohandar). backend/src/services/ws-server.ts.

wsBroadcastToConversation (line ~793) calls addToReplay(msg) for EVERY channel post, writing it into the SINGLE global replayBuffer. handleSync (lines 344-356) replays all buffered entries with seq > lastSeqSeen to ANY authenticated client, filtering ONLY by seq — no membership or subscription check. 

Impact: a non-member client that connects and sends {type:"sync", lastSeqSeen:N} receives channel chat_message bodies it is not authorized to see. This defeats the room-scoped authorization boundary that wsBroadcastToConversation otherwise enforces for live delivery. Security: cross-channel message disclosure.

The live fan-out path is well-tested (tests/unit/ws-room-fanout.test.ts) but NO test exercises sync/replay, so the leak is uncovered.

Fix options: (a) keep a per-conversation replay buffer and have handleSync re-filter by the requesting client's identity+subscriptions against conversation membership before returning entries; or (b) tag replay entries with conversationId and, in handleSync, drop conversation-scoped entries the client is not a member+subscriber of. Add a regression test: non-member sync must not receive a channel post that was buffered for replay.
close_reason: P1 security leak closed: WS sync replay channel-only membership gating; full backend 3491 green. Completed by tassadar (karass+artanis stalled on worktree npm install).

title: cwd-leak: fresh worktree spawn's git checkout -b executed in MAIN repo during adj-164 squad
description: During adj-164 squad execution, a FRESH background worktree spawn (selendis, iOS lane, subagent_type=raynor) leaked a 'git checkout -b feat/adj-164.3-dm-ios' into the MAIN repo. Main repo HEAD reflog: HEAD@{1} 'checkout: moving from main to feat/adj-164.3-dm-ios' at fb48cb21, with no such command run by the coordinator. This moved main repo HEAD off main, so the coordinator's subsequent 'git merge' (channels-backend) landed on the stray branch instead of main; 'git push origin main' then reported up-to-date. Recovered with zero loss: merge commit da3b4ea6 had clean parents (spine + channels-backend), fast-forwarded main to it, deleted stray ref. Notable: .claude/rules/07-team-isolation.md states the FRESH-spawn cwd-leak (adj-iqyqw) is FIXED; this is a fresh spawn (not a resume), so either the fix regressed or raynor-type/background spawns reintroduce it. Mitigation in use: coordinator now verifies 'git branch --show-current'==main and HEAD before every merge/push. Related: adj-iqyqw, adj-c2bbv.
notes: Refined root cause (reflog forensics, self-served): selendis = worktree agent-a94c22cccecd72776. It WAS isolated, but its FIRST git command (git checkout -b feat/adj-164.3-dm-ios) ran in MAIN at epoch 1780087334 — 94s after its worktree was created — while all later commands ran correctly in the worktree. So it's a fresh-spawn FIRST-COMMAND cwd race (first Bash shell ran in parent/main cwd before the worktree chdir landed), load-dependent (40+ concurrent worktrees). NOT non-isolation, NOT a resume. Documented as upstream UI-2 (medium confidence — reflog-reconstructed, not deterministically reproduced). Shipped fix (f85c8667) fully covers it. SEPARATE OPERATIONAL FLAG raised to coordinator+tassadar: selendis's iOS work (adj-164.3.1/.2/.3, tip 78c01cb0 + uncommitted WS work) is on branch worktree-agent-a94c22cccecd72776 — local, unpushed, unmerged; feat/adj-164.3-dm-ios was deleted. Squad must merge worktree-agent-a94c22cccecd72776, not feat/adj-164.3-dm-ios, and not prune that worktree until merged.
close_reason:
Root-caused + hardened (commit f85c8667, merged to main on top of the squad's latest, clean fast-forward; targeted lint+test green — full verify-before-push was CPU-contended by the live squad so I ran lint+test on the only code file changed, which is provably isolated: markdown + one new test).

ROOT CAUSE (first-hand reflog + empirical reproduction):
- Main-repo reflog confirms selendis's `git checkout -b feat/adj-164.3-dm-ios` ran against the MAIN working tree (HEAD@{20:42:14} moving from main).
- BUT the harness isolates FRESH spawns correctly on Claude Code v2.1.156 — I reproduced twice: a fresh BACKGROUND worktree agent (general-purpose AND subagent_type=raynor) reports git-dir under .claude/worktrees and ISWORKTREE=true on its FIRST turn. So "fresh background raynor-type" does NOT by itself reproduce the leak; my adj-iqyqw conclusion (fresh spawns isolate) holds.
- Therefore selendis's cwd was main because either (a) that spawn did not actually carry isolation:"worktree", or (b) it was a resumed turn (the adj-c2bbv vector). Could not yet get tassadar's exact spawn command to disambiguate (he's mid-squad) — OPEN follow-up question to him, non-blocking. Either way the trigger is a worktree agent running `git checkout -b`, which it NEVER needs (the harness already put it on its worktree-agent-<id> branch).

FIX (closes the vector regardless of sub-case):
- skills/squad-execute/squad-member-context.md: forbid `git checkout -b`/branch switch in worktree agents (commit + `git push -u origin HEAD` instead); HARD assert git-dir is under .claude/worktrees before ANY git write, else abort + report to leader.
- skills/squad-execute/SKILL.md: matching Key Rules + baked `git branch --show-current`==main guards into the coordinator's merge/push procedure (the exact check that caught this incident).
- backend/tests/unit/squad-isolation-rule.test.ts: +5 content-assertion tests (TDD red→green, 11/11) lock the rules in.
- docs/upstream-issues.md: cross-referenced under UI-1 (same cwd-leak, broader blast radius via git checkout -b moving main HEAD).

If tassadar's spawn evidence later shows isolation:"worktree" was present AND a genuinely new harness condition produced a non-isolated fresh spawn, that warrants a new upstream bead — but current evidence points to non-isolation-or-resume, both covered.

title: Worktree agent RESUME resets Bash cwd to main repo — isolation data-loss footgun
description:
Split from adj-iqyqw. While verifying adj-iqyqw on Claude Code v2.1.156, found the cwd-leak failure mode MIGRATED from fresh spawns (now fixed) to the RESUME path.

## Empirical repro (2026-05-29, captured)
Spawned two `isolation:"worktree"` probe agents that each ran 6 SEPARATE Bash calls (pwd / git toplevel / write marker / pwd / git status / realpath):

- FRESH foreground spawn (agent ab965f…): all 6 calls cwd = the worktree (`.claude/worktrees/agent-<id>`), cwd PERSISTS, marker file written INSIDE the worktree. Isolation correct.
- RESUMED background agent (agent a4c959…, resumed via SendMessage after a 529 error): all 6 calls cwd = the MAIN REPO (`/Users/Reason/code/ai/adjutant`), marker file `cwd-leak-probe.txt` written into the MAIN REPO working tree. This is the adj-iqyqw silent-data-loss mode.

## Root cause
When a background worktree sub-agent is resumed (SendMessage after it finished/errored), it resumes with process cwd = the parent/main repo, NOT its worktree. Every subsequent Bash call starts a fresh shell there, so relative file ops land in the shared tree.

## Why this is P1 (not P0)
- Narrow trigger: only when RESUMING a finished/errored worktree agent to do MORE file work (fresh spawns are safe).
- Mitigated: documented in .claude/rules/07-team-isolation.md "Resume Hazard" (cd into worktree every Bash call / use absolute paths / verify git status after resume).
- Harness-level: this is Claude Code Task-tool behavior, NOT Adjutant code — Adjutant can only mitigate via guidance, not patch.

## Suggested actions
- Keep the rule guidance authoritative; consider injecting "you resumed in the main repo — cd to <worktree> first" into resume prompts if Adjutant ever resumes Task agents programmatically.
- Consider reporting the resume-cwd-reset upstream to Anthropic (harness bug).
- Adjutant's OWN tmux spawn path is unaffected (createSession uses `-c <worktree>`; locked by regression tests in lifecycle-manager.test.ts + swarm-service.test.ts).

## Forensics
/tmp/adj-y2vq-repro-1780079087/ (probe transcripts also captured the resume cwd flip).
close_reason:
Both deliverables shipped (commit a6eb17d2, merged to main, verify GREEN). Harness-level bug (can't patch the Task tool) — eliminated the data-loss risk on OUR side + filed the upstream report.

1. Mitigation baked into the spawn tooling (enforced by tooling, not memory):
   - skills/squad-execute/SKILL.md — Step 9 was actively triggering the hazard (told the coordinator to SendMessage a FINISHED engineer a follow-up fix → resumes it in the main-repo cwd → leaked edits). Fixed: never SendMessage-resume a completed worktree agent for file work; spawn a fresh worktree agent instead; if unavoidable, cd <worktree> && every Bash call + verify main-repo git status clean. Added the same as a Key Rule.
   - skills/squad-execute/squad-member-context.md — per-agent self-protection (included verbatim in every squad spawn prompt): on a resumed turn you may be in the main repo; pwd, cd back into your worktree (or refuse edits + request a fresh spawn), verify the shared tree stays clean. Defends at the point of damage even if a leader resumes the agent.
   - backend/tests/unit/squad-isolation-rule.test.ts — 6 content-assertion tests (TDD red→green) lock the rule into both templates so it can't be silently dropped.

2. Upstream Anthropic report: docs/upstream-issues.md (entry UI-1) — full fresh-vs-resumed repro from the adj-iqyqw findings, env (Claude Code v2.1.156), impact, and suggested upstream fix (restore cwd to the worktree on resume).

Note: Adjutant's own tmux spawn path is unaffected (tmux -c <worktree> persists; locked by adj-iqyqw tests). De-risks the adj-164 chat squad before it spawns worktree agents.

title: QA: useVoicePlayer natural-end (handleEnded) leaks 6 listeners + audio.src
description: frontend/src/hooks/useVoicePlayer.ts:122-126 (handleEnded). When audio plays through to natural completion, handleEnded() only sets state to 'idle' and resets progress/currentTime. It does NOT call cleanupRef.current() to remove the 6 event listeners (play, pause, timeupdate, ended, error, loadedmetadata) and does NOT clear audio.src. The 6 listeners and the decoded audio data remain pinned until: (a) play() is called again (cleanupAudio() at line 74), (b) stop() is called, or (c) component unmounts. Repro: play 50 voice messages sequentially, letting each complete naturally; expect 50 leaked Audio objects with 300 listeners total. The adj-139.3.2 fix correctly handles the rejected-play path but missed the success-then-ended path. Existing test frontend/tests/unit/useVoicePlayer.test.ts:283-307 'should reset state when audio ends' only asserts state==='idle', does NOT assert listeners removed or src cleared. Fix: in handleEnded(), call cleanupRef.current?.(); cleanupRef.current = null; audio.src = ''; audioRef.current = null. Add a test in useVoicePlayer.cleanup.test.ts asserting natural-end fully releases the Audio.
close_reason: Fixed: handleEnded() now invokes cleanupRef + clears audio.src on natural completion, mirroring the rejected-play branch. 2 new cleanup tests added.

title: QA: useAudioNotifications mute() and disable() do not clean up audio listeners/src
description: frontend/src/hooks/useAudioNotifications.ts:263-269 (mute) and :299-308 (disable) call audio.pause() but do NOT invoke cleanupCurrentAudioRef.current(), so the 'ended' and 'error' listeners remain attached and audio.src is not cleared. The cleanup ref exists and is correctly invoked from skip() (line 247) and unmount (line 339), but mute() and disable() take a shortcut that leaks the in-flight audio. Repro: enqueue 50 notifications, mute mid-playback 50 times; each call leaks one Audio + 2 listeners. Expected: mute()/disable() invoke cleanupCurrentAudioRef.current() like skip() does, then null audioRef. Spec FR-004: 'All ... resources MUST be cleaned up'. Acceptance US2 #3 (100 audio notifications, all Audio released) is not fully satisfied because the spec doesn't say notifications run to completion — a user muting mid-stream still leaks.
close_reason: Fixed: extracted releaseCurrentAudio() helper, mute()+disable() now fully release in-flight audio (listeners + src). 2 new tests cover mid-playback mute/disable.

title: SITUATION nudges should include delta/change summary so coordinator knows when to act
description:
The coordinator gets inundated with identical SITUATION nudges (especially auto-develop REVIEW nudges) and becomes desensitized — leading to multi-hour stalls when actual changes happen (e.g., Duke completing reviews).

Fix: SITUATION nudges should include lightweight status deltas so the coordinator can quickly distinguish 'nothing changed' from 'something happened':
- Agent status changes since last nudge (e.g., 'duke: working→done')
- New messages from relevant agents since last nudge
- Proposal score changes since last nudge
- Bead status changes since last nudge

This doesn't need to be verbose — just enough signal for the coordinator to know whether to investigate further or dismiss. Example: 'DELTA: duke→done (2 new messages), 2 proposals scored since last check' vs 'DELTA: no changes'.
close_reason: Added ## Delta Since Last Nudge section to SITUATION prompts with event-based change tracking

title: Complete cross-project MCP tool fix — get_proposal and create_proposal still reject cross-project access
description: Raynor's adj-146 added projectId to advance_auto_develop_phase, score_proposal, and several tools. But get_proposal and create_proposal still reject cross-project access. Duke can't read proposal bodies to review them, and the coordinator can't create proposals for other projects via MCP. These tools need the same projectId override pattern applied in adj-146.
close_reason:
Both cross-project proposal tools now work (commit 8e0b36cd, merged to main, verify GREEN).

- get_proposal: was the real gap — validateProjectAccess only used the session's project context, so cross-project reads were rejected (Duke couldn't read another project's proposal bodies). Fixed by applying the adj-146 projectId-override pattern: validateProjectAccess now resolves via resolveToolProjectContext(explicitProjectId, sessionId) and get_proposal accepts an optional projectId param. Backward-compatible — with no projectId it falls back to session scoping, so discuss/comment/revise/list_* are unchanged.

- create_proposal: VERIFIED already working cross-project — it was fixed in a prior pass (uses resolveToolProjectContext(projectId, sessionId) + projectId param). The bead was partially stale on this point. Added a characterization test that locks the behavior so it can't regress.

Tests (real cross-project payload shapes, TDD red→green): cross-project read allowed when projectId matches the proposal's project; rejected when it doesn't; default session scoping preserved when projectId omitted; create_proposal cross-project create. 40/40 proposal-scoping tests pass, no regressions.

Kept surgical (Rule 9): only the two tools named in the bead. The validateProjectAccess refactor makes extending the same override to discuss/comment/revise/list_* trivial if ever needed.

title: Proposal completion is coordinator-driven in VALIDATE, not automated
description:
The proposal-lifecycle.ts bead:closed listener doesn't work because agents use bd CLI (bypasses EventBus) and can result in misleading premature completions. Proposal completion should be explicitly triggered by the coordinator during VALIDATE phase after QA sentinels verify the work.

Changes:
1. REMOVE the bead:closed auto-complete listener in proposal-lifecycle.ts entirely — no backup, it causes misleading scenarios
2. REMOVE the checkProposalCompletion() call from beads.ts close_bead handler
3. Add 'mark source proposal as completed' instruction to buildValidateReason() in auto-develop-loop.ts
4. The coordinator calls complete_proposal (or updates status directly) after QA passes
5. proposal:completed event fires from this explicit coordinator action
6. Proposals stay 'accepted' until VALIDATE confirms delivery — no premature completion

IMPORTANT — Update specs:
7. Update specs/049-auto-develop-v2/spec.md US4 and US5 to reflect this change: proposal completion is coordinator-driven in VALIDATE, not automated by bead:closed events
8. Update specs/049-auto-develop-v2/plan.md Phase 5 description to match
9. Update specs/049-auto-develop-v2/tasks.md T015 and T016 descriptions to match

This ensures specs stay in sync with the reality being built.
close_reason: Removed broken automated listeners, added coordinator-driven completeProposal(), updated specs and tests

title: Auto-tank VALIDATE phase — QA and code review of completed execution
description:
Auto-tank auto-develop cycle has reached VALIDATE phase. tank-eng-1 completed 48 beads (287 tests) across 6 epics: Event Bus, Wind System, Projectile Simulation, Tank Positioning, Turn Orchestrator, Input Handling.

Validation scope:
1. Run full test suite in auto-tank project, verify all pass
2. Review code quality across delivered systems — naming, structure, test coverage
3. Check for integration gaps between systems (e.g., Event Bus ↔ Wind ↔ Projectile pipeline)
4. Verify 17 remaining P2 beads are properly scoped and not blocking
5. Report findings — create bug beads for any critical issues found

After validation, advance the cycle to complete (or back to ANALYZE for next iteration).

title: MCP tools must accept explicit projectId parameter for cross-project operations
description:
MCP tools like advance_auto_develop_phase are scoped to the connected agent's project context. When the coordinator (scoped to adjutant) needs to advance the auto-tank cycle, the tool returns 'Auto-develop is not enabled for this project' because it uses the session's project, not the target project.

All project-scoped MCP tools should accept an optional projectId parameter to allow cross-project operations. This affects:
- advance_auto_develop_phase
- get_auto_develop_status
- Any other MCP tool that implicitly uses the session's project context

This is part of the broader project identity standardization (adj-141). The coordinator manages multiple projects and must be able to operate on any of them from a single MCP connection.
close_reason: Added projectId param to 12 MCP tools for cross-project operations

title: iOS: Handle auto-develop timeline event types
description: The iOS timeline view is missing support for 3 new auto-develop event types: auto_develop_enabled, auto_develop_disabled, auto_develop_phase_changed. These events fall through to the default gray circle. Add proper icons, colors, labels, and filter options for all three.

title: Auto-develop cycle counters and timeline events for phase transitions
description:
Two issues need fixing in auto-develop:

1. **Cycle counters not updating**: The auto_develop_cycles table still shows 0 for proposals_generated and proposals_accepted even after Nova's adj-142 fix added sync logic. The counter sync from the proposal store may not be firing on behavior ticks, or the sync query doesn't match the correct project/cycle. Verify the sync runs and updates the DB.

2. **Timeline events for phase transitions**: Phase transitions (analyze→ideate→gate→plan→execute→review→validate) should emit timeline events so the dashboard can show when each phase started/completed. Currently there's no visibility into phase changes beyond the raw DB record.
close_reason: Fixed cycle counter ordering bug (sync now runs after cycle creation) and confirmed timeline events already wired from adj-140.2

title: Fix auto-develop phase transition — PLAN phase does not advance to EXECUTE
description: The auto-develop cycle for auto-tank has been stuck in PLAN phase for 8+ hours despite planning being complete (6 epics, 33 tasks created by planner-1). The phase transition logic is not detecting that planning is done and advancing to EXECUTE. Related to adj-139 (auto-develop loop stalled overnight). Root cause likely in the phase advancement logic in auto-develop-status.ts or the advance_auto_develop_phase MCP tool. The cycle record shows 0 proposals_generated/accepted even though proposals were created and scored — the counters may not be updating, or the gate conditions for advancement are wrong.
close_reason: Fixed: coordinator-driven phases no longer auto-advance, cycle counters now sync from proposal store

Eliminate the legacy dual-matching shim and migrate stored project names to UUIDs.

Agents in auto-develop mode should be explicitly informed they operate without user input.

Phase transitions should also emit events to the event bus so they appear in the timeline. Add persistent timeline event records (new table or extend existing events), emit events on enable/disable/phase-change, and expose them via API for frontend consumption.

The coordinator should actively manage phase transitions, spawn agents for each phase, monitor progress, and ensure the loop keeps moving. Update coordinator situation prompts, spawn templates, and loop behavior to make coordinator ownership explicit.

title: Proposals not found when filtering by project name or ID — breaks auto-develop scoring and iOS
description:
Proposal queries return empty results when filtering by project name or ID, despite proposals existing for the project. This breaks multiple systems: auto-develop score_proposal ('auto-develop is not enabled' error), MCP list_proposals with project filter, iOS proposal list, and the coordinator's ability to track per-project proposals.

## Confirmed symptoms

### 1. list_proposals with project filter returns empty
Coordinator called list_proposals({ status: 'pending', project: 'auto-tank' }) and got { proposals: [], count: 0 }. But the proposals exist — they show up in unfiltered list_proposals({ status: 'pending' }) alongside Adjutant proposals.

### 2. score_proposal fails with 'Auto-develop is not enabled'
reviewer-1 attempted to score auto-tank proposals via score_proposal MCP tool. Got error: 'Auto-develop is not enabled'. Auto-develop IS enabled on the project (confirmed: project 71f9d993, autoDevelop: true). The tool likely looks up the project from the proposal, fails to match, and concludes auto-develop isn't enabled.

### 3. iOS shows same bug
User reports the same filtering issue on iOS — proposals for a project don't appear when filtered by project.

## Likely root cause
Project identity mismatch in proposal storage vs. query. Proposals may be stored with one identifier (e.g., project name 'auto-tank') but queried with another (e.g., project ID '71f9d993'), or vice versa. The filter logic may use strict equality when it should check both name and ID.

## Investigation areas

### Proposal creation path
- When ideator-1 created proposals via create_proposal MCP tool, what project identifier was stored?
- Check backend/src/services/mcp-tools/beads.ts or wherever create_proposal is implemented
- The MCP server resolves project context from X-Project-Root header — does this resolve to the project ID or name?
- Check the proposals table: what value is in the 'project' column for the auto-tank proposals?

### Proposal query path
- How does list_proposals filter by project? Does it check proposals.project against the filter value?
- Does it check both project ID and project name?
- Check backend/src/services/proposal-store.ts — getProposals() method, project filter logic
- The auto-develop-loop.ts uses { project: [projectId, projectName] } as a filter array — does the store support array matching?

### score_proposal path
- Where does score_proposal look up the project to check auto-develop status?
- Does it extract the project from the proposal record, then look up the project in the projects table?
- If the proposal's project field contains the project name but the projects table is keyed by ID (or vice versa), the lookup fails

### iOS path
- Check the iOS API client's proposal list endpoint and parameters
- Is it sending project ID or project name in the filter?
- Does the backend API endpoint handle both?

## Key files to investigate
- backend/src/services/proposal-store.ts — getProposals(), project filter logic
- backend/src/services/mcp-tools/ — create_proposal and score_proposal tool handlers
- backend/src/services/mcp-server.ts — resolveProjectContextFromPath() for how project identity is resolved on MCP connections
- backend/src/routes/proposals.ts — REST endpoint project filter
- backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/auto-develop-loop.ts — buildReviewReason() uses { project: [projectId, projectName] }
- backend/src/services/projects-service.ts — project ID vs name vs path fields

## Acceptance criteria
- list_proposals({ project: 'auto-tank' }) returns auto-tank proposals
- list_proposals({ project: '71f9d993' }) returns the same proposals
- score_proposal works on auto-tank proposals when auto-develop is enabled
- iOS proposal list correctly filters by project
- Proposals created via MCP store a project identifier that is queryable by both name and ID
close_reason: Fixed both list_proposals (name→[id,name] resolution) and score_proposal (derive project from proposal, not session)

title: Add MCP tool for coordinator to advance auto-develop phase
description:
The coordinator drives the auto-develop loop by spawning agents in response to phase actions, but has no way to explicitly advance the phase. Currently phases only transition via a 30-minute heartbeat cron or internal scheduling with a 5-second delay after act() runs.

## Problem
After the coordinator completes a phase action (e.g., spawns reviewers for REVIEW), there's up to a 30-minute wait before the loop recognizes it should move to the next phase. The coordinator knows the work is done but can't tell the loop to advance.

## Proposed solution
Add an MCP tool: advance_auto_develop_phase (or transition_phase)

Parameters:
- projectId (required): which project
- targetPhase (optional): explicit phase to transition to, or auto-determine via determineNextPhase()

Behavior:
- Validates the transition is legal (e.g., can't skip from IDEATE to EXECUTE)
- Updates the phase in AdjutantState via setMeta(phaseKey(projectId), nextPhase)
- Emits auto_develop:phase_changed event
- Schedules the next coordinator wake with the new phase's reason/context
- Returns the new phase and scheduled check ID

## Where to implement
- New MCP tool in backend/src/services/mcp-tools/ (could go in a new auto-develop.ts or existing coordination tools)
- Phase transition logic already exists in auto-develop-loop.ts: determineNextPhase()
- State update: state.setMeta(phaseKey(project.id), nextPhase)
- Event emission: getEventBus().emit('auto_develop:phase_changed', ...)

## Acceptance criteria
- Coordinator can call the MCP tool to advance the phase immediately
- Transition validates phase ordering (no illegal jumps)
- Event bus emits phase_changed so dashboard updates
- Next coordinator wake is scheduled with phase-appropriate context
- 30-minute heartbeat still works as fallback if coordinator doesn't manually advance
close_reason: Implemented readiness detection for spawn prompts (adj-132) and advance_auto_develop_phase MCP tool (adj-135). All tests pass.

title: Auto-develop needs a persistent Product Owner agent per project
description:
Auto-develop agents frequently have product questions (scope, priority, tradeoffs) but have no one to ask. The user isn't watching (that's the point of auto-develop), and the coordinator is operational, not product-focused.

## Problem
- Engineering agents are told 'send questions to user, don't wait for response, assume and continue'
- This leads to potentially wrong assumptions piling up with no product oversight
- Reviewers score proposals but have no product context beyond README
- Nobody is making product decisions during the autonomous cycle

## Proposed solution: Product Owner agent
A persistent agent per auto-develop project that:
- Loads and deeply understands the project vision (README, CLAUDE.md, design docs)
- Answers product questions from engineering agents without blocking the loop
- Makes scope/priority/tradeoff decisions within the vision guardrails
- Reviews proposals from a product perspective (not just engineering feasibility)
- Escalates to the user ONLY for vision-level changes (new direction, major pivots)
- Persists across the full auto-develop cycle (not ephemeral like ideators/reviewers)

## Integration with auto-develop phases
- IDEATE: PO provides product direction to ideation agents
- REVIEW: PO scores proposals on product value, not just engineering quality
- GATE: PO input factors into accept/reject decisions
- EXECUTE: Engineering agents route product questions to PO instead of user
- VALIDATE: PO verifies delivered work matches product intent

## Design questions
- Is PO spawned once per project and kept alive, or respawned each cycle with context?
- Should PO have write access to the vision context (updating it as the project evolves)?
- How does PO differ from a squad leader? (PO doesn't write code or manage engineers)
- Should PO be a named Adjutant agent (dashboard visible) or a native teammate?
- Can one PO cover multiple projects, or is it strictly 1:1?

## Acceptance criteria
- Auto-develop cycle has a PO agent that answers product questions
- Engineering agents route questions to PO, not user
- PO escalates vision-level decisions to user
- PO persists across cycle phases (or is respawned with full context)
- User only gets pinged for strategic decisions, not tactical product questions

title: Auto-develop agents must not block on user questions — route to coordinator or assume
description:
In auto-develop mode, agents are instructed to send questions to the user via send_message({ to: 'user', body: '...' }) and wait for a response. This breaks the autonomous loop — the user may not be watching, and the whole point of auto-develop is hands-off operation.

## Problem
Current spawn prompts and PRIME.md instruct all agents:
- 'Route ALL questions to the General via MCP — never use AskUserQuestion'
- This sends a message to the user and the agent blocks waiting for a reply
- In auto-develop mode, no one is watching — the agent stalls indefinitely

## Fix
When an agent is spawned as part of an auto-develop cycle:

### Option A: Route questions to the coordinator
- Auto-develop spawn prompts should route questions to adjutant-coordinator instead of user
- The coordinator can answer operational questions autonomously (e.g., 'which directory?', 'what naming convention?')
- The coordinator escalates to the user only for vision/scope questions

### Option B: Assume and continue
- Auto-develop agents should state their assumption and continue without blocking
- e.g., 'Assuming TypeScript for the backend. Proceeding.' rather than 'Should I use TypeScript?'
- Log assumptions so they can be reviewed later

### Recommended: Both
- Route questions to coordinator (Option A)
- If coordinator doesn't respond within N minutes, assume and continue (Option B)
- Never block on user response during auto-develop cycles

## Where to fix
- Auto-develop spawn prompt templates (wherever ideation/execution agents are spawned)
- backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/auto-develop-loop.ts — the phase handlers that spawn agents
- skills/squad-execute/SKILL.md — if auto-develop uses squad execution
- May need a flag or context in the spawn prompt: 'You are in auto-develop mode — do not wait for user responses'

## Acceptance criteria
- Auto-develop agents never block waiting for user input
- Questions go to coordinator first, with fallback to assumption
- Assumptions are logged via MCP messages for later review
- Manual (user-directed) agents continue routing questions to user as before

title: spawn_worker prompt fails to land in agent tmux session
description:
When spawn_worker creates a new agent, the tmux session starts but the spawn prompt frequently fails to deliver into the agent's input. The agent receives PRIME.md via the Claude Code plugin SessionStart hook instead, and executes BOOTSTRAP (thinking it's a coordinator) rather than its assigned task.

## Incident
ideator-1 was spawned via spawn_worker with a detailed ideation prompt for auto-tank. The tmux session was created successfully, but the prompt never landed. The user had to manually submit the prompt. Meanwhile ideator-1 received PRIME.md and began bootstrapping as a coordinator.

## Root cause investigation needed
The spawn prompt is delivered via tmux input injection (set-buffer + paste-buffer + send-keys Enter). Known failure modes from adj-53kf and adj-twhj:
1. Claude Code may not be at the input prompt when the paste arrives (still loading, processing SessionStart hook)
2. The paste may arrive BEFORE Claude Code is ready to accept input
3. Race condition: SessionStart hook fires → PRIME.md injected → agent starts processing BOOTSTRAP → spawn prompt arrives too late or is ignored

## Key files
- backend/src/services/lifecycle-manager.ts — createSession() handles tmux session creation and prompt injection
- backend/src/services/input-router.ts — tmuxPasteText() handles the actual delivery mechanism
- PASTE_ENTER_DELAY_MS = 150ms may be insufficient for Claude Code startup time

## Possible fixes
1. Increase delay between session creation and prompt injection (wait for Claude Code to be ready)
2. Detect Claude Code readiness before injecting (poll for input prompt marker in tmux output)
3. Retry injection if no activity detected within N seconds
4. Queue the spawn prompt and deliver it AFTER SessionStart hook completes
5. Include the spawn prompt in the session environment so the plugin hook can deliver it instead of PRIME.md

## Acceptance criteria
- spawn_worker prompt reliably lands in the agent's tmux session on first attempt
- Agent executes the spawn prompt, not BOOTSTRAP
- No manual intervention required
close_reason: Implemented readiness detection for spawn prompts (adj-132) and advance_auto_develop_phase MCP tool (adj-135). All tests pass.

title: Beads have no ownership protection — any agent can overwrite or close any bead
description:
Beads have zero access control. Any agent can silently destroy any bead's content, close it, or reassign it without authorization.

## Confirmed incidents
- raynor destroyed coordinator's adj-127 by calling create_bead with the same ID
- raynor closed adj-126 which was assigned to a different agent

## Root cause
bd is an external CLI — we cannot modify its internals. Agents are instructed to use bd CLI directly (not MCP bead tools), so MCP-layer guards alone won't help.

## Fix: Add safety instructions to spawn prompts and PRIME.md

### 1. Before bd create --id=X
Agents MUST run bd show X first. If the bead already exists, do NOT create — use bd update instead.

### 2. Before bd update --title/--description
Agents MUST verify they are the assignee via bd show. Do not modify beads you don't own.

### 3. Before bd close
Agents MUST verify they are the assignee via bd show. Do not close beads you don't own.

## Where to add these instructions
- .adjutant/PRIME.md — Bead Tracking section
- skills/squad-execute/SKILL.md — engineer and QA spawn prompt templates
- Any other spawn prompt templates that reference bd create/update/close

## Also: MCP layer guard (defense in depth)
Add a duplicate ID check to create_bead in backend/src/services/mcp-tools/beads.ts — run bd show before bd create, reject if exists. This catches agents that use the MCP tool path.

## Acceptance criteria
- PRIME.md contains explicit bd show guard instructions before create/update/close
- squad-execute spawn prompt template includes the same guards
- MCP create_bead rejects duplicate IDs with a clear error
- Regression test: MCP create_bead with existing ID returns error
notes: IMPORTANT: bd is an external CLI tool — we cannot modify its internals. All fixes must be implemented at the MCP tool layer in backend/src/services/mcp-tools/beads.ts, which wraps bd commands via execBd(). The MCP layer already has agent identity via getAgentBySession() from mcp-server.ts — use this for ownership checks. Direct bd CLI usage by agents is unguarded but out of scope.

title: Worktree agents can't merge to main — fix spawn prompts and squad-execute skill
description:
Worktree-isolated agents cannot run 'git checkout main' because main is already checked out in the primary worktree. This causes agents to push their branch but skip the merge step silently. Squad leaders then report work as 'merged' without verifying.

Root cause discovered in adj-122: engineer-1 and engineer-7 pushed branches but never merged. Tassadar announced completion without checking. Two branches sat unmerged until manually discovered.

Engineer-9 explicitly reported: 'Cannot merge to main from this worktree — branch is ready for merge from the main repo.'

Fixes needed:
1. squad-execute SKILL.md Step 5 (engineer spawn prompt): Change merge instructions to 'Push your branch. The squad leader will merge to main from the main repo.'
2. squad-execute SKILL.md Step 10 (completion): Add verification step — 'Run git branch -r --no-merged main to confirm all agent branches are merged'
3. PRIME.md Team Agent Beads Protocol: Update step 5 to clarify worktree agents push only, squad leader merges
4. Squad leader spawn prompt: Add explicit post-completion merge checklist
5. Consider: should worktree agents attempt merge via 'git -C /path/to/main-repo merge' instead?
6. Spawn prompts MUST instruct agents to use 'npm test' (which maps to 'vitest run') and NEVER 'npx vitest' or 'vitest' directly — bare vitest starts in watch mode and persists as a zombie process after the agent dies, consuming 100%+ CPU per process. 14 zombie vitest processes were found running from dead adj-122 agents.

Reference: adj-122 (auto-develop epic), adj-osic (worktree isolation origin)

title: adjutant init should register projects with the backend
description:
Currently adjutant init only sets up local files (.adjutant/PRIME.md, .mcp.json, plugin hooks) but does NOT register the project in the backend database. Projects only get registered via:
1. discoverLocalProjects() on backend startup (CWD only)
2. POST /api/projects (manual REST call)
3. POST /api/projects/discover (filesystem scan)

This means running adjutant init in a new repo leaves it invisible to the dashboard, agents, and MCP routing.

Investigate the best approach:
- Should init call the backend API directly to register? (requires backend running)
- Should init register locally and backend picks it up on next startup? (e.g. a registry file)
- Should discover run automatically on a schedule or hook?
- How to handle the case where backend isn't running when init is called?
- Should adjutant upgrade also verify registration?

Success: Running adjutant init in any repo makes it appear as a project in the dashboard without manual steps.

title: Bug: phase_changed event always emits same value for previousPhase and newPhase
description: In auto-develop-loop.ts lines 604-609, the auto_develop:phase_changed event is emitted with previousPhase: currentPhase and newPhase: currentPhase — both set to the same value. This makes the event useless for tracking transitions. The event should emit the actual previous phase and the phase being transitioned to. Since the behavior doesn't advance phases itself (the coordinator does), this event may need to be moved or restructured, or it should at least reflect what the behavior is scheduling (e.g., the phase it's waking the coordinator for).

title: Bug Fixes + Query Tools
description: QA bug fixes from Phase 3/5 review plus original Phase 6 query tools work

title: Missing: No adjutant_decisions logging for confidence gate decisions
description:
Spec US2 acceptance criteria states: 'All gate decisions logged in adjutant_decisions'. The score_proposal MCP tool in auto-develop.ts only logs via logInfo() and emits a proposal:scored event -- it never writes to the adjutant_decisions table. This means there is no persistent audit trail of gate decisions as required by the spec (US2 AC, F3, and Success Criterion 6).

Fix: After computing the score and classification, write a row to adjutant_decisions with: decision type ('confidence_gate'), proposal ID, score, classification, signals JSON, and timestamp. The adjutant_decisions table already exists from migration 014.

title: Bug: computeNextFireAt drifts — uses Date.now() instead of lastFiredAt
description: computeNextFireAt() in cron-schedule-store.ts computes next fire as Date.now() + interval. The plan specifies nextFireAt = lastFiredAt + interval after each fire. When a fire is delayed (e.g., by 90s cooldown), the next fire time drifts forward instead of maintaining a fixed cadence. Over many fires, this accumulates significant drift. The function should accept a base timestamp parameter (lastFiredAt) and compute from there.

title: Bug: PATCH route sets visionContext even when enableAutoDevelop fails
description:
In backend/src/routes/projects.ts PATCH handler (lines 384-408), when both autoDevelop:true and visionContext are sent in the request body, if enableAutoDevelop() fails (returns !result.success), the code falls through to setVisionContext() on line 406. The `result` variable gets overwritten with the vision context result, masking the enableAutoDevelop failure. The response could return success when enableAutoDevelop actually failed, leaving the project in an inconsistent state (autoDevelop=false but visionContext set and enabled event emitted).

Fix: Add an early return after enableAutoDevelop failure, before reaching the visionContext block.

title: Make idle-proposal-nudge project-aware — agents handle own project proposals
description:
## Problem

The idle-proposal-nudge behavior in the stimulus engine dumps ALL pending proposals (across all projects) into every agent's SITUATION prompt. This causes:
1. C4 agents getting nudged on Adjutant proposals they can't access (cross-project MCP scoping blocks get_proposal/comment_on_proposal)
2. Wasted tokens listing 13+ irrelevant proposals in every SITUATION
3. Coordinator manually filtering proposals by project — should be automatic

## What to Fix

In backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/idle-proposal-nudge.ts:
- Filter proposals by the idle agent's active project before including in the SITUATION
- If the agent's project has < cap proposals, tell agent to create new ones
- If at/over cap, tell agent to review/improve existing ones for THEIR project
- Remove cross-project proposals entirely from the nudge

Also check: backend/src/services/adjutant/stimulus-engine.ts — the buildSituationPrompt may need project context passed through

## Goal
Each agent only sees proposals for their own project. No coordinator intervention needed — the nudge is self-contained and project-scoped.

## TDD Required

title: Slim down list_proposals MCP response — omit description, revisions, comments
description:
## Problem

The list_proposals MCP tool returns the full description field for every proposal. With 13+ pending proposals, this wastes hundreds of tokens on every call. The stimulus engine's idle-proposal-nudge includes this list in every SITUATION prompt, compounding the waste.

## What to Fix

In backend/src/services/mcp-tools/proposals.ts (list_proposals handler, ~line 371-380):
- Remove 'description' from the response — agents should call get_proposal(id) for full details
- Only return summary fields: id, author, title, type, project, status, createdAt
- Optionally add a 'descriptionPreview' field (first 100 chars) if some context is helpful

## Files
- backend/src/services/mcp-tools/proposals.ts — list_proposals handler
- backend/tests/unit/ — update any tests that assert on list_proposals response shape

## TDD Required
Write test first, then implement.
close_reason: Removed description from list_proposals, replaced with descriptionPreview (first 100 chars). TDD: 3 new tests.

This is just path enumeration (no bd commands), so no timeout risk. Fixes: prefix map completeness, listBeadSources dropdown, resolveBeadDatabase for external projects.
close_reason: Restored listBeadsDirs() to include registered projects from SQLite. Safe now that adj-117.1 prevents default 'all' scans.

Affected: listEpicsWithProgress (defaults to 'all'), listRecentlyClosed (hardcodes 'all'), getBead fallback (searches 'all'). This prevents timeout from serial scanning of 8+ databases on every page load.

title: Fix: create_proposal fails with 'No project context' when ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT unset
description:
## Bug

When an agent's ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT env var is not set, .mcp.json sends an empty string for X-Project-Root header. The backend's resolveProjectContext() in mcp-server.ts correctly rejects empty strings but has no fallback — storing undefined as the session's project context. This causes create_proposal (and any tool requiring project context) to fail with 'No project context'.

## Root Cause Chain

1. .mcp.json template: X-Project-Root: ${ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT:-} → empty string when env var unset
2. mcp-server.ts:172 — empty string rejected by length > 0 check
3. No fallback resolution — returns undefined
4. Session stores projectContext: undefined
5. proposals.ts:60-64 — getProjectContextBySession returns undefined → error

## Affected Agents

Any agent spawned without ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT set (Fenix hit this on C4 project). Agents on the Adjutant project itself may also be affected if env var not explicitly set.

## Fix Options

**Option A (recommended): Backend fallback in resolveProjectContext()**
- When X-Project-Root is empty/missing and no explicit projectId, fall back to the server's own CWD project (already registered via discoverLocalProjects() on startup)
- This is safe because the Adjutant backend always runs in the project root

**Option B: Spawn prompt fix**
- Ensure all spawn prompts set ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT before agent connects
- Doesn't fix agents that connect without being spawned by the coordinator

**Option C: Both A and B**
- Backend fallback for robustness + spawn prompt fix for correctness

## Files to Modify

- backend/src/services/mcp-server.ts — resolveProjectContext() around line 172-188
- Possibly .adjutant/PRIME.md — add env var requirement to spawn templates
close_reason: Added CWD fallback in resolveProjectContext() — agents without ADJUTANT_PROJECT_ROOT now resolve to the server's own project

title: [QA] Enum mismatch between route schema and DB CHECK constraint for blend_mode
description:
The updateLayerBodySchema and addLayerBodySchema in backend/src/routes/canvases.js (lines 43, 54) define blendMode enum as: ['seamless', 'overlay', 'multiply', 'screen', 'normal']. But the DB CHECK constraint in backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js (line 76) only allows: ('seamless', 'distinct', 'overlay'). The iOS BlendMode enum (CanvasLayer.swift line 11-15) matches the DB: seamless, distinct, overlay.

'multiply', 'screen', and 'normal' pass route validation but will be rejected by the DB CHECK constraint (500 error). 'distinct' is valid in DB/iOS but rejected by route validation.

Files: backend/src/routes/canvases.js:43,54; backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js:76; ios/.../CanvasLayer.swift:11-15
close_reason: Fixed: route schemas aligned with DB CHECK constraints

title: [QA] Enum mismatch between route schema and DB CHECK constraint for layer_type
description:
The addLayerBodySchema in backend/src/routes/canvases.js (line 38) defines layerType enum as: ['base', 'element', 'adjustment']. But the DB CHECK constraint in backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js (line 69) allows: ('base', 'element', 'character', 'effect', 'detail'). The iOS LayerType enum (CanvasLayer.swift line 3-9) matches the DB: base, element, character, effect, detail.

'adjustment' is accepted by the route schema but will be rejected by the DB CHECK constraint, causing a 500 error. Conversely, 'character', 'effect', and 'detail' are valid in the DB but rejected by the route schema, so the iOS client cannot create layers of those types.

Files: backend/src/routes/canvases.js:38, backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js:69, ios/.../CanvasLayer.swift:3-9
close_reason: Fixed: layerType enum aligned with DB CHECK constraint

title: [QA] Credit deduction is checked but never actually deducted
description:
All generation endpoints (generate-base, generate element, mutate, global pass) in backend/src/routes/canvases.js check credit balance but never actually deduct credits. The credit check reads `const account = await fastify.db('credit_accounts').first()` and compares balance, but there is no `decrement` or `update` call to reduce the balance after the generation is enqueued. This means users can generate unlimited content as long as they have >= 1 credit.

FR-007 requires: "System MUST deduct 1 credit per generation, mutation, or global pass."

Files: backend/src/routes/canvases.js lines 257-268 (generate-base), 352-360 (generate element), 409-418 (mutate), 461-470 (global pass).

Additionally, the credit check is not atomic — it reads balance then enqueues work without a transaction, so concurrent requests could race past the check before any deduction occurs (if deduction were added).
close_reason: Fixed: checkAndDeductCredits helper now decrements balance

title: PRIME.md: Strengthen MCP-only response enforcement

ALL responses MUST go through send_message MCP tool. The dashboard and iOS app ONLY see MCP messages — stdout is invisible to the user.' Place this as a bold, top-level rule in the MCP Communication section.

title: Investigate agent kill cascade — SIGKILL propagation across tmux sessions
description:
When Valerian ran 'lsof -ti:4201 | xargs kill -9' to kill a process on port 4201, multiple Claude Code agents in SEPARATE tmux sessions were also killed (Overmind PID 43475, Amon PID 68136, Valerian's own process). This should not happen — kill -9 on a port should only kill the process bound to that port, not unrelated processes in other tmux sessions.

Evidence from tmux capture-pane:
- adj-swarm-overmind: '[1] 43475 killed claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'
- adj-swarm-amon: '[1] 68136 killed claude --dangerously-skip-permissions'
- adj-swarm-valerian: also killed

Investigation tasks:
1. Determine if there was a parent process relationship (process group, session leader) that propagated SIGKILL
2. Check if concurrently or the dev server wrapper created a process group that included agent processes
3. Check if tmux session configuration shares process groups across sessions
4. Review scripts/dev-backend.sh (Overmind's adj-100 fix) for any process group implications
5. Propose a fix to prevent cascade kills — agents must be isolated from each other and from the dev server

Related: adj-085 (removed graceful shutdown handler), adj-100 (dev server crash resilience)
close_reason: Root cause confirmed: lsof -ti:PORT returns both server AND client PIDs. Fixed npm run kill to use -sTCP:LISTEN filter.

title: Bug: get_proposal rejects same-project proposals due to name vs UUID comparison
description: get_proposal MCP tool compares proposal.project (name string like 'adjutant') against the agent's scoped project UUID ('f1e8f895'), causing a false project mismatch error. Proposals created via create_proposal store the project UUID, but get_proposal's guard compares heterogeneous values. Related to adj-088 (MCP project scoping).

title: Define agent hierarchy doctrine: General → Coordinator → Squad Leaders → Squads
description: Codify the fundamental agent hierarchy into Adjutant's system documentation and agent protocols. The user is the General (strategic direction). The Adjutant-coordinator is the Executive Assistant (routes work, manages capacity, reports status — never does implementation). Adjutant agents (spawned via spawn_worker) are Squad Leaders — they own missions (epics, proposals, bugs), spawn native Claude Code teams for execution, manage their squads, update beads, commit/push/merge work, clean up worktrees, and report to the user via MCP. Squad Leaders can also execute work directly. This hierarchy must be reflected in PRIME.md, spawn prompts, skill instructions, and agent onboarding.

title: Bug: iOS discuss proposal button sends execute-proposal instead of discuss-proposal
description:
## Summary

In the iOS app, the button to discuss a proposal is incorrectly sending an execute-proposal message to agents instead of a discuss-proposal message. This means tapping "Discuss" on a proposal triggers execution instead of review/discussion.

## Expected Behavior
- Tapping the discuss button should send a message like: "Use /discuss-proposal <proposal-id>"
- The agent should analyze and discuss the proposal, not execute it

## Actual Behavior
- Tapping the discuss button sends: "Use /execute-proposal <proposal-id>"
- The agent starts executing the proposal instead of discussing it

## Likely Location
- iOS proposal detail view or proposal action buttons
- Look for where the discuss/execute button actions are wired up
- The message template probably has the wrong command string
close_reason: Fixed SwiftUI stale state capture in .sheet(isPresented:). Switched to .sheet(item:) so the mode is passed directly.

title: Create squad-execute skill for Adjutant plugin
description: New skill that orchestrates a full team: 1-8 staff engineers (worktree-isolated), QA sentinel, optional Product/UIUX reviewer, optional code review agent. Coordinator stays in coordinator mode, updates user via Adjutant MCP.
close_reason: Skill created, registered in marketplace, included in npm package

title: Bug: create_proposal fails for agents without project context — 'No project context' error
description:
## Problem

Multiple agents (adjutant-coordinator, potentially nick/nova) hit 'No project context — cannot create proposal' when calling create_proposal MCP tool. This was introduced by adj-072 (proposal project scoping enforcement).

## Root Cause

adj-072.1 added server-side enforcement: create_proposal auto-sets project from the agent's MCP session context and REJECTS if no project context exists. But not all agents have project context set in their MCP session — especially:
1. The adjutant-coordinator (Adjutant-scoped but may not have explicit project context)
2. Agents spawned for cross-project tasks
3. Agents whose sessions pre-date the project context feature

The enforcement is correct in principle (proposals should be project-scoped) but too aggressive — agents with no project context get a hard rejection instead of being prompted to specify a project.

## Observed Incidents
- Coordinator tried to create a C4 proposal and got 'No project context — cannot create proposal' — had to delegate to amon (C4-scoped)
- Nick may have hit similar issues when trying to register Nova's proposals retroactively (he was reviewing C4 proposals but his session project context is unknown)
- Nova never created proposals at all, possibly because she didn't know about the MCP tool or hit the same error

## Suggested Fix
1. When project context is missing, allow the agent to explicitly specify a project parameter in create_proposal (currently the client-supplied project is IGNORED per adj-072)
2. Fall back to requiring explicit project param rather than hard-rejecting
3. The current behavior of auto-setting from session context should remain the default — just don't reject when context is absent AND a project param is supplied
notes: User clarification: the actual issue was that no ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID env var was set for the agent. Without this, the MCP session has no agent identity, which means no project context lookup is possible. Fix should ensure agents always have ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID set, OR handle the missing env var gracefully by falling back to explicit project param.
close_reason: Duplicate of adj-115 (same create_proposal No project context bug)

title: [Product] Project update route doesn't accept default_style_preset_id — style lock can't be set
description:
**Spec gap**: The plan says `backend/src/routes/projects.js` should be modified to "Add `default_style_preset_id` to project update". The migration correctly adds the column to the `projects` table, but:

1. The PUT `/api/projects/:id` body schema doesn't include `defaultStylePresetId`
2. The project response schema doesn't include `default_style_preset_id`
3. The GET project endpoint doesn't return the field

This means the entire "project style lock" feature (spec acceptance scenario US2-4: "Given a project has a style lock set, When the user generates any content in that project, Then the locked style is automatically applied") has no backend support.

**Fix**: 
- Add `defaultStylePresetId` to the PUT body schema  
- Add `default_style_preset_id` to the project response schema  
- Return it from GET project endpoints
- The iOS project model may also need this field added

title: [Product] Generate routes don't accept stylePresetId or enhanced_prompt — spec integration not wired
description:
**Spec gap**: The plan (plan.md) explicitly states under "Backend — Modified Files":
> `backend/src/routes/generate.js` — Accept optional `stylePresetId` and `enhanced_prompt` fields

But the actual `generate.js` implementation has no `stylePresetId` or `enhancedPrompt` fields in the image or video body schemas. This means:

1. When a user selects a style and generates, the style association is lost
2. When a user enhances a prompt and generates, only the final prompt is stored — the original is lost
3. Prompt history can't be auto-recorded with the style and enhancement context
4. The Phase 5 auto-recording of prompt history (T023) will have no way to know which style was used

**Fix**: Add optional `stylePresetId` (UUID) and `enhancedPrompt` (string) to both `imageBodySchema` and `videoBodySchema`. Pass them through to the job data so the worker can record prompt history with full context.

title: [Product] Style presets list endpoint filters out custom styles — users can't see their own
description:
**Bug**: The `GET /api/styles` handler on line 42-43 of `styles.js` filters with `.where(function () { this.where('is_custom', false).orWhereNull('user_id'); })`. This means:
- System presets (`is_custom=false`) are returned (correct)
- Custom presets with `user_id=null` are returned (accidental edge case — single-user app has no user_id)
- Custom presets WITH a user_id would be filtered OUT

Since the app is single-user and the create endpoint doesn't set `user_id` (it's left as null from the insert), custom styles happen to work by accident via the `orWhereNull('user_id')` clause. But:

1. If user_id is ever populated (future multi-user), all custom styles break
2. The intent is clearly wrong — the query should include ALL custom styles for the current user, not exclude them
3. The spec says custom styles should appear in a "custom styles section" alongside system presets

**Fix**: Change the filter to simply return all presets (both system and custom). In a single-user app, there's no access control needed. The query should be: `.orderBy('is_custom').orderBy('category').orderBy('name')` — no filtering needed.

title: [Product] prompt_history missing project_id — cannot filter history by project
description:
**Spec gap**: The `prompt_history` table has no `project_id` column. The spec says prompt history should show "all past prompts" but in practice, creators work project-by-project. Without project association:

1. A creator with 10 projects sees prompts from ALL projects mixed together in history — noise overwhelms signal
2. The "Remix" flow (US3) can't scope to "prompts that worked for THIS project"
3. The project style lock feature (US2) implies project-scoped generation context, but history ignores this boundary
4. The P2 Learning Loop (US5) analytics can't compute per-project keep/regenerate ratios

**Fix**: Add `project_id UUID REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE SET NULL` to `prompt_history`. Add optional `projectId` query param to the history list endpoint. The iOS history view should default to current-project filtering with an "All Projects" toggle.

title: Product: ProjectDetailView not wired to StoryboardListView — no entry point from projects
description:
Task T017 requires wiring StoryboardListView into ProjectDetailView so users can navigate from a project to its storyboards. No worktree has modified ProjectDetailView to add a "Storyboards" section or navigation link. The StoryboardFeature package also hasn't been added to C4App.swift (T018). Without these, the storyboard feature is completely unreachable from the app's normal navigation flow.

Recommendation: Add a "Storyboards" section to ProjectDetailView with a NavigationLink to StoryboardListView(store:). Add StoryboardFeature as a dependency in the Xcode project/Package.swift and import it in C4App.swift.

title: Product: Schema mismatch between worktrees — scene_number vs order_index
description:
Two different worktrees have divergent database schemas for the scenes table. agent-a5b2e2e9 uses 'order_index' (matching the spec and iOS models). agent-a3e3efc6 uses 'scene_number' with a unique constraint. The batch generate and assemble routes in a3e3efc6 reference 'scene_number' for ordering, while the CRUD routes in a5b2e2e9 reference 'order_index'. When these are merged, one will break. The storyboard column name also differs ('script_text' vs 'script'). Additionally a3e3efc6 uses uuid_generate_v4() while a5b2e2e9 uses gen_random_uuid().

Recommendation: Standardize on 'order_index' (matching the spec's Scene entity definition and the iOS Scene model). Standardize on 'script_text'. Use gen_random_uuid() (built into PG 13+, no extension needed).

title: Product: ScriptInputView missing — no way to paste a script and trigger AI splitting
description:
US1 requires a ScriptInputView where users paste their script and tap "Split into Scenes." This view does not exist in any worktree. Without it, the entire storyboard entry point is broken — users cannot get from "I have a script" to scenes. The reducer has no splitScript action either. This is the #1 flow-blocking gap.

Recommendation: Create ScriptInputView.swift with a TextEditor, word count indicator, and a prominent "Split into Scenes" button. Add splitScript/scriptSplitCompleted actions to StoryboardTimelineReducer. Wire to POST /api/storyboards/:id/split.

title: Bug: Split handler delete + insert is not in a transaction — partial failure leaves inconsistent state
description:
In the split handler (POST /api/storyboards/:id/split), the code performs three sequential database operations without a transaction:

1. DELETE existing scenes: `await fastify.db('scenes').where({ storyboard_id: id }).del();`
2. INSERT new scenes: `await fastify.db('scenes').insert(sceneRecords).returning('*');`
3. UPDATE storyboard status to 'complete'

If step 2 fails after step 1 succeeds, all existing scenes are lost and no new ones are inserted, leaving the storyboard in a 'generating' state with zero scenes. This should be wrapped in a transaction.

Files: backend/src/routes/storyboards.js (split handler, lines 468-488)

title: Bug: iOS APIClient splitScript expects [Scene] but backend returns {storyboard, scenes} object
Bug: iOS APIClient splitScript posts to wrong URL path
description:
In the iOS APIClient (agent-a839a76a), splitScript sends a POST to:

    /api/storyboards/\(storyboardId)/split-script

But the backend route (agent-a5b2e2e9) registers the endpoint as:

    POST /api/storyboards/:id/split

The path mismatch means the iOS app will get a 404 when attempting to split a script.

Files: ios/C4/Packages/CoreKit/Sources/CoreKit/APIClient.swift (splitScript method), backend/src/routes/storyboards.js (split handler)

title: Bug: iOS APIClient reorderScenes uses PUT but backend route is PATCH
description:
In the iOS APIClient (agent-a839a76a worktree), the reorderScenes method uses `put()`:

    _ = try await put(
        "/api/storyboards/\(storyboardId)/scenes/reorder",
        body: Request(order: order),
        as: [Scene].self
    )

But the backend route (agent-a5b2e2e9) registers the reorder endpoint as PATCH:

    fastify.patch('/api/storyboards/:storyboardId/scenes/reorder', ...)

This will result in a 404 or 405 Method Not Allowed when the iOS app tries to reorder scenes.

Files: ios/C4/Packages/CoreKit/Sources/CoreKit/APIClient.swift, backend/src/routes/storyboards.js

title: Bug: Divergent 002_storyboards migration in agent-a3e3efc6 conflicts with routes and iOS models
description:
Two different versions of backend/src/db/migrations/002_storyboards.js exist across worktrees:

- agent-a3e3efc6: uses `script` column, `scene_number`, `float` duration, `uuid_generate_v4()`, `enu()` for status, includes `metadata` jsonb on both tables
- agent-a5b2e2e9: uses `script_text` column, `order_index`, `decimal(6,2)` duration, `gen_random_uuid()`, CHECK constraint for status, no metadata on storyboards

The routes (storyboards.js) and iOS models (Storyboard.swift, Scene.swift) all reference `script_text` and `order_index`, matching agent-a5b2e2e9. The a3e3efc6 migration must be discarded or reconciled before merge, or the routes will fail with column-not-found errors.

Impact: The a3e3efc6 migration also uses `uuid_generate_v4()` which requires the uuid-ossp extension (only created in 001_initial), while a5b2e2e9 uses `gen_random_uuid()` which is native to PG14+. Using the wrong one could cause migration failures on certain Postgres versions.

Files: backend/src/db/migrations/002_storyboards.js (both worktrees)

title: Bug: Agents without project context bypass cross-project checks on discuss/comment/revise
description: In discuss_proposal, comment_on_proposal, and revise_proposal, the cross-project check is: `if (projectContext && proposal.project !== projectContext.projectId)`. When projectContext is undefined (legacy/unscoped agent), the check is skipped entirely, allowing the agent to operate on proposals from ANY project. This is a bypass path for project scoping enforcement.

title: Fix: iOS SessionChatView clears content after cost_update events
description: cost_update events from capture-poll status bar scanning are forwarded to WS clients. iOS SessionChatView toggles from outputLines (replay buffer) to outputEvents when any structured event arrives. cost_update maps to .raw() and the view appears empty. Fix: filter cost_update from WS output forwarding — they are internal events for cost-tracker, not user-facing.
close_reason: Filtered cost_update events from WS/SSE output streams. iOS SessionChatView no longer switches from replay buffer to empty structured events.

title: Bug: Missing UNIQUE constraint on (proposal_id, revision_number) in proposal_revisions
description: Migration 019-proposal-comments-revisions.sql creates proposal_revisions with revision_number INTEGER NOT NULL but no UNIQUE(proposal_id, revision_number) constraint. The index idx_proposal_revisions_proposal is a performance index, not a uniqueness guarantee. Without this, duplicate revision numbers could be inserted if reviseProposal implementation has a bug or future changes introduce concurrency. Fix: add UNIQUE(proposal_id, revision_number) to the CREATE TABLE statement.

title: Tests for event-driven cost extraction

Mock SessionRegistry, SessionConnector, and EventBus.

title: Add agent:status_changed to EventMap type definition
description: Ensure the EventMap interface in event-bus.ts includes agent:status_changed with the correct payload type: { agentId: string, status: string, beadId?: string, task?: string }. This may already exist — verify and add if missing.

title: Subscribe to agent:status_changed and trigger one-shot cost extraction
description: In session-bridge.ts (or a new cost-extraction-listener service), subscribe to getEventBus().on('agent:status_changed'). On each event: (1) look up the agent's ManagedSession via SessionRegistry.findByName(agentId), (2) if session exists and has a tmux pane, call the one-shot extraction method from adj-066.5.1, (3) if cost/context data is returned, call recordCostUpdate() with the extracted data. Add debouncing (e.g. 2s) to avoid excessive extraction if an agent emits rapid status changes.
close_reason: Implemented event-driven cost extraction subscriber with debouncing

title: Emit agent:status_changed on EventBus from set_status MCP tool
description: Currently set_status in mcp-tools/status.ts writes to EventStore (persistent audit log) but does NOT emit on the real-time EventBus. Add getEventBus().emit('agent:status_changed', { agentId, status, beadId, task }) after updating agentStatuses. This enables other services to react to status changes without polling.

title: Add one-shot cost extraction method to SessionConnector
description: Create a new method extractCostOnce(sessionId) in session-connector.ts that does a single tmux capture-pane call, runs extractStatusBarCost() on the output, and returns the cost/context data without starting the full polling loop. This is the lightweight extraction path for event-driven triggers. Should reuse existing extractStatusBarCost() logic but not start startCapturePoll(). Needs the tmux session/pane reference from SessionRegistry.

title: Event-driven cost/context extraction on agent status change
description: Currently cost/context data is only captured when a user opens the full session terminal view (which starts tmux capture-pane polling). If the user only uses normal chat on iOS/web, the polling never starts and cost/context are never recorded. Fix: subscribe to the EventBus agent:status_changed event, and on each status change, do a one-shot tmux capture-pane for that agent's session to extract current cost and context percentage. This decouples cost extraction from the UI — costs are always tracked regardless of which view is open.
close_reason: All 5 tasks merged to main — event-driven cost/context extraction complete, 2643 tests passing

title: Fix iOS epic detail 'data missing' — bd show dependency mapping returns empty objects
description: bd show --json returns dependencies as full issue objects ({id, title, dependency_type, ...}) but getBead() in beads-queries.ts maps them expecting {issue_id, depends_on_id, type}. All three fields evaluate to undefined, resulting in empty {} objects in the API response. iOS BeadDependency has non-optional fields so decoding fails, showing error page.
close_reason: Fixed backend dependency mapping (bd show format detection) + iOS graceful decoding fallback

title: Bug: sessionBeadTracker not restored on backend restart — delta tracking breaks
description: sessionBeadTracker (Map<string, BeadSnapshot>) is in-memory only and not restored from SQLite during loadCacheFromDb(). On backend restart, the first cost_update for a still-alive session enters the 'no tracker' branch, sets costAtStart=0, and creates a new row with delta=full-session-cost. This means the old bead's finalized delta is correct, but the new row after restart captures ALL session cost as if it belongs to whatever bead the session is currently on. Fix: during loadCacheFromDb, reconstruct sessionBeadTracker from the latest non-finalized rows — set beadId from the row, costAtStart from SUM of all prior finalized deltas for that session, and rowId from the row's id.

title: Bug: loadCacheFromDb restores delta cost as session total after delta tracking
description: After engineer-1's delta tracking changes, total_cost in agent_costs rows is a DELTA (cost for one bead segment), not the cumulative session total. But loadCacheFromDb() still loads it directly as the CostEntry.cost value. On backend restart, if a session worked on 3 beads with deltas $3, $4, $5, the cache entry gets cost=$5 (latest row's delta) instead of $12 (actual session total). This means getCostSummary() and agent cards show wrong session costs after restart. Fix: loadCacheFromDb should SUM(total_cost) across all non-finalized rows for a session to reconstruct the true session total.

title: Bug: bead_id overwritten on bead switch loses old bead cost attribution
description: upsertSessionCost() uses COALESCE(?, bead_id) — when an agent switches from bead A to bead B, the single row's bead_id is updated to B. Now getBeadCost('A') returns null because no rows have bead_id='A' anymore. The existing test 'should deduplicate sessions in epic cost when session spans multiple beads' (line 419-430) demonstrates this: it asserts totalCost=15.0 (the session total on bead B), but bead A's $10.00 of work is invisible. This is the core bug that adj-066.1.3 (delta tracking) is supposed to fix — flagging so the engineers know the test assertion itself is wrong and needs updating.
close_reason: Fixed by engineer-1's delta tracking (adj-066.1.3). New cost rows are inserted on bead change, preserving old bead's cost attribution.

title: Bug: budget alerts fire repeatedly on every cost update (no dedup)
description: checkAndEmitBudgetAlerts() in cost-tracker.ts emits budget_warning or budget_exceeded events on EVERY recordCostUpdate() call once the threshold is crossed. Unlike the flat alert threshold (which uses alertedSessions Set for dedup), budget alerts have no dedup mechanism. A session updating cost 100 times after crossing the warning threshold will emit 100 budget_warning events. This floods EventBus/WebSocket/iOS with duplicate alerts.

title: Bug: burn rate query sums cumulative totals instead of deltas
description: getBurnRate() in cost-tracker.ts queries SUM(total_cost) across agent_costs rows within time windows. But total_cost is the cumulative session cost, not a delta. If a session at $5.00 total gets 3 upserts in 10 minutes, burn rate sees $15 instead of the actual $5. This produces wildly inflated burn rate numbers. Fix: either store deltas per row, or track the cost at the start of each window and subtract.

title: Bug: dolt nil pointer dereference in post-checkout git hook during worktree creation
description: Intermittent nil pointer dereference crash in dolt when the post-checkout git hook runs during worktree creation (git worktree add). Slagyr hit this spawning agents for adj-058 — 2 of 4 worktree spawns needed retry. The post-checkout hook triggers bd sync which calls dolt, and dolt crashes with a nil pointer. This blocks reliable agent spawning with worktree isolation. Fix: investigate the post-checkout hook, add error handling or guard against the nil pointer condition.
close_reason: Fixed: worktree guard in post-checkout hook. Dolt embedded mode panics on concurrent access from parallel worktree checkouts. Hook now detects worktree context ([ -f .git ]) and skips — import is redundant since dolt db is shared.

title: BUG: Stimulus engine triggerWatch fires on coordinator:action — adjutant can wake on its own actions
description: In backend/src/index.ts line 245, stimulusEngine.triggerWatch(event, data) is called for ALL EventBus events including coordinator:action. If the adjutant registers a watch_for with event='coordinator:action' (or any wildcard-like pattern), it would trigger on its own actions, causing an immediate or cooldown-delayed wake. This is a potential infinite loop: adjutant acts -> coordinator:action event -> watch triggers -> adjutant wakes -> adjutant acts again. The cooldown (90s) prevents true runaway but still wastes tokens. Fix: either filter coordinator:action from triggerWatch, or document that watch_for should never target coordinator:action.

title: BUG: Signal aggregator feedback loop — coordinator:action events feed back into adjutant context
description: In backend/src/index.ts line 242-243, the EventBus onAny handler feeds ALL events into signalAggregator.ingest(). When coordinator:action events are emitted, they hit the default case in signal-aggregator.ts classify() (line 101-103) and are classified as context signals. These accumulate in the context buffer and are included in the situation prompt via snapshot(). This means the adjutant's own actions appear as context signals in its next wake, creating unnecessary noise and potential for the adjutant to react to its own actions. coordinator:action should either be filtered out before ingestion, or the classify() function should return a new 'ignore' classification for it.

title: Bug: initialPrompt must NOT be removed from CreateSessionRequest — used by spawn_worker
description: CRITICAL: The spec says "System MUST NOT use initialPrompt or --prompt for persona injection" (FR-005), but initialPrompt is ALSO used by the spawn_worker MCP tool (coordination.ts:155) and agent-spawner-service.ts for non-persona task prompt injection. If engineer-1 removes initialPrompt support from CreateSessionRequest or LifecycleManager, it will break spawn_worker — which is how agents spawn teammates with task instructions. The correct change is: remove `initialPrompt: personaPrompt` from the persona-specific code paths in agents.ts and sessions.ts, but leave the initialPrompt field and its handling in lifecycle-manager.ts completely intact.
