title: QA: shouldAct rejects non-idle events, preventing debounce reset — agents only get one nudge
description: BUG in idle-proposal-nudge: shouldAct() returns false for non-idle agent:status_changed events, but act() contains debounce-clearing logic for non-idle transitions. Since the behavior registry only calls act() when shouldAct() returns true, the debounce key is never cleared when an agent goes from idle to working. Result: agent goes idle->working->idle and the second idle never triggers a scheduleCheck. Fix: shouldAct must return true for ALL agent:status_changed events so act() can handle both idle (schedule) and non-idle (clear debounce) paths. The test also masks this bug because it calls act() directly without going through shouldAct().
close_reason: Fixed: shouldAct now returns true for all agent:status_changed events

title: Bug: tmux send-keys Enter not reliably submitting prompts
description: The 50ms delay fix (adj-53kf) didn't resolve the issue. Prompts still get stuck in the tmux input buffer without submitting. Need to investigate: longer delays, load-buffer/paste-buffer approach, or fundamental send-keys -l issues with long prompts.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced racy send-keys -l + Enter with atomic set-buffer + paste-buffer. Trailing newline in buffer acts as Enter, eliminating the race condition entirely.

title: Investigate broken push notifications
description: Push notifications stopped working. Investigate root cause and fix.
close_reason: Fixed: reactive BadDeviceToken cleanup, APNS env from config, announcement handler in iOS. 4 new tests, 2189 passing.

title: Fix voice synthesis spam on iOS app resume — make TTS on-demand only
description: When the iOS app resumes after idle, BeadStatusMonitor and OverseerMailAnnouncer detect all changes that happened during idle and fire synthesis API calls for each one, spamming the API. Voice synthesis should only happen on-demand (user taps play), not automatically from polling or push notifications.
close_reason: Removed automatic voice synthesis from BeadStatusMonitor and OverseerMailAnnouncer. TTS is now on-demand only.

title: update_memory + reinforce_memory MCP tools
description: Add two MCP tools: (1) update_memory — update an existing learning's content, confidence, category, or topic by ID. Calls memoryStore.updateLearning(). (2) reinforce_memory — increment confidence on a learning the agent found useful. Calls memoryStore.reinforceLearning(). Both in backend/src/services/mcp-tools/memory.ts. TDD: tests first in backend/tests/unit/mcp-memory.test.ts.

title: store_memory MCP tool — create new learnings
description: Add store_memory MCP tool to backend/src/services/mcp-tools/memory.ts. Accepts: content (string), category (operational|technical|coordination|project), topic (string), source (string, e.g. 'adjutant-observation'), confidence (0-1, default 0.5). Calls memoryStore.insertLearning(). Include input validation via Zod. TDD: write tests first in backend/tests/unit/mcp-memory.test.ts.

title: MCP memory write tools for adjutant agent
description: The memory store has full CRUD (insertLearning, updateLearning, reinforceLearning, supersedeLearning) but the MCP tools only expose query_memories and get_session_retros. The adjutant agent cannot store learnings, record corrections, or reinforce existing memories. Add MCP tools: store_memory, update_memory, reinforce_memory, record_correction. This completes the memory system loop — agents can learn from experience and persist it.

title: Adjutant-only access guard for coordination tools

title: Signal Aggregator
description: Unified signal buffer with urgency classification, deduplication, and auto-expiry. Foundation for reactive prompting.

title: Intelligent Adjutant — Signal-Driven Coordination
description: Replace disabled adj-052 automation with event-driven intelligence. The Overmind pattern: critical events wake the adjutant, it reasons and acts, then schedules its own follow-ups. No polling, no cron timers.

title: shouldDecayConfidence always returns true because lastValidatedAt is never set
description: The memory-reviewer's shouldDecayConfidence function (line 260) checks if learning.lastValidatedAt is null and returns true for decay. However, lastValidatedAt is NEVER SET anywhere in the codebase. Neither reinforceLearning (which only updates confidence, reinforcement_count, updated_at) nor insertLearning nor any other code path ever calls updateLearning with lastValidatedAt. Result: every learning will ALWAYS be decayed during weekly review, including freshly created and recently reinforced ones. Fix: either (a) set lastValidatedAt when reinforceLearning is called, or (b) change shouldDecayConfidence to check updatedAt instead. File: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/memory-reviewer.ts lines 259-266, backend/src/services/adjutant/memory-store.ts lines 248-254
close_reason: Fixed: shouldDecayConfidence falls back to updatedAt

title: Memory-reviewer weekly review only fires once then becomes permanent no-op
description: The act() method guards with if(\!lastReviewAt) -> startup, else if(\!lastWeeklyReviewAt) -> weekly, else skip. Once both meta keys are set after the first startup and first weekly review, the behavior permanently skips all subsequent events and cron ticks. The weekly review never runs again. The schedule '0 0 * * 1' (Monday midnight) is meaningless because the guard prevents re-execution. Fix: the weekly review guard should check if enough time has elapsed since last_weekly_review_at (e.g., >= 7 days), not just whether the key exists. File: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/memory-reviewer.ts lines 62-78
close_reason: Fixed: weekly review fires when 7+ days elapsed

title: Bug: Memory-reviewer startup/weekly logic is broken — weekly review fires on 2nd agent event, not on Monday schedule
description: Found in adj-053.4.1 (memory-reviewer.ts). The act() method checks: if no last_review_at -> startup review. Else if no last_weekly_review_at -> weekly review. Else skip. This means: (1) First agent:status_changed fires startup review, sets last_review_at. (2) SECOND agent:status_changed fires weekly review (because last_review_at exists but last_weekly_review_at doesn't), even if it's not Monday. (3) All subsequent events are skipped forever — the cron schedule '0 0 * * 1' never triggers useful work because both meta keys are set. The fix: startup and weekly should be independent checks. Weekly should check if last_weekly_review_at is older than 7 days (not just if it exists). The cron should reset last_weekly_review_at, not the event trigger.
close_reason: Superseded by adj-89r7 — the Phase 4 implementation was rewritten before merging to main, but the same root bug (weekly review only fires once) persists in the new version

title: Bug: Session retrospective metrics count ALL decisions, not just today's or since last retro
description: Found in adj-053.3.1 (session-retrospective.ts). gatherMetrics() calls state.getRecentDecisions(500) which returns the most recent 500 decisions regardless of date. beadsClosed/beadsFailed counts are for ALL TIME, not for 'today' or 'since last retro'. The spec says 'Gather metrics since last retro.' Similarly, correctionsReceived calls getUnresolvedCorrections() which returns ALL unresolved corrections, not ones from today's session. This makes retrospective metrics meaningless — a retro generated after 100 days of accumulated work would show inflated numbers. Fix: Either filter decisions by createdAt >= lastRetroDate, or pass a timeframe to the metrics gatherer.
close_reason: Fixed: metrics filtered to current retro period

title: Bug: findSimilarLearnings called with raw message body — inherits FTS5 injection from adj-adqi
description: Found in adj-053.2.1 (memory-collector.ts line ~135). The correction detection flow calls memoryStore.findSimilarLearnings(topic, body) where body is the raw user message. This passes directly to FTS5 MATCH without sanitization (see adj-adqi). A user message containing FTS5 syntax like 'don't use NOT IN queries' would crash findSimilarLearnings. The try/catch in findSimilarLearnings returns [] on error, which means the dedup check silently fails and a new duplicate learning is created instead of reinforcing an existing one. This is a concrete exploitation path for adj-adqi.
close_reason: Store-level FTS5 sanitization covers the memory-collector callsite

title: Bug: correction:detected event is never emitted by memory-collector
description: Found in adj-053.2.1 (memory-collector.ts). The EventBus has a correction:detected event type (added in adj-053.1.3), and the plan says the memory-collector should emit it. But the implementation only emits learning:created (line ~178). correction:detected is never emitted anywhere. This breaks any downstream consumer subscribing to correction:detected, including potentially the session-retrospective behavior which needs to count corrections. The fix is to emit correction:detected before creating the learning, as the plan specifies: 'If match: emit correction:detected, then create learning'.

title: Bug: Decommissioner must exclude the Adjutant coordinator from shutdown targets
description:
The spec for adj-052.2.4 (Agent Decommissioner) says: find agents idle 30+ minutes with no pending work, send graceful shutdown. But the Adjutant coordinator agent (`adjutant-coordinator`) itself may appear idle for extended periods between heartbeat cycles (it's a long-running session that wakes on prompts). If the decommissioner doesn't explicitly exclude it, it will send a shutdown request to the Adjutant itself, killing the coordinator.

The health-monitor behavior (health-monitor.ts) separately ensures the Adjutant is alive. If the decommissioner kills it and health-monitor respawns it, you get a decommission/respawn thrashing loop every 30 minutes.

**Impact**: P1 — killing the coordinator paralyzes the entire system. All behaviors lose their prompt-injection target, summaries stop, and work assignment stalls until health-monitor respawns it.

**Fix**: The decommissioner's shouldAct() or act() must have an explicit exclusion list containing at minimum `adjutant-coordinator` (imported from adjutant-spawner.ts as ADJUTANT_SESSION_NAME). Consider a configurable `protected_agents` list in metadata.

**Files**: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/agent-decommissioner.ts (to be created per adj-052.2.4), backend/src/services/adjutant-spawner.ts (ADJUTANT_SESSION_NAME constant)
close_reason: Fixed: decommissioner explicitly excludes adjutant-coordinator and adjutant agent IDs

title: Bug: LifecycleManager.createSession ignores workspaceType — no worktree creation
description:
The spec for adj-052.2.3 (Agent Spawner behavior) requires spawned agents to use worktree isolation (mandatory per project rules and CLAUDE.md). However, LifecycleManager.createSession() (lifecycle-manager.ts:106-208) accepts a `workspaceType` parameter but never acts on it. It stores the value in the registry (line 151) but never calls `git worktree add` or sets up a worktree directory.

Only swarm-service.ts (line 132-138) actually creates worktrees via `git worktree add`. If the agent-spawner behavior calls `getSessionBridge().lifecycle.createSession({ workspaceType: 'worktree' })` as the spec says, the spawned agent will run in the main project directory despite the registry claiming it's in a worktree.

**Impact**: P1 — spawned agents will share the main working directory, causing the exact silent file overwrite problem documented in adj-osic. This directly contradicts the mandatory worktree isolation rule.

**Fix**: Either (a) LifecycleManager.createSession must handle worktree creation when workspaceType=worktree, or (b) the agent-spawner behavior must create the worktree itself before calling createSession with the worktree path as projectPath, or (c) use swarm-service.ts to spawn agents. The cleanest approach is (a): add worktree setup to createSession.

**Files**: backend/src/services/lifecycle-manager.ts (createSession method), backend/src/services/swarm-service.ts (reference implementation)
close_reason: Accepted: workspaceType is stored in registry for future use. Current spawner uses swarm mode which delegates workspace setup to Claude Code --worktree. Full worktree creation in LifecycleManager is deferred to a future improvement.

title: Bug: TOCTOU race in LifecycleManager max-session cap check
description:
LifecycleManager.createSession() (lifecycle-manager.ts:106-113) checks `registry.size >= maxSessions` then proceeds to create the tmux session asynchronously. Between the check and the registry.create() call at line 145, another concurrent createSession call (e.g., from the spawner cron + a bead:created event firing simultaneously) can also pass the guard, leading to exceeding the max-session limit.

**Impact**: The max_concurrent_agents cap (default 5) can be silently exceeded when two spawn paths race (e.g., agent-spawner periodic check + bead:created event trigger). This wastes machine resources and can snowball into many excess agents.

**Reproduction**: Two async calls to createSession nearly simultaneously when registry.size == maxSessions - 1. Both pass the guard, both create sessions, registry ends up with maxSessions + 1 entries.

**Fix**: Add a createSession mutex/lock in LifecycleManager (similar to work-assigner's `assigning` guard) so only one createSession executes at a time. Or use an atomic check-and-reserve pattern.

**Files**: backend/src/services/lifecycle-manager.ts (lines 106-113)
close_reason: Accepted: TOCTOU in LifecycleManager is bounded — max 1 extra session per race window. The spawner's own concurrency lock + 60s cooldown make double-spawn unlikely. Not worth adding a mutex to LifecycleManager for this edge.

title: Bug: searchLearnings and findSimilarLearnings don't sanitize FTS5 query input
description: Found in adj-053.1.2 (memory-store.ts). The searchLearnings() method passes user input directly to FTS5 MATCH without sanitization. FTS5 special syntax like *, NEAR, NOT, OR, AND, column:value can be injected. This causes: (1) FTS5 syntax errors that crash the query, (2) unintended query behavior. The existing message-store.ts correctly sanitizes by wrapping in double quotes. findSimilarLearnings() has a try/catch that partially mitigates this, but searchLearnings() has no protection at all. Reproduction: store.searchLearnings('NOT valid OR *') will either crash or return unexpected results. Expected: Apply the same sanitization pattern as message-store.ts: const sanitized = '"' + query.trim().replace(/"/g, '""') + '"'. This is the most critical bug found — unsanitized FTS5 input is the #1 gotcha in SQLite full-text search.
close_reason: FTS5 sanitization added to searchLearnings and findSimilarLearnings via sanitizeFts5Query helper

title: Bug: Heartbeat prompt injection delivers duplicate/stacked prompts to adjutant agent
description: The heartbeat mechanism that injects prompts into the adjutant coordinator's tmux session is delivering multiple duplicate copies of the same heartbeat prompt in a single turn. In the latest instance, 5 identical HOURLY HEARTBEAT CHECK prompts were stacked into one message, along with duplicate copies of earlier instructions ('add monitor-only rule' appeared 3 times, 'what is your context' appeared 4 times). This wastes context window tokens, causes redundant processing, and creates confusing echo loops where the agent's own nudge messages bounce back. The injection mechanism needs deduplication — either debounce at the sender, or detect+discard duplicate prompts at the receiver.
close_reason: Fixed: added 50ms delay between text and Enter in tmux send-keys, plus per-pane deduplication in InputRouter

title: Retro analysis + generation logic
description: Add analysis logic to session-retrospective: generate went_well (high throughput, zero corrections, successful assignments), went_wrong (recurring corrections, build failures, stale agents, orphaned beads), action_items (derived from went_wrong + unresolved corrections from memory store). Format as structured JSON arrays. Insert retrospective row via memory store. Send summary to user via comm.sendImportant() with formatted markdown. Update meta key last_retro_at. File: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/session-retrospective.ts

title: Create session-retrospective behavior
description: Create session-retrospective behavior implementing AdjutantBehavior. Name: session-retrospective. Triggers: [] (schedule-only). Schedule: '0 23 * * *' (daily 11 PM). shouldAct: always true. act: gather metrics since last retro (check meta key last_retro_at): count beads closed from decisions log, count corrections inserted today from memory store, count distinct active agents, calculate avg bead time. Supersedes adj-052.4.5. TDD mandatory. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/session-retrospective.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/session-retrospective.test.ts

Add unit tests for all scoring edge cases. File: backend/src/services/adjutant/memory-store.ts

title: Add bead outcome capture to memory-collector

Detect failure patterns: bead was reopened (check decisions log for reopen actions), bead spawned bug children, bead took multiple attempts (same agent assigned/unassigned). On failure pattern: create learning with source_type=bead_outcome, source_ref=beadId. On agent:status_changed to blocked/disconnected while working on bead: log potential learning about task difficulty. File: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/memory-collector.ts

title: Create memory-collector behavior with correction detection

On mail:received from user: match body against CORRECTION_PATTERNS regex array (don't/never/stop + verb, always/must/should + verb, remember/note/important, wrong/mistake + should/instead). If match: infer category (operational/technical/coordination/project) from keyword sets, extract topic, create learning via memory store, emit correction:detected and learning:created events. Dedup: before creating, call findSimilarLearnings — if match exists, reinforce instead. TDD mandatory. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/memory-collector.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/memory-collector.test.ts

Retrospectives: insertRetrospective, getRecentRetrospectives. Corrections: insertCorrection, findSimilarCorrection, incrementRecurrence, getUnresolvedCorrections. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/memory-store.ts + backend/tests/unit/adjutant/memory-store.test.ts

Add FTS5 virtual table adjutant_learnings_fts with insert/update/delete triggers. Add indexes on category, topic, confidence, correction learning_id, retro date. title: Bug: Work-assigner using raw execBd will not emit bead:updated or bead:assigned events
description: The work-assigner will call bd update <id> --assignee=<agent> --status=in_progress via execBd (bd-client.ts). But execBd is a raw CLI wrapper — it does NOT emit EventBus events. Events are only emitted in two places: (1) beads-mutations.ts updateBead() function (line 139), which is used by the REST API route. (2) mcp-tools/beads.ts update_bead tool handler, which also emits events via eventStore. If work-assigner uses execBd directly, then: (a) No bead:updated event fires, so bead-assign-notification won't send the agent a message. (b) No bead:assigned event fires (even after adj-052.1.2 adds it), so Phase 3 agent-spawner won't trigger. (c) The frontend won't get real-time WebSocket updates about the assignment. (d) The eventStore timeline won't record the assignment. The work-assigner must either: (a) Use the beads-mutations.updateBead() service function which handles event emission, or (b) Manually emit the appropriate events after a successful execBd call. Option (a) is preferred as it avoids duplicating event emission logic.
close_reason: Fixed: both work-assigner and work-rebalancer use updateBead() from beads-mutations.ts

title: Bug: Work-rebalancer should not unassign beads from blocked agents
description: The work-rebalancer spec says: 'When an agent goes blocked or disconnects, check their in-progress beads.' Treating 'blocked' the same as 'disconnected' is incorrect. An agent that reports status=blocked is still alive and working — it's indicating it's stuck on something (e.g. waiting for a dependency, permission issue, build failure). Unassigning its beads would: (1) Lose the agent's in-progress work and context. (2) Cause another agent to be assigned the same bead, duplicating effort. (3) When the blocked agent becomes unblocked, it would continue working on a bead now assigned to someone else. 'Disconnected' means the agent is gone (crashed, shut down). 'Blocked' means the agent is present but needs help. The correct behavior for blocked agents is to escalate to the user or try to resolve the blocker, NOT to strip their work. Fix: Only unassign beads for disconnected agents. For blocked agents, send a message asking what the blocker is and/or escalate to the user after a timeout (e.g. 30 minutes blocked without progress).
close_reason: Fixed: work-rebalancer triggers only on mcp:agent_disconnected, not blocked status

title: Bug: Work-assigner must use bd ready, not bd list --status=open for unblocked beads
description: The spec says work-assigner should find 'ready beads' (open + unblocked + unassigned). The bd CLI has a dedicated 'bd ready' command with blocker-aware semantics. The bd ready --help explicitly warns: 'bd list --ready is NOT equivalent — it only filters by status=open.' If the work-assigner uses bd list --status=open (as the MCP list_beads tool does today), it will return beads that have unfulfilled dependencies. Assigning a blocked bead to an agent is actively harmful — the agent can't make progress and will sit idle or waste tokens. Fix: The work-assigner MUST call bd ready --json --unassigned (not bd list). Additionally, bd ready supports --limit, --sort=priority, and --parent for epic scoping, which align well with the matching algorithm requirements.
close_reason: Fixed: work-assigner uses bd ready --json, not bd list --status=open

title: Bug: Race condition — concurrent act() calls can double-assign beads
description: In adjutant-core.ts, dispatchEvent() calls behavior.act() as fire-and-forget (line 101: behavior.act(...).catch(...)). There is no await and no serialization between concurrent act() invocations. This creates a race condition for work-assigner: (1) Two agents go idle simultaneously, emitting two agent:status_changed events. (2) Both events trigger work-assigner. Both shouldAct() calls pass the 30s debounce check. (3) Both act() calls read the same bead list and try to assign the same highest-priority bead. (4) Result: two bd update --assignee calls for the same bead, or the second overwrites the first. Similarly, the 5-minute cron tick can race with an event-triggered run. The debounce (stored as setMeta in SQLite) might prevent some races but the check-then-act pattern is inherently non-atomic — shouldAct reads the timestamp, but the timestamp isn't updated until act() completes the assignment. Fix: Add a per-behavior mutex or in-flight lock so only one work-assigner act() runs at a time. The stale-agent-nudger uses an in-memory Map for debounce which also would not protect against this.
close_reason: Fixed: work-assigner uses in-memory boolean lock to prevent concurrent act() calls

title: Bug: bead:created event never emitted — work-assigner trigger is dead code
description: The work-assigner behavior lists bead:created as a trigger event, but bead:created is NEVER emitted anywhere in the codebase. The BeadCreatedEvent type exists in event-bus.ts (line 78) and is in the EventMap, but no code path calls getEventBus().emit('bead:created', ...). Neither beads-mutations.ts, mcp-tools/beads.ts, nor any other service emits this event. This means the work-assigner will never react to new beads being created — it will only run on agent:status_changed, bead:closed, or the 5-minute cron sweep. This is a critical gap: if a bead is created and an idle agent is already waiting, the assignment won't happen until the next cron tick (up to 5 minutes later). Fix: Either (a) add bead:created emission to the create_bead MCP tool handler and the REST bead creation path, or (b) remove bead:created from work-assigner triggers and document reliance on cron sweep. Option (a) is strongly recommended.
close_reason: Fixed: engineer-5 added bead:created emission to MCP create_bead tool

title: Integrate Phase 3 behaviors into index.ts

Logic: find agents idle for 30+ minutes with no pending work, send graceful shutdown request, escalate if no response in 5 min. TDD. Files: backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/agent-decommissioner.ts, backend/tests/unit/adjutant/behaviors/agent-decommissioner.test.ts

title: Agent Spawner behavior
description: Create behaviors/agent-spawner.ts. Triggers: bead:created, bead:assigned. Schedule: */10 min capacity check. title: Bug: Message and Proposal models allocate ISO8601DateFormatter in computed date properties

Proposal.relativeDate (Proposal.swift:64-68) calls createdDate then also allocates a RelativeDateTimeFormatter. ProposalDetailViewModel.formattedUpdatedDate (line 86-88) allocates 2x formatter inline. These are lower frequency than Bead sorts but contribute to cumulative main-thread blocking.

title: Bug: Duplicate DataSyncService.start() on app launch
description: ContentView.task{} at line 46 calls DataSyncService.shared.start() synchronously. AdjutantApp.onChange(scenePhase: .active) at line 28 also calls DataSyncService.shared.start(). The .active scene phase fires during the first launch, and .task{} also fires -- both trigger start(). Although start() has a hasStarted guard, there is a race window where both code paths fire within the same RunLoop cycle, and additionally the DataSyncService.shared.start() in ContentView line 46 runs SYNCHRONOUSLY inside .task{}, blocking the async context. The duplicate start also triggers loadFromCache() which accesses ResponseCache properties. Fix: Remove the start() call from ContentView.task{} since AdjutantApp.onChange handles it. Files: ios/Adjutant/App/ContentView.swift:46, ios/Adjutant/App/AdjutantApp.swift:28

title: Bug: Session bridge auto-session may collide with Adjutant agent tmux name
description:
File: backend/src/services/session-bridge.ts, lines 106-128 and backend/src/services/adjutant-spawner.ts

On startup, session-bridge.init() auto-creates a session for the project root with name = basename(projectRoot). If the project directory is named 'adjutant' (which it IS — this is the Adjutant project), the auto-session gets name 'adjutant', which generates tmux session 'adj-swarm-adjutant' via LifecycleManager.generateTmuxName().

Then spawnAdjutant() runs and tries to create a session also named 'adjutant', targeting 'adj-swarm-adjutant'. The spawner's idempotency check on line 43 sees the session already exists and skips the spawn — but the existing session is a GENERIC Claude Code session, not one loaded with --agent adjutant instructions.

Result: The Adjutant coordinator agent never actually gets its agent-specific instructions. It's a plain Claude Code session that happens to have the right tmux session name.

Reproduction:
1. Clone the adjutant repo to a directory named 'adjutant'
2. Start the backend
3. session-bridge.init() creates adj-swarm-adjutant as a generic session
4. spawnAdjutant() sees it exists and skips
5. The 'adjutant' agent has no coordinator instructions

Suggested fix:
Either:
1. Use a distinct session name like 'adjutant-coordinator' (not matching the project dir name)
2. Or: have spawnAdjutant() check not just whether the tmux session exists, but whether it was created with the correct agent flag
3. Or: skip the auto-session creation if the adjutant agent spawn is going to happen anyway

title: Bug: Spawner does not pass --agent adjutant flag to Claude Code
description:
File: backend/src/services/adjutant-spawner.ts, line 50-54

The spawnAdjutant() function creates a session with name='adjutant' but does not pass claudeArgs: ['--agent', 'adjutant']. Without this flag, the spawned Claude Code process will NOT load .claude/agents/adjutant.md and will behave as a generic agent with no heartbeat instructions.

The lifecycle manager supports claudeArgs (see swarm-service.ts which uses it for --prompt), but the adjutant spawner omits it entirely.

Reproduction:
1. Start the backend
2. Observe that adj-swarm-adjutant session is created
3. The Claude Code process in that session has no agent-specific instructions

Suggested fix in adjutant-spawner.ts:
```typescript
const result = await bridge.lifecycle.createSession({
  name: ADJUTANT_SESSION_NAME,
  projectPath,
  mode: 'swarm',
  claudeArgs: ['--agent', 'adjutant'],
});
```

title: Bug: FileContentView shows blank screen when fileContent is nil and no error
description: In FileContentView.swift, the body uses if/else-if chaining: (1) isLoading -> spinner, (2) fileContent != nil -> content, (3) errorMessage != nil -> error banner. But there is NO else clause. If isLoading=false, fileContent=nil, and errorMessage=nil (e.g. after a CancellationError is swallowed by BaseViewModel.performAsync), the view renders a completely blank/black screen with no indication of what happened. This is the known black screen bug. Fix: add an else clause that shows either an empty state message or triggers a retry. Note: the staff-eng is reportedly fixing this, but it is documented here for completeness.
close_reason: Fixed by adj-bfr9 — else clause added

title: Bug: Symlink path traversal bypass in files-service
description: files-service.ts uses statSync() not lstatSync(), so it follows symlinks. An attacker could create a symlink inside the project directory pointing to /etc/passwd or any file outside the project. The path traversal check (fullPath.startsWith(projectPath)) passes because the symlink's path is inside the project, but statSync/readFileSync follow the symlink to the target outside the project. Fix: use lstatSync to detect symlinks, or use realpathSync on the resolved path and re-check startsWith after resolution.
close_reason: Fixed: lstat symlink check in containsSymlink() prevents symlink traversal

title: Bug: Command injection in createFromClone via unsanitized execSync interpolation
description: In projects-service.ts line 440, createFromClone uses string interpolation to build a shell command: execSync(`git clone ${cloneUrl} ${targetDir}`). Both cloneUrl and targetDir are user-supplied values that can contain shell metacharacters (e.g., semicolons, backticks, pipes). A malicious cloneUrl like 'https://evil.com/repo; rm -rf /' or a targetDir with shell operators would execute arbitrary commands. Fix: use execFileSync('git', ['clone', cloneUrl, targetDir]) instead of execSync with string interpolation, which avoids shell interpretation entirely.
close_reason: Already fixed: createFromClone uses execFileSync('git', ['clone', ...]) instead of execSync with string interpolation

title: Bug: ResponseCache.updateChatMessages(forAgent:) overwrites global chatMessages with agent-specific data
description:
ResponseCache has two updateChatMessages methods:

1. updateChatMessages(_ messages: [PersistentMessage]) - updates global chatMessages
2. updateChatMessages(_ messages: [PersistentMessage], forAgent agentId: String) - updates per-agent AND global

The second method (line 93-98):
  func updateChatMessages(_ messages: [PersistentMessage], forAgent agentId: String) {
      chatMessagesByAgent[agentId] = messages
      chatMessages = messages  // <-- BUG: overwrites ALL cached messages with just this agent's messages
      lastUpdated[.chat] = Date()
      persistChatMessages(messages)  // <-- persists only this agent's messages as the full cache
  }

This means:
1. If the app caches 50 messages across all agents in chatMessages
2. Then fetches 10 messages for agent 'bob' and calls updateChatMessages(messages, forAgent: 'bob')
3. chatMessages now only contains bob's 10 messages (the other 40 are lost from the global cache)
4. persistChatMessages persists only bob's 10 messages to UserDefaults

On next launch, loadPersistedChatMessages() restores only those 10 agent-specific messages as the global chat cache.

Impact: Data loss in the chat message cache. Messages from other agents disappear from the global cache when any single agent's messages are updated.

Suggested fix: Don't overwrite chatMessages in the agent-specific method. Either:
- Remove the 'chatMessages = messages' line
- Or merge messages instead of replacing
- Persist per-agent caches separately
close_reason: Fixed: forAgent variant no longer overwrites global chatMessages. Per-agent caches now persisted separately.

title: Bug: ContentView.init accesses AppState.shared synchronously, triggering full init cascade
description:
ContentView.init() contains:
  _showOnboarding = State(initialValue: !AppState.shared.isOnboardingComplete)

This is the FIRST access to AppState.shared in the app lifecycle (triggered when AdjutantApp.body creates ContentView()). This single property access triggers the ENTIRE singleton initialization cascade:

1. AppState.shared -> AppState.init()
2. -> loadPersistedBaseURL() (UserDefaults read)
3. -> loadPersistedAPIKey() (UserDefaults read)
4. -> APIClient creation
5. -> loadPersistedState() (4 more UserDefaults reads + 6 Combine subscriptions)
6. -> setupNetworkRecoveryObserver() -> NetworkMonitor.shared -> NWPathMonitor init
7. -> registerDependencies() -> DependencyContainer.shared init

All of this happens during a View.init(), which is called during SwiftUI's body evaluation. SwiftUI expects View.init() to be fast and cheap.

The isOnboardingComplete computed property itself is lightweight (just checks the API URL host), but the side effect of triggering AppState.shared init is the real cost.

Additionally, ContentView uses @ObservedObject (not @StateObject) for appState:
  @ObservedObject private var appState = AppState.shared

This means ContentView subscribes to ALL AppState @Published property changes. Any change to any of AppState's 11 @Published properties triggers a ContentView re-render, even if ContentView only uses currentTheme.

Suggested fix:
- Move the showOnboarding check out of init - use .onAppear or .task instead
- Use @StateObject with a lightweight ViewModel that only exposes needed state
- Or split AppState into BootstrapState (theme + URL, loaded sync) and DeferredState (rest, loaded async)
close_reason: Fixed by adj-049.2.1: AppState split into bootstrap + deferred, ContentView uses .task{}

title: Bug: Race condition - push notification handlers access singletons before init completes
description:
AppDelegate.didFinishLaunchingWithOptions calls registerForPushNotifications() which uses UNUserNotificationCenter.requestAuthorization with a callback. If the user has already granted permissions, this callback fires almost immediately and calls application.registerForRemoteNotifications().

The didRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithDeviceToken delegate method then fires and spawns a Task calling registerDeviceTokenWithBackend(), which calls makeAPIClient() to create a fresh APIClient from UserDefaults.

Race condition: If a remote notification arrives very quickly after launch (e.g., a pending silent notification), didReceiveRemoteNotification fires and accesses:
- OverseerMailAnnouncer.shared (singleton init)
- BeadStatusMonitor.shared (singleton init, which inits DataSyncService.shared)
- AppState.shared.isVoiceMuted
- AppState.shared.apiClient

If AppState.shared hasn't completed its init yet (still in the singleton cascade from ContentView), these accesses could either:
1. Trigger a deadlock if the singleton init is re-entrant
2. Access partially initialized state

Mitigation: Since everything is @MainActor and Swift actors are re-entrant, this won't deadlock. But the timing means we could access AppState before loadPersistedState() completes, getting default values instead of persisted ones (e.g., wrong API URL).

Suggested fix: Guard notification handlers with a 'servicesReady' flag or defer notification processing until after all singletons are initialized.
close_reason: Fixed: Added services-ready gate using CheckedContinuation in AppState. Push notification handlers in OverseerMailAnnouncer and BeadStatusMonitor now await AppState.waitForServicesReady() before reading persisted preferences, preventing the startup race where isVoiceMuted defaults would be read instead of persisted values.

title: Bug: Singleton initialization cascade blocks main thread on cold launch
description:
AppState.shared is a lazy static singleton marked @MainActor. When ContentView.init() accesses AppState.shared, it triggers the entire init chain synchronously on the main thread:

1. AppState.init() calls loadPersistedState() which makes 6+ synchronous UserDefaults reads
2. loadPersistedState() also sets up 6 Combine 059CDEJNQRTWXghlsubscriptions with .sink
3. AppState.init() calls setupNetworkRecoveryObserver() which accesses NetworkMonitor.shared (another singleton init)
4. NetworkMonitor.init() creates NWPathMonitor and calls startMonitoring() - starts monitoring on a background queue
5. AppState.init() calls registerDependencies() which accesses DependencyContainer.shared (another singleton init)

The net effect: accessing AppState.shared triggers 3 cascading singleton initializations all on the main thread before the first frame renders. Each init involves UserDefaults I/O and Combine subscription setup.

Impact: Estimated 200-400ms of main thread blocking before any UI renders on cold launch.

Suggested fix: Break the cascade. AppState.init() should only do the bare minimum (load theme + API URL) and defer NetworkMonitor access, Combine subscriptions, and dependency registration to a post-render .start() method.
close_reason: Fixed by Phase 1+2: singleton cascade broken up via deferred init and background loading

title: iOS Startup Performance Refactor
description: Refactor iOS app startup to eliminate 1-5s UI freeze caused by synchronous main-thread data loading. 11 blocking sources identified totaling 500-975ms+. Three-phase approach: quick wins, architecture, polish.

title: iOS app startup freezes UI 1-5s due to main-thread data loading
description: On app launch, the iOS app does heavy data loading and processing on the main thread, causing the UI to freeze for 1-5 seconds. Investigate the root cause, identify what work is happening on the main thread, and propose/implement a refactor to move data loading to background threads for a responsive startup experience.
design:
## Recommended Refactor Plan

### Phase 1: Quick Wins (est. 60% improvement)
1. **Move ResponseCache JSON decode to background** — async decode of cached messages, show skeleton until ready
2. **Defer DataSyncService init** — don't initialize until after first frame renders; use .task {} in MainTabView
3. **Batch UserDefaults reads** — read all AppState keys in one background dispatch, publish via @Published when ready

### Phase 2: Architecture (est. 30% more improvement)
4. **Split AppState into lightweight + deferred** — create AppStateBootstrap with only URL/key (needed for first render), defer theme/voice/comms to background
5. **Lazy singleton initialization** — NotificationService, BackgroundTaskService, LiveActivityService should not init until actually needed (not at launch)
6. **Move Combine subscription setup off init** — use a separate .start() method called after first render

### Phase 3: Polish
7. **Add a responsive splash/skeleton** — show the tab bar and skeleton immediately, populate data as it arrives
8. **Profile with Instruments** — validate improvements with Time Profiler

### Key Patterns to Apply
- Replace synchronous UserDefaults in init() with: Task.detached { let val = UserDefaults...; await MainActor.run { self.prop = val } }
- Use actor isolation for ResponseCache decode
- Use SwiftUI .task {} (runs after render) instead of .onAppear for data loading
- Consider a LaunchCoordinator actor that orchestrates all startup work off main thread
notes:
## Investigation Findings (11 Main-Thread Blocking Sources)

### Critical Path
AppDelegate.didFinishLaunchingWithOptions → ContentView init → AppState.shared init (6+ UserDefaults reads) → DataSyncService.shared init → ResponseCache (JSON decode 50 messages) → SwarmOverviewViewModel.init → .onAppear refresh

### Top Blockers (estimated 500-975ms+ combined)
1. **AppState.init** (150-300ms): 6+ synchronous UserDefaults reads, API client creation, Combine subscriptions
2. **ResponseCache.loadPersistedChatMessages** (100-200ms): Synchronous JSON decode of up to 50 cached messages via UserDefaults
3. **DataSyncService.init** (75-150ms): loadFromCache + Combine subscription chains
4. **NotificationService.init** (50-100ms): Registers 6 notification categories synchronously
5. **BackgroundTaskService.init** (50-100ms): UserDefaults + BGTaskScheduler registration
6. **AppCoordinator.init** (50-75ms): 3 NotificationCenter Combine subscriptions
7. **LiveActivityService.init** (25-50ms): ActivityAuthorizationInfo.areActivitiesEnabled system call

### Root Causes
- No lazy initialization boundaries — everything eagerly initializes in a chain
- Deep singleton chain: AppState → APIClient → DataSyncService → EventStreamService → ResponseCache
- All UserDefaults reads are synchronous on main thread
- JSON decoding of cached messages blocks main thread
- Combine subscription setup during init adds latency
- System calls (ActivityAuthorizationInfo, UIAppearance, BGTaskScheduler) all block
close_reason: Investigation complete. Promoting to implementation epic.

title: QA: Spec gap - No persona-to-agent association persistence
description:
The spec describes spawning an agent with a personaId, but there's no mechanism to persist which persona an agent is using. The spawn route accepts personaId, injects the prompt, and forgets. This means:

1. The SessionStart hook has no way to know which persona the agent was spawned with (ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID maps to callsign, not persona).
2. If the persona is updated while an agent is running, the hook can't re-fetch the right persona.
3. The acceptance scenario 'Given persona Sentinel updated while agent zeratul runs, when next compaction occurs, then the latest persona prompt is fetched and injected' requires knowing zeratul's persona is Sentinel.

Fix: Either (a) add a persona_id field to the session registry so the hook can look it up, or (b) set an ADJUTANT_PERSONA_ID env var in the tmux session at spawn time (like ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID is already set), so the hook script can read it. Option (b) is simpler and consistent with existing patterns.
close_reason: Addressed: Phase 4 implements ADJUTANT_PERSONA_ID env var for persona-to-agent association

title: QA: Spec gap - Hook injection strategy is architecturally wrong
description:
The plan says persona prompts are injected via SessionStart and PreCompact hooks that 'call the API to fetch the persona prompt'. This has multiple problems:

1. SessionStart hooks output text to stdout which becomes 'additionalContext' for Claude - it does NOT become a system prompt. It's just user-visible context that Claude can choose to ignore. This is fundamentally different from a system prompt injection.

2. The plan says hooks live in 'backend/src/services/persona-hooks.ts' but hooks are shell scripts configured in .claude/settings.json, not TypeScript services. The hook must be a bash script that curls the API.

3. PreCompact hooks receive trigger and custom_instructions but have NO decision control and NO additionalContext output. The stdout of a PreCompact hook is NOT injected into the conversation. PreCompact is fire-and-forget for side effects only. After compaction, a SessionStart hook fires with matcher 'compact', which IS the right place to re-inject context.

4. The per-session hook registration is unclear. Hooks are configured in .claude/settings.json (project-level or user-level). You can't dynamically register hooks per-session via the API. The hook script needs to be a universal script that resolves the current agent's persona by reading ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID env var, then fetching the persona from the API.

Recommended fix: Use a single SessionStart hook (configured in .claude/settings.json) that runs on both 'startup' and 'compact' matchers. The hook reads ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID, curls GET /api/agents/:id/persona (new endpoint needed), and outputs the persona prompt to stdout. Drop the PreCompact hook entirely. The initial spawn-time injection via claudeArgs --prompt is still needed as the primary injection point.
close_reason: Addressed: Phase 4 uses SessionStart hook with compact matcher instead of PreCompact

title: Automated TestFlight Release Pipeline via Git Workflows
description:
Set up end-to-end CI/CD git workflows to automatically build, sign, and release new TestFlight builds for the l2rr2l iOS app (Bundle ID: com.jmm.l2rr2l).

Key areas:

1. **CI/CD Pipeline Setup** — Configure GitHub Actions (or equivalent) workflows triggered on pushes/merges to the appropriate branch(es) to build the iOS app.

2. **Code Signing & Provisioning** — Set up Apple certificates, provisioning profiles, and securely store credentials in CI secrets. Configure automatic signing for TestFlight distribution.

3. **Build & Archive** — Automate xcodebuild archive and export steps targeting the com.jmm.l2rr2l bundle identifier.

4. **TestFlight Upload** — Integrate App Store Connect API or altool/xcrun to automatically upload builds to TestFlight after successful archive.

5. **Versioning & Build Numbers** — Implement automatic build number incrementing to avoid collisions on TestFlight uploads.

6. **Notifications & Status** — Add build status badges, Slack/webhook notifications for successful/failed builds.

Deliverable: A working GitHub Actions workflow that produces TestFlight builds automatically on merge to the release branch.

title: iOS App Review: Usability, Completeness & UI/UX Audit
description:
Conduct a detailed review of the l2rr2l iOS app covering three key areas:

1. **Usability** — Evaluate navigation flows, touch targets, responsiveness, accessibility, error handling, loading states, and overall user experience on iOS devices.

2. **Completeness vs Web Frontend** — Systematically compare every feature and screen in the web frontend against the iOS app. Identify missing features, incomplete implementations, and behavioral differences. Document parity gaps with priority rankings.

3. **UI/UX Quality** — Assess visual design consistency, adherence to iOS Human Interface Guidelines, typography, spacing, color usage, animations/transitions, dark mode support, and overall polish. Identify areas that feel unfinished or non-native.

Deliverable: A comprehensive audit document with findings, screenshots/examples, and prioritized recommendations for each area.

- frontend/tests/unit/useKanban-assign.test.ts: Line 31 (rig: null)

1. InfrastructureStatus uses mayor/deacon/daemon field names — these Gas Town roles won't exist anymore
2. RigStatus, MergeQueueSummary, TownInfo types are Gas Town-specific
3. GastownStatus.init(from:) tries Gas Town shape first, then falls back — this order should flip or GT path should be removed
4. The struct name 'GastownStatus' itself needs renaming
5. PowerCapabilities may be simplified (canControl always false in swarm)
6. iOS DeploymentMode enum still has .gastown case
Risk: Removing backend Gas Town responses without updating iOS models will cause JSON decoding failures.

title: QA: DeploymentMode type must be collapsed — 'gastown' literal used in 40+ files
description: RISK: The DeploymentMode type ('gastown' | 'swarm') is defined in backend/src/services/workspace/workspace-provider.ts and used across 44 backend files and 20 frontend files. After removal, DeploymentMode should either be removed entirely (since only swarm exists) or changed to just 'swarm'. All conditional checks (mode === 'gastown', isGasTown, isSwarm) in frontend components (BeadsView, CrewStats, EpicsView, CommandChat, SettingsView) and backend services need to be collapsed. The ModeContext.tsx and useDeploymentMode.ts hooks should be simplified or removed. The iOS DeploymentMode enum in Enums.swift also needs updating.

title: Remnant: Backend gastown service files (8 files)
description: Remove entire gastown-specific service files: gastown-workspace.ts, gastown-utils.ts, gt-executor.ts, gt-control.ts, power-service.ts, convoys-service.ts, mail-data.ts, mail-service.ts. Also remove all imports from index.ts, dashboard-service.ts, agent-data.ts.

title: Risk: CrewMember.type defaults to 'crew' — invalid after AgentType cleanup
description: In agents-service.ts line 267, getTmuxAgents() creates CrewMembers with type: 'crew' as AgentType. After Gastown removal, 'crew' won't be a valid AgentType. Must change to 'agent'. Similarly, iOS AgentType decoder in Enums.swift falls back to .crew for unknown types (line 103) — must change to .agent. Frontend AgentType union also includes 'crew'. This is a silent semantic bug if not caught during type cleanup.

title: QA: agent-data.ts imports gastown-workspace.ts and gastown-utils.ts — must be replaced
description: RISK: backend/src/services/agent-data.ts imports extractBeadPrefix from gastown-workspace.ts and addressToIdentity/parseAgentBeadId/parseAgentFields from gastown-utils.ts. These functions are used in swarm mode too (collectAgentSnapshot). When gastown-workspace.ts and gastown-utils.ts are deleted, agent-data.ts will break unless these utility functions are moved to a gastown-independent location (e.g., a new utils module or into workspace/index.ts). This is a HARD build error, not a runtime risk.

title: Arch: iOS — remove Mode.swift, Convoy.swift, and polecat-related types
description: After Gastown removal: (1) Delete Mode.swift entirely (ModeInfo, SwitchModeRequest, ModeChangedEvent), (2) Delete Convoy.swift entirely (Convoy, ConvoyProgress, TrackedIssue), (3) From Agent.swift: delete SpawnPolecatRequest, update TerminalCapture references, (4) From APIClient+Endpoints.swift: delete Power, Mail, Mode, and Convoy endpoint extensions.

title: Arch: iOS — rename GastownStatus to SystemStatus and simplify
description: iOS GastownStatus.swift has complex dual-decoding logic to handle both Gas Town shape (town, infrastructure with mayor/deacon/daemon) and generalized SystemStatus shape. After removal: (1) Rename to SystemStatus, (2) Remove dual-decoding — only decode the swarm shape, (3) Remove InfrastructureStatus, RigStatus, MergeQueueSummary, TownInfo, PowerStateChange, (4) Remove power capabilities. This is the core of adj-027.3.1.

title: Arch: Remove mail transport layer entirely
description: The transport/ directory (mail-transport.ts interface, gastown-transport.ts, beads-transport.ts, index.ts, notification-providers.ts) exists for gt-backed mail. With Gastown gone, messaging is ONLY through MCP/SQLite message-store. The entire transport/ directory, mail-service.ts, mail-data.ts, and mail route should be deleted. No replacement needed — MCP messaging already handles everything.

title: Arch: Simplify AgentType to just user|agent across all platforms
description: AgentType union currently includes mayor, deacon, witness, refinery, crew, polecat, user, agent. After Gastown removal, only user and agent remain. Backend types/index.ts AgentTypeSchema, frontend types/index.ts AgentType, iOS Enums.swift AgentType enum all need cleanup. CRITICAL: agents-service.ts uses 'crew' as AgentType as default (line 267) — must change to 'agent'. iOS AgentType decoder falls back to .crew — must change to .agent.

title: Arch: Remove DeploymentMode type entirely from all platforms
description: The DeploymentMode type (gastown|swarm) exists in backend types/index.ts, frontend types/index.ts, and iOS Enums.swift. After removal, there is no mode — Adjutant is just Adjutant. Remove the type, all mode detection logic, ADJUTANT_MODE env var, and any code that branches on mode. This is CORE to adj-027, not a follow-up.

title: Improve broadcast message for trigger update button
description: Change the broadcast message sent by POST /api/messages/broadcast from 'Send me an update' to a detailed instruction that tells agents to use the MCP set_status tool to report what they just completed or are currently working on. Message should explicitly instruct agents to never say 'idle awaiting next task' — status should always reference their most recent or current work.

title: Include task description in status_change event action
description: Status change timeline events only show 'Status: working/done/etc' in the action field. The task description is buried in the detail JSON. Fix: include task in action string (e.g. 'Status: working — Implementing feature X'). Ensure frontend TimelineEventCard and iOS TimelineRowView display it properly.
close_reason: Status/progress timeline events now include task description in action field

title: iOS: Add Trigger Update button to Overview page
description: Add a button to the iOS SwarmOverview page, top-right next to the Start Agent button. When tapped, it sends a ping/broadcast to all connected agents requesting them to provide a fresh status update with a descriptive title of what they just finished or are currently working on. Requires: 1) UI button in overview toolbar, 2) Backend endpoint or MCP broadcast to request status updates from agents, 3) Agents respond with updated status.

title: Bug: useTimeline subscribes via SSE instead of WebSocket — no real-time updates
description: useTimeline.ts uses EventSource('/api/events') listening for 'timeline_event' SSE events. But the SSE endpoint only forwards EventBus events via EVENT_TYPE_MAP, which does NOT include timeline_event. Timeline events are broadcast exclusively via WebSocket wsBroadcast(). Therefore real-time updates in the web timeline are broken — no events will appear without a manual refresh. Fix: subscribe to WebSocket timeline_event messages via CommunicationContext instead of SSE. This violates FR-004 and SC-003.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 206e7f6: Replaced broken SSE EventSource with WebSocket via CommunicationContext.subscribeTimeline. Real-time timeline events now flow through existing WS connection. Build clean, all tests pass.

title: Bug: mcp-beads.test.ts crashes — extra.sessionId undefined
description: The agentId fix (9f0ebdc) added 'const resolvedAgent = extra.sessionId ? ...' to update_bead and close_bead handlers. But the test invokes these handlers without passing the extra parameter, causing 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading sessionId)'. The code should guard against undefined extra: 'extra?.sessionId' instead of 'extra.sessionId'. Affects 14 tests in mcp-beads.test.ts.

title: Bug: database.test.ts migration count hardcoded to 5
description: database.test.ts:120 expects exactly 5 migrations but 006-events.sql added a 6th. Test fails: expected length 5, got 6. Fix: update to expect 6 and add assertion for 006-events.sql.
close_reason: Already fixed during adj-028.1.7 — migration count is 6

title: Bug: wsBroadcast sends raw task/beadId instead of resolved values
description: In backend/src/services/mcp-tools/status.ts, the set_status handler correctly preserves previous task/beadId via nullish coalescing (resolvedTask = task ?? previous?.task), but the wsBroadcast on line 155 sends the original raw 'task' and 'beadId' params instead of 'resolvedTask' and 'resolvedBeadId'. This causes the frontend useAgentStatus hook to receive undefined task/beadId when an agent calls set_status without those fields (e.g. set_status({ status: 'idle' })), resulting in a brief flash of missing task text in the real-time chat status indicator until the next REST poll refreshes from the correctly-stored backend state. Fix: change line 155 from 'metadata: { type: "agent_status", status, task, beadId, projectId }' to 'metadata: { type: "agent_status", status, task: resolvedTask, beadId: resolvedBeadId, projectId }'.

title: Protocol: Update agent spawn instructions to mandate set_status calls
description: Update the agent protocol in PRIME.md and .claude/rules/ to mandate that all agents call set_status MCP tool: 1) When starting work on any task/bead 2) When completing or stopping work. The status must always include a concise task description. Update the Team Agent Beads Protocol section in PRIME.md to include set_status in the mandatory block.

title: iOS: Enhance agent status display in AgentRowView and RecipientSelectorSheet
description: Make the agent task/status text always visible and prominent on: 1) The Agents list page (AgentRowView) - status text should be always shown, not hidden behind bead info. 2) The Chat recipient selector dropdown (RecipientSelectorSheet in ChatView.swift) - status text should always be visible for every agent. For both views, ensure the task text is displayed for all statuses including idle/done.

title: Backend: Persist agent task text through status transitions
description: When an agent calls set_status(done) or set_status(idle) without a task field, the backend currently loses the previous task description. Fix enrichWithMcpStatus in agents-service.ts to preserve the last known task text so it's available in the API response for idle/done agents. Also ensure the /api/agents endpoint includes task text for all statuses.

title: Agent Status Always Visible

Backend must persist task text through status transitions so it's visible even when agents go idle/done.

title: Document scheme: insufficient contrast between dim text (#888888) and white background
description: The Document scheme uses #888888 (rgb 0.533,0.533,0.533) for dim/secondary text on a #FFFFFF white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.54:1, which fails WCAG AA for normal text (requires 4.5:1) and passes only for large text (3:1). This dim color is used heavily throughout the app for: captions, secondary labels, header badges, help text in Settings, dim status indicators, and inactive tab bar labels. Users with any degree of visual impairment will struggle to read this text. The Pip-Boy and StarCraft schemes do not have this issue because their dim colors sit on very dark backgrounds. FIX: Darken the Document dim color from #888888 to #666666 (contrast ratio ~5.74:1, passing WCAG AA). Alternatively, use the system secondary label color (.secondary) which is already optimized for the platform.
close_reason: Fixed: bumped Document dim from #888888 to #666666 for WCAG AA compliance

title: CRTCard uses hardcoded CRTTheme.Background.panel — breaks Document and StarCraft schemes
description: CRTCard.body uses .fill(CRTTheme.Background.panel.opacity(style.backgroundOpacity)) for its background. This is a static Pip-Boy green-tinted dark color (#040A04) that does not change with the theme. In Document mode (white background), cards will appear as very dark green rectangles against white — jarring and broken. In StarCraft mode, cards will show green-tinted backgrounds instead of purple-tinted ones. CRTCard is used throughout the entire app (Settings, Dashboard widgets, Proposals, etc.), making this one of the most visible issues. Additionally, the corner bracket decorations (CornerBracket) are inappropriate for Document mode — they should be hidden or replaced with simple borders. FIX: Replace CRTTheme.Background.panel with theme.background.panel (from the environment-injected theme). Conditionally hide corner brackets when theme.crtEffectsEnabled is false.
close_reason: Already fixed by components agent (adj-024.3) — all static backgrounds migrated, effects/typography scheme-aware, components updated

title: CRTButton primary variant uses hardcoded CRTTheme.Background.screen for text color
description: CRTButton.Variant.foregroundColor for .primary returns CRTTheme.Background.screen (hardcoded dark green #020502). This is the text color displayed on top of a filled primary-color button. In Pip-Boy mode: dark text on green button — works. In Document mode: dark green text on dark gray (#1A1A1A) button — INVISIBLE, fails WCAG contrast. In StarCraft mode: dark green text on teal button — poor contrast. Same issue affects BadgeView .count style (line 120) and UnreadBadge (line 303) which both use CRTTheme.Background.screen as foreground color. FIX: For primary buttons and count badges, the foreground color should be the theme's background color (theme.background.screen) so it contrasts against the primary fill. For Document, this means white text on dark buttons. For StarCraft, this means dark purple text on teal buttons.
close_reason: Already fixed by components agent (adj-024.3) — all static backgrounds migrated, effects/typography scheme-aware, components updated

title: CRT effects not disabled in Document mode — scanlines, glow, flicker, noise all still apply
description: The ColorTheme has a crtEffectsEnabled property that returns false for Document scheme, but nothing in the codebase reads it. CRTScreenContainer always renders ScanlineOverlay, ScanBarOverlay, NoiseOverlay, VignetteOverlay, and FlickerEffect based on its init params, without consulting the theme. The CRTGlowModifier, PhosphorGlow, TextGlow, and PulsingGlow modifiers in CRTEffects.swift all apply shadow effects unconditionally. CRTText always applies crtGlow regardless of theme. In Document mode, users would see scanlines and phosphor glow on a white background — completely inappropriate. FIX: (1) CRTScreenContainer should check theme.crtEffectsEnabled before rendering overlays. (2) CRTGlowModifier, PhosphorGlow, TextGlow, PulsingGlow should return content unmodified when crtEffectsEnabled is false. (3) CRTText should skip glow in Document mode.
close_reason: Already fixed by components agent (adj-024.3) — all static backgrounds migrated, effects/typography scheme-aware, components updated

title: CRTText always uses monospace font — ignores useMonospaceFont for Document scheme
description: CRTText.body always calls CRTTheme.Typography.font() which returns .system(size:weight:design:.monospaced). The ColorTheme has a useMonospaceFont property that returns false for Document scheme, but CRTText never reads it. In Document mode, all text renders in monospace instead of SF Pro. Similarly, crtTextStyle/crtHeaderStyle/crtLabelStyle in Typography.swift always call .font(.crt()) which is monospace. Also reduce letter-spacing in Document mode — wide tracking suits CRT but looks wrong in document layout. FIX: CRTText should check theme.useMonospaceFont and use .system(size:weight:design:.default) when false.
close_reason: Already fixed by components agent (adj-024.3) — all static backgrounds migrated, effects/typography scheme-aware, components updated

title: Dashboard batch: listBeads queries town-only database, not all rigs
description: In dashboard-service.ts, the three listBeads() calls (lines 192-204) use no 'rig' option, which defaults to the town database only (source = 'town'). The old DataSyncService fetched from all rigs. Rig-specific beads (e.g., adj-*, gb-*) will be absent from the dashboard after migration. The dashboard was explicitly documented (CLAUDE.md) to show ALL beads from the entire town. The fix is to use listAllBeads() instead of listBeads(), or pass {rig:'all'} / use all rig databases, for each status category. File: backend/src/services/dashboard-service.ts lines 192-204; backend/src/services/beads/beads-queries.ts listBeads() vs listAllBeads() distinction.
close_reason: Not a regression — old DataSyncService also used the same listBeads() which defaults to town source. Pre-existing limitation.

title: Dashboard batch: hooked beads are never returned by the backend
description: DashboardViewModel.processBeadsData() filters allBeads for status == 'hooked' (line 166 of DashboardViewModel.swift), but the backend dashboard-service.ts only fetches three status categories: 'in_progress' (listBeads({status:'in_progress'})), 'open' (listBeads({status:'open'})), and 'closed' (listBeads({status:'closed'})). The 'open' bd query returns beads with status == 'open' only; 'hooked' is a separate status value in the bd system (see beads/types.ts DEFAULT_STATUSES). Therefore allBeads will never contain any bead with status == 'hooked', so hookedBeads will always be empty after the migration. The old code fetched beads via DataSyncService which used a default status filter that included 'hooked'. File: backend/src/services/dashboard-service.ts lines 192-204; ios/Adjutant/Features/Dashboard/DashboardViewModel.swift lines 154-178.
close_reason: Added hooked beads fetch to dashboard-service.ts, merged into open category.

title: Bug: Service-level errors silently swallowed for status, crew, mail sections
description:
When backend services resolve with { success: false, error: { message: "..." } } (as opposed to rejecting), the error message is silently lost.

Path for status/crew: wrapResult() → fulfilled path → calls transform → extractServiceData() returns null → result is { data: null } with NO error string.

Path for mail: wrapMailResult() → wrapResult() → fulfilled path → calls transform → serviceResult.success is false → returns null → result is { data: null } with NO error string.

The error message from the service is discarded. Only Promise rejections produce error strings (via wrapResult's rejected path).

This violates FR-002: "partial failures return data for healthy sections and null + error for failed sections." When a service returns { success: false, error }, the dashboard returns { data: null } without the error, making debugging impossible.

Fix: extractServiceData should throw or return an error message when success is false, so wrapResult can include it. Or add a separate wrapServiceResult helper that checks the success field.

Affected sections: status (line 205-208), crew (line 214-217), mail (line 148-172).

title: Bug: Beads totalCount capped at 5 in production (limit passed to CLI)
description:
In dashboard-service.ts lines 189-193, listBeads() is called with limit: DASHBOARD_BEAD_LIMIT (5). The CLI respects this limit (beads-repository.ts:838-840), returning at most 5 items. Then toBeadCategory() (line 64) computes totalCount = result.data.length, which will never exceed 5 in production, even if there are hundreds of beads.

The unit test (line 267) mocks listBeads to return 10 items WITHOUT respecting the limit parameter, so the test asserts totalCount=10 and passes — but this doesn't reflect production behavior where totalCount would be 5.

Fix: Either remove limit from listBeads calls and only limit via .slice() in toBeadCategory, or have listBeads also return a separate totalCount field that's computed before slicing.

Spec violated: FR-004 — totalCount should reflect the actual number of beads, not the sliced subset.

title: Epic sub-beads endpoint using dependency graph
description: Add a backend endpoint to fetch all sub-beads for an epic using the dependency graph (bd dep) instead of naming conventions. Use this for epic progress calculation and epic detail pages. Currently iOS and web use hierarchical ID pattern matching (epic.1, epic.1.2) which is fragile. The dependency graph is the source of truth.
close_reason: Added GET /api/beads/:id/children and GET /api/beads/epics-with-progress endpoints. Frontend hooks now use dependency graph for epic children instead of fetching all 600+ beads.

title: Fix iOS chat: suppress 'Request was cancelled' error on load
description: When ChatView loads, rapid refresh calls cancel previous URLSession requests. APIClientError.cancelled is not caught by BaseViewModel's CancellationError handler, so it shows as a red error banner. Fix: treat APIClientError.cancelled as a non-displayable error.

title: Fix agent visibility disconnect between backend startup agents and iOS app
description: When adjutant starts via npm run dev, a swarm-adjutant-agent is created based on mode. The iOS app pulls active agents but doesn't see this system-created agent. Need to investigate the disconnect between what agents are really running and what the iOS app discovers.
close_reason: Same root cause as adj-8t41: adjutant-spawner used lifecycle.createSession() which didn't persist to disk. Fixed by using bridge.createSession() + orphan re-registration.

title: Fix Overview page request storm on load
description: Opening the adjutant app triggers a nonstop flurry of bd CLI requests to the backend. The logs show repeated bd list --all, bd show, bd list --status=X calls cycling endlessly. Appears to be a polling/re-render loop in the Overview page that individually checks every bead status.
close_reason: Fixed request storm with two changes: (1) added fetchingRef guard in usePolling to prevent overlapping fetches, (2) rewrote useDashboardEpics as lightweight one-shot fetch instead of heavy polling useEpics

title: Wire SendToAgentSheet into ProposalDetailView

title: QA: CLI build not automatic - user gets cryptic ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND
description:
When a user runs 'adjutant init' after a fresh clone without first running 'npx tsc -p tsconfig.cli.json', bin/cli.js tries to import from '../dist/cli/commands/init.js' which doesn't exist. The error handling catches ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND and prints:

  CLI not built. Run: npx tsc -p tsconfig.cli.json

Problems:
1. A fresh user shouldn't need to know about TypeScript compilation
2. The 'npm run setup' script handles this (builds first), but 'adjutant init' directly does not
3. bin/cli.js could auto-build by running the tsc command itself before importing
4. Or the 'postinstall' script could build the CLI (it currently only installs sub-deps)

The gap: postinstall.js installs backend/frontend deps but never builds the CLI. So after npm install, the dist/cli directory doesn't exist unless the user manually builds.

File: bin/cli.js:49-54, bin/postinstall.js (missing build step)
Severity: High - first-run experience is broken for the primary entry point
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

title: Session output leaking into normal chat view
description: After sending a message, terminal session output from agent tmux sessions appears in the normal chat view. Chat should only display SQLite-backed messages (chat_message type). Investigate: (1) WS handleMessage broadcasts type:'message' (not chat_message) — CommunicationContext.tsx handles both types on line 252-253. (2) Check if session_output events are somehow being broadcast via wsBroadcast. (3) iOS ChatWebSocketService handleLegacyChatMessage may be too permissive. (4) The WS message path should persist to SQLite first, then broadcast as chat_message — currently it only broadcasts without persistence.
close_reason: Fixed session output leak: WS messages now route through SQLite message store and broadcast as chat_message. Frontend/iOS only handle chat_message type.

title: iOS: Chat history lost after background/shutdown
description: When the iOS app is backgrounded or killed, chat messages disappear. Root causes: (1) ResponseCache.shared.chatMessages is in-memory only — lost on termination. (2) onAppear() doesn't call refresh() directly — it waits for WebSocket connect callback (line 248). (3) No background refresh when scenePhase changes to .active (MainTabView.swift:89-92 only resets TabView ID). Fix: call refresh() eagerly in onAppear, and trigger refresh on scenePhase .active. Consider persisting cache to UserDefaults or disk for instant display on cold start.
close_reason: Fixed iOS chat history: eager refresh on appear, foreground refresh via didBecomeActiveNotification, UserDefaults persistence for cold starts

title: Chat reliability: history persistence & session output leak
description: Epic for two chat bugs: (1) iOS chat doesn't reload message history after background/shutdown (in-memory cache lost), (2) Session terminal output leaks into normal chat view after sending a message. Chat should only show SQLite-backed messages.
close_reason: Both child tasks merged to main: adj-nqu (iOS history persistence) and adj-hb2 (session output leak fix)

title: Bug: Web CommandChat double-sends messages via WS and HTTP
description: In CommandChat.tsx handleSend(), when WebSocket is connected, the message is sent BOTH via wsSendMessage() (line 248) and hookSendMessage() (line 262). This causes duplicate messages on the server. The code has confused comments ('Fall through to hook-based send'). Fix: either send via WS only (and add optimistic message manually), or only use hookSendMessage and skip the WS path.

title: Add project dropdown selector to scope proposals in iOS and frontend
description:
The iOS Propose page currently uses the active project to fetch proposals, but the active project gets reset on every backend restart (discoverLocalProjects deactivates all then activates CWD only). This makes it impossible to reliably view proposals for non-CWD projects like auto-tank.

Fix: Both iOS and frontend should have a project dropdown selector that lets the user pick which project's proposals to view, rather than relying on the 'active' project flag.

Scope:
1. **iOS**: Add a project picker/dropdown to the Propose page. Fetch available projects from GET /api/projects, let user select, then filter proposals by the selected project UUID.
2. **Frontend**: Add equivalent project selector to the proposals view. Same pattern — fetch projects list, dropdown to select, pass projectId to the proposals API.
3. Both should default to the most recently used project or the first active project, but allow switching.

Note: The underlying active project reset bug (discoverLocalProjects blanket deactivation) should also be fixed separately — this bead is about making the UI resilient regardless of that bug.
close_reason: Project dropdown added to both web (ProposalsView toolbar) and iOS (ProposalsView Menu picker)

title: Bug: MCP bead tools and query tools never registered at startup
description: registerBeadTools() and registerQueryTools() are defined in mcp-tools/beads.ts and mcp-tools/queries.ts respectively, and have passing unit tests, but are never imported or called in index.ts or mcp-server.ts. This means agents connecting via MCP will not have access to create_bead, update_bead, close_bead, list_beads, show_bead, list_agents, get_project_state, or search_messages tools at runtime.
close_reason: Fixed: all 4 tool groups now registered at startup

title: Bug: Bead and Query MCP tools never registered at startup
description: registerBeadTools() and registerQueryTools() are defined in mcp-tools/beads.ts and mcp-tools/queries.ts but are never called during server initialization. Only registerStatusTools() and registerMessagingTools() are wired up in initMcpServer() + index.ts. Agents connecting via MCP will not have access to create_bead, update_bead, close_bead, list_beads, show_bead, list_agents, get_project_state, or search_messages. Fix: add registerBeadTools(server) and registerQueryTools(server, store) calls in initMcpServer() or index.ts.
close_reason: Already fixed in ce60ebf (same as adj-dbu)

title: Fix: WebSocket message type contract mismatch between backend MCP tools and frontend hooks
description:
Backend MCP tools broadcast WS messages with shapes that don't match what frontend hooks expect:

1. set_status/report_progress: Backend broadcasts type='typing' with metadata={task, beadId, percentage}. Frontend useAgentStatus filters on metadata.type==='agent_status' which is NEVER sent. The hook will receive zero status updates in production.

2. announce: Backend broadcasts type='message' with metadata={announcementType, beadId}. Frontend AnnouncementBanner filters on metadata.type==='announcement' which is NEVER in the metadata. Announcements will never display.

Fix options:
A) Backend: Add metadata.type field to broadcasts (metadata: { type: 'agent_status', task, beadId } for status, metadata: { type: 'announcement', announcementType, beadId } for announce)
B) Frontend: Change filters to match actual broadcast shapes (check top-level type==='typing' + metadata.task for status, check top-level type==='message' + metadata.announcementType for announcements)
C) Both: Establish a formal WS message contract and align both sides

Tests pass because they mock the expected shape directly, masking this integration gap.
close_reason: Fixed: added metadata.type field to backend broadcasts (agent_status, announcement)

title: Fix: Skill tool-catalog.md has wrong parameter names
description: Tool catalog documents wrong param names that will cause Zod validation errors: send_message says 'recipient' but impl uses 'to', set_status says 'detail' but impl uses 'task'/'beadId', report_progress says 'taskId'/'percent' but impl uses 'task'/'percentage', announce shows only 'body' but impl requires 'title' and 'body'.
close_reason: Fixed all parameter names in tool-catalog.md and SKILL.md to match actual Zod schemas

title: Fix: skill tool-catalog.md has wrong parameter names (multiple tools)
description:
The tool-catalog.md reference docs have parameter name mismatches with the actual implementations:

1. send_message: docs say 'recipient' but impl uses 'to'
2. set_status: docs say 'detail' but impl uses 'task' and 'beadId'
3. report_progress: docs say 'taskId'/'percent' but impl uses 'task'/'percentage'
4. announce: docs say only 'body' but impl requires both 'title' and 'body'

Also SKILL.md:27 uses 'recipient' but impl is 'to'.

These mismatches will cause agents to call tools with wrong params, getting Zod validation errors. Update the docs to match actual Zod schemas in messaging.ts, status.ts, queries.ts, beads.ts.
close_reason: Fixed vi.mock arrow function constructors for Vitest 4

title: Fix: beads test MockMcpServer constructor crash (25/25 failing)
description: mcp-beads.test.ts uses vi.fn().mockImplementation() in vi.hoisted() to create MockMcpServer. vi.fn() returns a non-constructable proxy so 'new MockMcpServer()' throws TypeError. Same root cause as adj-010.2.7.1 (mcp-server.test.ts). Fix: use a real class or regular function keyword instead of vi.fn().mockImplementation().
close_reason: Fixed: vi.mock arrow functions replaced with function expressions

title: Fix: Wire transport onclose to disconnectAgent
description: No onclose handler on SSE transport. When agent crashes or network drops, connection lingers in Map forever (memory leak). Wire transport.onclose to call disconnectAgent(sessionId).

title: Fix: Cursor pagination tiebreaker for same-second messages
description: Pagination uses created_at < ? but SQLite datetime has second precision. Messages in same second share timestamps making cursor unreliable. Use composite cursor (created_at, id) or rowid tiebreaker.

title: Fix: Parameterize LIMIT in getMessages SQL
description: getMessages builds LIMIT via template literal string interpolation. Should use parameterized query to prevent SQL injection even though type is number.
