title: iOS WebSocket still failing -- ngrok may add compression independently
description: adj-9kq added perMessageDeflate:false to ws-server.ts but iOS still gets 'input control frame can not be compressed' through ngrok. The fix may not be deployed (backend not restarted), OR ngrok's WebSocket proxy re-negotiates compression independently of the origin server. Need to: 1) Verify backend was restarted with the fix 2) If still failing, investigate ngrok WebSocket compression behavior 3) May need iOS-side fix to reject compression negotiation, or configure ngrok to disable WS compression

title: iOS WebSocket session connection failures through ngrok
description: iOS app fails to connect WebSocket sessions with 'Socket is not connected' (POSIX error 57) and 'Protocol error'. Connections go through ngrok tunnel (3.22.30.40:443). Backend only sees clean disconnects. Key errors: 'input control frame can not be compressed' and 'nw_ws_validate_server_response' suggest WebSocket protocol/compression negotiation failure through the ngrok proxy. Need to investigate ChatWebSocketService.swift for compression settings, connection retry logic, and ngrok WebSocket compatibility.

title: bd CLI SIGSEGV crash on concurrent list operations
description: After running for a few minutes, bd list commands crash with nil pointer dereference in dolt's DoltDB.SetCrashOnFatalError. Exit code 2, SIGSEGV. Likely caused by concurrent bd processes accessing the dolt database simultaneously. The backend spawns multiple bd commands (list epics, list tasks, etc.) in parallel, and dolt's SQL engine doesn't handle concurrent connections to the same database safely. Need to investigate and add serialization or connection pooling on the adjutant side.

description: Reported during bloomfolio-goals scaffold 2026-06-06. beads pre-commit hook ('bd hooks run pre-commit', tagged BEADS INTEGRATION v0.60.0 — NOT regenerated on 1.0.4 upgrade) auto-exports .beads/issues.jsonl from Dolt + stages it into UNRELATED code commits (repro: staged 4 src files; commit 667dd2d swept a 1731-issue jsonl). Impact: guaranteed jsonl merge conflicts on parallel commits; careless resolution (smaller snapshot) silently rolls back beads. FIX options: (1) add .gitattributes '.beads/issues.jsonl merge=union' to auto-resolve by union (no clobber); (2) make hook export opt-in / stop auto-staging jsonl into code commits; (3) interim guard: 'git reset .beads/issues.jsonl' before code commits. With Dolt as source of truth (after adj-182.3.8), jsonl conflicts become regenerable.

It's a shared-server/remote-configured project (likely shares bloomfolio's DB). Left CLEAN: orphan servers killed, .local_version honest at 0.60.0, DB intact + backed up (.beads/dolt.pre-bd104.*). Its OWNER should migrate it via 'bd bootstrap' / its shared-server config on bd 1.0.4. Keep bloomfolio-backlog's swarm PAUSED until done (bd is now 1.0.4 machine-wide; its agents will hit the migration/bootstrap need on first use).

Added ios/AdjutantKit/Tests/AdjutantKitTests/QuestionsContractTests.swift: drives the public listQuestions/answerQuestion/dismissQuestion through the real decode path with the EXACT backend shapes ({questions,total} and {question}), and asserts the OLD wrong shapes (bare array, {success}) THROW — so the bug class can't silently return. Backend side already pinned by questions-routes.test.ts (asserts data.questions/total, data.question). Audit of all other iOS endpoints found no further mismatches. Verified swift build + 25 AdjutantKit tests green.

title: Bug: doctor --fix / init risk double-open on first install — refuse-to-kill + no port-owner guard lets a rogue + supervised server share one data-dir
description: On a FIRST install (launchd agent not yet loaded), fixDolt (cli/commands/doctor.ts) reads launchctlSupervisedPid()=null, so by design it REFUSES to kill any dolt on the data-dir (correct: do not kill the wrong process). It then proceeds to install+bootstrap the supervised server on the SAME data-dir while the pre-existing rogue self-managed dolt is still running — the exact two-servers-on-one-data-dir double-open corruption the step-2 comment (line 561) warns against. Compounding it: if the rogue already holds the pinned port, installSupervisor's SQL probe (a bare TCP connect, realSqlProbe) will CONNECT to the ROGUE on that port and report verified:true — a FALSE-POSITIVE verification. initDoltSupervisor (adj-182.2.3) shares the same first-install exposure (it has no rogue-kill at all). GAP: there is no guard that (a) detects a non-supervised owner of the pinned port before declaring success, nor (b) escalates loudly/aborts when a rogue sits on the data-dir but cannot be classified. Tests cover refuse-to-kill (cli-doctor-fix.test.ts:145) and kill-before-install ordering (:203) but NOT the first-install-with-rogue path. FIX OPTIONS: probe port-owner identity (pid via lsof) and abort/escalate when the verified server is not the one we just bootstrapped; or kill rogues that hold the PINNED PORT specifically (port ownership is unambiguous) even when launchd PID is unknown. Add regression tests for both. Suites: backend/tests/unit/cli-doctor-fix.test.ts, cli-init-dolt.test.ts.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main (9c33c477). Tests GREEN, CLI+backend typecheck clean.

title: Review: self-managed project yields 3 simultaneous doctor FAILs for one root cause (noisy + exit 1 pre-migration)
description: Code review of adj-182.2.1 checkDolt(). On a not-yet-migrated (self-managed) project — every project before `adjutant doctor --fix`/`init` runs — pinnedPort/metadataPort are null, so check #1 (port pinned) FAILs with "self-managed mode — run adjutant doctor --fix", AND check #3 (server reachable) FAILs "no pinned port to probe", AND check #4 (port file matches) FAILs "no pinned port". That is three fails for a single root cause, and `runDoctor()` returns exit 1 (hasFail) on every healthy pre-migration project. Risk: trains operators to ignore doctor output, and breaks any CI/script gating on `doctor` exit code during the rollout window. Recommend: when pinnedPort+metadataPort are both null (clean self-managed state), emit ONE actionable result (warn or info, not fail) — "self-managed Dolt; run adjutant doctor --fix to adopt supervised mode" — and SKIP the dependent reachable/port-file checks rather than failing each. Keep hard FAILs for genuine inconsistency (metadataPort != pinnedPort, stale port file, rogue). Coordinate the exit-code contract with adj-182.2.2 (--fix) and 2.3 (init).
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main (9c33c477). Tests GREEN, CLI+backend typecheck clean.

title: Review: test the runReal* checkDolt seams (lsof/ps/launchctl parsing) — Rule 1 real-output risk
description: Code review of adj-182.2.1/2.7 (cli/commands/doctor.ts). The pure checkDolt() orchestration has excellent unit coverage (19 tests). But the PRODUCTION seam functions are untested: realScanDoltProcesses (regex parsing of `ps -axo pid,command` + `lsof -anP -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN` + `lsof -p` cwd line), realLaunchctlSupervisedPid (`launchctl print` `pid = N` scrape), realSqlProbe, and runRealCheckDolt (metadata.json -> options assembly). Per testing Rule 1, parsing real CLI output is exactly where assumptions diverge from reality and ship bugs. Add unit tests that feed captured real `ps`/`lsof`/`launchctl print` output fixtures into the parsers (inject execFile via a seam or refactor the parse step out as a pure fn). Acceptance: each parser has happy-path + malformed-output + empty-output tests using real captured shapes.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main (9c33c477). Tests GREEN, CLI+backend typecheck clean.

title: Harden SUPERVISED_PID via launchd probe in checkDolt (from QA adj-182.1.5.2)
description: QA gap from Phase 1: bd-doctor/checkDolt derives SUPERVISED_PID from a possibly-stale .beads/dolt-server.pid; a rogue reusing that PID is trusted. Probe the supervised PID from launchd (launchctl print) in checkDolt() rather than trusting the pid file. TDD.

title: Bug: dolt-pin re-pin strips trailing comment on the active listener port line
description: cli/lib/dolt-pin.ts applyListenerPort() rewrites the matched port line as `${indent}port: ${port}` — any trailing inline comment or content is DROPPED. Repro: config.yaml line 'port: 17005  # pinned by adjutant' re-pinned to 17009 becomes 'port: 17009' (comment gone). The first pin of a clean beads template is fine (port: 49599 has no trailing comment), so this bites the live-cutover RE-PIN path (US1) and any operator-annotated config. The module's own idempotency/comment-preservation guarantee is violated for annotated lines. Fix: preserve any text after the port value (capture and re-append the trailing comment). Add a regression test for a port line with a trailing comment.
close_reason: Fixed: applyListenerPort preserves trailing inline comment on re-pin. Regression tests RED->GREEN. Merged f26444a7.

title: Review: dolt-pin pinMetadata must reject non-object metadata.json (silent port drop / unhandled TypeError)
description: cli/lib/dolt-pin.ts pinMetadata() (lines 47-59) JSON.parses metadata.json but never asserts the result is a plain object. Edge cases verified empirically: (a) metadata.json containing a JSON ARRAY ([]) -> port assignment to array['dolt_server_port'] is dropped by JSON.stringify -> pinDoltPort SILENTLY returns the env line as if success while metadata never gets dolt_server_port, leaving beads in self-managed mode (the exact ephemeral-port churn this epic exists to fix); (b) a JSON scalar (null/42) -> throws a raw TypeError that escapes the parse-only try/catch, instead of the clean wrapped 'Dolt pin: ...' error the rest of the module uses. Fix: after JSON.parse, assert (typeof meta==='object' && meta!==null && !Array.isArray(meta)); else throw new Error(`Dolt pin: malformed metadata.json at ${metadataPath}: expected a JSON object`). Add 2 tests (array, scalar). Low real-world likelihood (live metadata is always an object) but matches the epic focus on malformed-metadata error handling.
close_reason: Fixed: pinMetadata asserts plain object; array/scalar throw clean wrapped error (no silent port drop, no raw TypeError). Regression tests RED->GREEN. Merged f26444a7.

Burn the baseline down to 0, then drop the ratchet and make frontend typecheck hard-blocking like backend.

title: UX: blocking urgency rows have no persistent visual anchor — red glow fades on hover-out
description: Blocking questions (urgency='blocking') get a left-border red color and a CSS box-shadow glow, which is good. However the rowBlocking style uses 'transition: box-shadow 0.15s ease' inherited from the row style. More critically, there is no persistent attention-demanding treatment: no pulsing animation, no sticky position, no background differentiation beyond the box-shadow. In a long list the blocking row can visually blur into high-urgency rows (both have colored left borders). The spec says 'blocking question is always at the top' (Success Criteria) but the sort is server-side — if a General has a category filter active that shows a mix of urgencies, blocking questions need a strong persistent indicator beyond a slightly redder left border. Fix: add a subtle repeating CSS animation (e.g. a slow glow pulse using @keyframes on the box-shadow, or a background-color flicker between #111111 and rgba(255,34,34,0.07)) to rowBlocking so it persists as visually distinct. File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx rowBlocking style.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: answer/dismiss error not surfaced per-row — only shown in global error bar
description: When answer() or dismiss() throws, the hook sets the top-level error state (line 215, 226) but the QuestionRow component catches the error itself and silently swallows it (catch block comment: 'error handled in hook'). The user sees a global error banner at the top of the view (errorBar style), but has no per-row feedback: the row doesn't flash, show an inline error, or give any affordance for which action failed. On a long list this is confusing — the General does not know which row's answer failed. Additionally the submitting=false reset in 'finally' makes the row appear ready again with no indication of failure. Fix: pass an error prop back down to QuestionRow (or use a rowError local state that gets set via a thrown re-catch) so the row shows an inline 'FAILED — retry' message below the answer form. File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx QuestionRow + frontend/src/hooks/useOpenQuestions.ts answer/dismiss.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: action_required category visually indistinguishable from question categories
description: The spec (US1, US3) explicitly distinguishes action_required (a blocking task the General must DO — provide a key, grant access, approve) from answer-type categories. The current implementation renders action_required as a cyan chip 'ACTION_REQ' with identical styling to every other category chip. There is no visual differentiation that communicates 'I need you to take an action, not answer a question'. A General scanning 20 questions needs to instantly recognize which rows require action vs. which need a decision/answer — right now both look the same. Fix: give action_required a distinct visual treatment: a different color (e.g. orange/amber — already used for 'high' urgency, or a separate accent), a stronger border, or an icon prefix (e.g. '>> ACTION_REQ') that makes it scan differently from question-type categories. File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx — CATEGORY_LABELS/categoryChip styles.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: question:new WS event bypasses active filters, pollutes filtered view
description: In useOpenQuestions.ts the question:new WebSocket handler (line 177-198) appends every new question to the list unconditionally — it does not check whether the incoming question passes the active category/urgency/agentFilter. This means if the General has filtered to 'BLOCKING urgency only' and a 'LOW' question arrives, it appears in the filtered view until next re-fetch, breaking the filter guarantee. The question body comment even acknowledges this is a known gap: 'add to open list (if it passes current filters in view)' but the filter check was never implemented. Fix: inside the question:new handler, check ev.urgency/ev.category/ev.agentId against the current filter state before calling setQuestions. The filter state needs to be captured in the useEffect closure (add them to the dependency array or use a ref). File: frontend/src/hooks/useOpenQuestions.ts
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: Agent filter exposed in hook but missing from view filter bar
description: The useOpenQuestions hook exposes agentFilter/setAgentFilter and the spec (US3) requires filtering by agent. The view's filter bar only renders URGENCY and CATEGORY filter groups — the AGENT filter row is completely absent. A General triage-ing questions from 10+ agents has no way to narrow by agent from the UI, despite the backend and hook both supporting it. Fix: add an AGENT filter row to the OpenQuestionsView filter bar. Since agent names are dynamic (not an enum), the most pragmatic approach is a text input or a dynamically-built chip list derived from the unique agentId values currently in the questions list. File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: QA: conversationId never persisted back to agent_questions row after DM creation
description:
The plan specifies (plan.md step 4 in fileQuestion orchestration): "questionStore updated with conversationId (best-effort, non-fatal if it fails)". This step is NOT implemented.

**Repro:**
1. Agent calls `file_question(...)` via MCP.
2. MCP tool calls `questionService.fileQuestion(input)` — input does NOT include `conversationId` (the tool schema doesn't expose it, correctly).
3. Service calls `questionStore.fileQuestion(input)` — question row is inserted with `conversation_id = NULL`.
4. Service calls `conversationStore.getOrCreateDm(...)` to get the DM conversation id.
5. Service mirrors the question as a message into the DM (correct).
6. **Missing:** service should update `agent_questions.conversation_id = dm.id` for the just-filed question.
7. WS broadcast receives `conversationId` in the event (correct for the broadcast), but it's never written to the DB.

**Effect:** `agent_questions.conversation_id` is always `NULL` for every question filed via the MCP tool. Querying a question via `GET /api/questions` will always return `conversationId: undefined` even though the DM conversation exists. This breaks any client logic that wants to navigate from a question to its DM thread.

**Root cause:** `QuestionStore` has no `updateConversationId` (or equivalent) method. The service has the conversationId in hand after step 3 but no mechanism to write it back.

**Fix:** Add `updateConversationId(id: string, conversationId: string): void` (or equivalent) to `QuestionStore`, call it in `QuestionService.fileQuestion` after the DM is obtained (wrapped in try/catch per the "best-effort" spec).

Files: `backend/src/services/question-service.ts` lines 159-175; `backend/src/services/question-store.ts` (QuestionStore interface missing method).
close_reason: Fixed with regression test, merged to main, 3673 tests green

title: QA: answer_question MCP tool never records answeredBy — always NULL in DB
description:
The `answer_question` MCP tool in `backend/src/services/mcp-tools/questions.ts` does NOT resolve the answering agent's identity from the session and does NOT inject `answeredBy` into the service call.

**Repro:**
1. Coordinator calls `answer_question({ id: "q-xxx", answerBody: "Done." })` via MCP.
2. The tool parses params via `AnswerQuestionSchema` — `answeredBy` is optional and not exposed as a tool parameter.
3. `parsed.data` has `answeredBy: undefined`.
4. `questionService.answerQuestion(id, parsed.data)` is called with no `answeredBy`.
5. Store runs `answered_by = input.answeredBy ?? null` → `NULL`.

**Expected:** The MCP tool should resolve the calling agent's identity via `getAgentBySession(extra.sessionId)` (same as `file_question` does) and inject it as `answeredBy`. If the session is unknown, it can default to a sentinel like `"user"` (matching the REST route's default).

**Contrast:** The REST route (`backend/src/routes/questions.ts` line 89) explicitly sets `answeredBy: parsed.data.answeredBy ?? "user"`. The MCP tool has no equivalent.

**Effect:** All questions answered via MCP will have `answeredBy = null`. The `answered_by` column is practically always NULL since the coordinator is the primary user of the MCP tools.

**Missing test:** No test verifies that `answer_question` MCP tool passes a non-null `answeredBy` to the service.

Files: `backend/src/services/mcp-tools/questions.ts` lines 226-269; compare with `backend/src/routes/questions.ts` line 89.
close_reason: Fixed with regression test, merged to main, 3673 tests green

title: QA: APNS urgency gating is dead code — normal/low always push same as blocking/high
description:
In `backend/src/services/question-service.ts` lines 206-207:

```typescript
const isAlways = urgencyAlwaysPushes(question.urgency);
const pushEnabled = isAlways ? isAPNsConfigured() : isAPNsConfigured();
```

Both branches of the ternary are identical — `isAPNsConfigured()` is called regardless of `isAlways`. The `urgencyAlwaysPushes()` function and the `ALWAYS_PUSH_URGENCIES` constant are computed but their result is never used in the control flow. This is dead code.

**Effect:** normal/low urgency questions push whenever APNS is configured — the same behaviour as blocking/high. The spec states "Blocking/high always push; normal/low respect existing notification prefs" (spec.md line 79). The plan comment acknowledges the pref API doesn't exist yet and says "treat not configured as the proxy gate", but the implementation never actually differentiates. The `urgencyAlwaysPushes` abstraction is entirely useless as written.

**Expected:** Either (a) the ternary is removed and replaced with the simpler `const pushEnabled = isAPNsConfigured()` with a comment explaining why normal/low also push today, OR (b) when the pref API lands, the gating will be wired in — but the dead code in its current form silently breaks the invariant and will mislead the engineer who implements the pref gate.

**Missing test:** There is no test for "normal urgency when APNS IS configured — does it push?" The existing test only covers "normal when NOT configured = no push". If the intent is to push for all urgencies when configured, that should be explicitly tested and the dead ternary removed.

Files: `backend/src/services/question-service.ts` lines 124-128, 206-207.
close_reason: Fixed with regression test, merged to main, 3673 tests green

title: Pre-provision node_modules into worktree spawns (symlink) to avoid npm-install watchdog stall
description:
Fresh worktree engineers stall on `npm install` (root/backend/frontend node_modules are gitignored, so `git worktree add` omits them), tripping the 600s spawn watchdog. Bit 3 engineers in the adj-164 squad (karass, artanis, +).

Fix: instantly provision node_modules into a new worktree by SYMLINKING from the main repo (copying GBs is infeasible; node resolves through symlinks fine, pnpm-style).

Deliverables:
- scripts/provision-worktree.sh <worktree-path>: idempotent symlink of root/backend/frontend node_modules from repo root into the worktree (skips missing sources + existing targets). Single source of truth, usable by both spawn paths.
- swarm-service: call it after `git worktree add` (createSwarm + spawnAgentIntoSwarm) so Adjutant-spawned worktrees are provisioned automatically.
- squad-execute templates: instruct Claude Code Task-tool worktree agents to run it as their FIRST setup step instead of `npm install` (that path is harness-created; Adjutant can't hook worktree creation, so it's a spawn-prompt instruction).
- TDD: temp-dir behavior test for the script; unit test for the swarm-service call; content test for the template reference.

Non-conflicting with feature work; spawn-isolation/infra only.
close_reason:
Shipped (commit 1472bf78, merged to main, verify GREEN). Root cause: root/backend/frontend node_modules are gitignored, so `git worktree add` creates a worktree without them → fresh engineers run a slow `npm install` that trips the 600s spawn watchdog (bit karass, artanis, + in the adj-164 squad).

Fix — instant symlink provisioning (copying GBs infeasible; Node resolves through symlinks, pnpm-style):
- scripts/provision-worktree.sh <worktree> [main]: idempotent symlink of root/backend/frontend node_modules; skips missing sources + existing targets (no-clobber). Auto-detects the main repo via git-common-dir when no main arg given, so a Task-tool agent runs `bash scripts/provision-worktree.sh .`. Live-tested on a real squad worktree (created the missing root symlink, skipped present ones, main repo untouched).
- swarm-service: provisionWorktreeDeps() runs after each `git worktree add` (createSwarm + spawnAgentIntoSwarm); BEST-EFFORT — provisioning failure does not block the spawn (agent can still npm install).
- squad-member-context.md (included in every squad spawn prompt): instruct Task-tool worktree agents to run it as their FIRST setup step instead of npm install. (Task-tool worktrees are harness-created — Adjutant can't hook their creation — so it's a spawn-prompt instruction; swarm-service path is wired directly.)

Tests (TDD red→green): 9 temp-dir behavior tests incl. real-git-worktree auto-detect, no-clobber, idempotency, missing-source skip, bad-path error; 2 swarm-service wiring tests (each worktree provisioned; best-effort on failure); template-reference content guard. 51 related tests pass; full verify-before-push GREEN.

Dogfooded the adj-laz97 branch guard during push (it correctly aborted a push attempt from the feature branch). Coordinator memory on the npm-install-watchdog pattern wasn't in the queryable store — proceeded from the bead context + first-hand repo inspection.

title: QA: unread watermark format mismatch zeroes channel unread counts (FR-009)
description:
EPIC adj-164. HIGH correctness bug in per-conversation unread tracking (adj-164.4.5). Classic adj-067 real-data-shape failure: tests use a synthetic timestamp shape, masking a production format mismatch.

ROOT CAUSE: getUnreadCount() (backend/src/services/conversation-store.ts:398-419) compares messages.created_at > conversation_members.last_read_at as STRINGS. The two columns are written in DIFFERENT formats:
- messages.created_at: written via insertMessage -> datetime('now') (message-store.ts:141) => 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' (SPACE separator, second precision).
- last_read_at default: markConversationRead() (conversation-store.ts:390-396) uses new Date().toISOString() => 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.sssZ' (T separator, ms, Z).
Lexically, space (0x20) < 'T' (0x54) at index 10, so a message string is NEVER > an ISO watermark string with the same date. After ANY default markConversationRead, getUnreadCount collapses to 0 for ALL messages stored via the real insert path — including messages posted strictly AFTER the read.

REPRO (confirmed by QA probe, real insert path):
1. createChannel + joinChannel raynor
2. markConversationRead(channel, 'user')  // default watermark
3. postToChannel(raynor, 'new intel 1'); postToChannel(raynor, 'new intel 2')  // posted AFTER read
EXPECTED getUnreadCount(channel,'user') === 2
ACTUAL === 0
Diagnostic: WATERMARK='2026-05-29T21:29:51.567Z', MESSAGE TS='2026-05-29 21:29:51'.

TEST GAP: tests/unit/conversation-unread.test.ts inserts via a local postAt() helper (line 31-37) that writes raw ISO 'T...Z' created_at values AND markConversationRead is called with ISO watermarks (line 95). So tests compare ISO vs ISO and pass — they never exercise insertMessage/postToChannel (the real datetime('now') path) against a default watermark. This violates Rule 1 (test against real data shapes).

FIX: normalize formats. Either (a) store last_read_at via datetime('now')/strftime to match message format, normalizing any caller-supplied 'at' to SQLite format, OR (b) compare using SQLite datetime(created_at) > datetime(last_read_at) so both are parsed to a common form. Add a regression test that uses postToChannel + default markConversationRead.

NOTE: markConversationRead() is not yet wired into any REST/MCP surface at this merge, so the bug is currently latent — but the store primitive is wrong and the web/iOS read-tracking lanes will build on it with the same ISO-shaped (passing) tests. Fix before those lanes land.

FILES: backend/src/services/conversation-store.ts:390-419; backend/src/services/message-store.ts:141; tests/unit/conversation-unread.test.ts:31-37,95.
close_reason: Unread timestamp-format fix (real insert path); green. By karass.

title: Review: conflict-resolved channel-post path in messaging.ts has no test coverage
description:
adj-164 code review (mohandar). backend/src/services/mcp-tools/messaging.ts lines 52-108 (the conversationId/channel-post branch added during the adj-164.4 + adj-164.2 merge).

send_message now has two coexisting paths: (1) channel post when conversationId+conversationStore are present (postToChannel + wsBroadcastToConversation), and (2) the legacy DM path (dmConversationId tagging + wsBroadcast). tests/unit/mcp-messaging.test.ts (1007 lines) contains ZERO references to conversationStore / conversationId / channel — the entire merge-resolved branch is untested. Per Rule 4 (MCP tool min 2 tests) and Rule 8.

The logic reads correctly (server-side identity via getAgentBySession; membership enforced in postToChannel; fail path returns structured error), and it typechecks, but "compiles" is not "covered" — this is exactly the conflict-resolved code flagged for scrutiny.

Add tests: (a) channel post success — conversationId present, agent is a member -> postToChannel called, wsBroadcastToConversation fan-out, returns messageId+conversationId; (b) channel post by non-member -> structured {error} (postToChannel throws, caught); (c) confirm the DM path is unaffected when conversationId is omitted.
close_reason: Channel-post path coverage added to mcp-messaging tests; green. By karass.

title: Review: backfill strands threaded DM history (thread_id conv id never resolved by getDm)
description:
adj-164 code review (mohandar). backend/src/services/conversation-backfill.ts lines 94-104.

The backfill groups messages that carry a thread_id into a conversation whose id IS the thread_id ("honor existing thread grouping"). But every LIVE send path keys the conversation id on dmConversationId("user", agent) and ignores threadId entirely:
 - routes/messages.ts: dmConversationId("user", to)
 - ws-server.ts handleMessage: dmConversationId("user", recipient)
 - mcp-tools/messaging.ts DM path: dmConversationId(agentId, dmPeer)
And clients resolve the DM via GET /api/conversations/dm/:agentId -> getOrCreateDm -> dmConversationId.

Consequence: for any pre-existing user<->agent DM that had a thread_id, backfill places the HISTORY into conversation "thread-xyz", while all NEW messages and the client's open DM resolve to dmConversationId("user", agent) — a DIFFERENT conversation. The historical messages become unreachable from the agent's DM view (orphan conversation getDm never returns). User-visible loss of DM history after migration.

The existing test (tests/unit/conversation-backfill.test.ts:76 "should reuse thread_id as the conversation grouping") asserts conversation_id == thread-xyz but never verifies reachability via getDm, so it validates the implementation rather than the end-to-end intent.

Fix: backfill threaded DM messages into dmConversationId("user", counterpart) (same key the live paths use), OR map the thread_id conversation's members so getDm aliases to it. Add a regression test: after backfill, GET /conversations/dm/:agent must return the conversation containing the threaded history.
close_reason: Backfill keys threaded DMs on dmConversationId (reachable via getDm); tests green. By karass.

title: Residual backend memory leak (~25 MB/hr) + un-done OOM hardening (timeline prune interval, streaming buffer caps)
description:
Follow-up to adj-zm2fh (P0 OOM crisis — now resolved/closed). The P0 leak rate was cut ~11× (from ~280 MB/hr to ~25 MB/hr steady-state, verified from /tmp/adj-mem/poll.log over an 18h window on PID 53870, 155MB→594MB). With dev-backend.sh auto-restart + tsx watch hot-reload, the OOM runway moved from ~14-46h to ~6 days, so the operational crisis is over. This bead tracks the REMAINING work that did not ship under the P0.

## Residual leak (~25 MB/hr, still present)
The dominant amplifier fixed under adj-zm2fh was the cost-tracker `session:cost` emit cascade (commit 5a754046: suppress emit on no-op cost updates). The remaining drip is most likely:
- EventBus.emit() fires TWO inner emits per event (named + `*` wildcard) regardless of listener count.
- AdjutantCore.onAny() → for-each-behavior `behavior.act().catch(...)` allocates a Promise + .catch closure per behavior per event, even for synchronous behaviors.
Next step: heap-diff two snapshots (preserved at /tmp/adj-zm2fh-snapshots/) to confirm the dominant retainer, then either avoid per-behavior Promise allocation for sync behaviors or reduce the wildcard double-emit overhead.

## Un-done acceptance criteria carried over from adj-zm2fh
These were in the original P0 AC but were NOT the measured root cause, so they were never implemented:

### Timeline events
- [ ] Pruning runs on a recurring interval (default every 60min, configurable via env) — currently prunes only on startup
- [ ] Configurable retention window (default 7 days)
- [ ] Bounded-batch SQL DELETE so cleanup can't OOM itself
- [ ] Test: 10k events older than retention → deleted on next interval fire
- [ ] Telemetry: events_pruned_total counter, last_prune_ts, events_remaining_after_prune gauges

### Streaming bridge buffers
- [ ] Per-session buffer hard cap (default 5MB or 10k lines, configurable)
- [ ] FIFO eviction when cap hit (drop oldest, keep newest)

## Forensics preserved
- /tmp/adj-zm2fh-snapshots/ — 3 pre-OOM heap snapshots
- /tmp/adj-mem/poll.log — full RSS trend log
- /tmp/adj-histogram-3.json — analyzed retainers from snapshot #3

title: QA: CRTScreen.css .crt-noise still has 50fps animation that recomposites full overlay
description: frontend/src/components/shared/CRTScreen.css:387-403. .crt-noise has 'animation: noise-shift 0.1s steps(5) infinite' which produces a 50fps full-screen transform repaint of an inset:0 overlay at z-index:8 (covers entire CRT area). Track E (adj-139.5.1/.5.2) successfully fixed the phosphor filter and scanline animation costs, but did not touch this noise animation. The noise-shift keyframes translate the overlay by 1% in 4 step phases (transforms only, GPU-accelerated, less expensive than filter, but still constantly active over the entire viewport with z-index above content). The .crt-scan-bar at line 376 also animates continuously (6s linear) but is small (8px tall) so less costly. Spec FR-010: 'CRT visual effects MUST NOT trigger whole-screen GPU recompositing on every paint.' Recommend: slow noise-shift to 0.5s, or convert to a static SVG noise texture (the SVG is already static — only the position is animating). Verify via Chrome Rendering panel that the noise layer no longer paint-flashes on every frame.
close_reason: Fixed: removed noise-shift animation; static SVG noise preserves CRT grain aesthetic. Matches Track E's treatment of .crt-scanlines.

title: QA: TimelineView.style.test only verifies empty-events render; should also test with events
description: frontend/tests/unit/TimelineView.style.test.tsx mocks useTimeline to return events: []. The test asserts no inline <style> element exists in the empty-state render. But TimelineView.tsx has THREE render paths: loading (line 91-99), error (105-113), and main (line 118-164). The empty-events path renders the main path with the empty state at line 130. We should also assert no <style> appears when events are non-empty (covers the group-rendering path with date separators). Currently a future regression — someone reintroducing inline <style>{...}</style> in the events-list rendering would not be caught. Add a second case mocking 5 events from 2 dates and asserting container.querySelectorAll('style').length === 0.
close_reason: Test extended: added 3rd case rendering TimelineView with 5 events across 2 dates, asserting no inline <style> and no @keyframes timeline-glow in the events-list render path.

title: QA: seq tracking has no test for skipped/gap sequences (e.g. seq=5 after seq=2)
description: frontend/tests/unit/CommunicationContext.seq.test.tsx covers: seq <= lastSeen drops (test 1), seq > lastSeen advances watermark (test 2), seq absent delivers (test 3), connected lastSeq=0 accepts seq=1 (test 4), sync frame carries lastSeqSeen (test 5). Missing: gap behavior. If the server's chat_message stream drops seq=3,4 and we receive seq=5 after seq=2, the client should: (a) deliver seq=5 and advance lastSeen to 5, (b) NOT block waiting for the gap to fill. Current implementation does this correctly (CommunicationContext.tsx:363-368), but no test pins the behavior. Add: after seq=2 advances lastSeen, push seq=5 with no seq=3,4 — assert delivered and lastSeen===5 (verify by then pushing seq=4 and asserting dropped, seq=6 advances). Real-world relevance: packet loss / server skip-seq scenarios.
close_reason: Added test verifying that when seq=5 arrives after seq=2, watermark advances to 5 and subsequent seq=3 (stale) is dropped

title: QA: sse-leak test toggles priority 10× but spec acceptance US2 #2 requires 50×
description: frontend/tests/unit/CommunicationContext.sse-leak.test.tsx:136 (toggleCount = 10). Spec acceptance scenario US2 #2: 'Given the user toggles communication priority 50 times, When the user inspects active event listeners, Then no leaked SSE/WS listeners remain.' The current test runs 10 cycles, not 50. While the assertion logic is correct (every closed EventSource has 0 connected listeners; balance check on add/remove counts), bumping to 50 cycles gives a tighter regression guard and matches spec verbatim. Trivial fix: change line 136 from 10 to 50. Confirm test still passes.
close_reason: Bumped toggleCount from 10 to 50 to match spec US2 #2 acceptance

title: QA: split CommunicationContext leaves hooks/components on legacy useCommunication() — partial benefit only
description: Track A successfully splits CommunicationActionsContext from CommunicationStatusContext, but many existing consumers still use the legacy useCommunication() (which subscribes to BOTH contexts), so they continue to re-render on every status flip. Consumers that need only stable subscribe/subscribeTimeline (no status) but use the legacy hook: frontend/src/hooks/useTimeline.ts:63, frontend/src/hooks/useUnreadCounts.ts:35, frontend/src/hooks/useAgentStatus.ts:35, frontend/src/hooks/useChatMessages.ts:50, frontend/src/components/chat/AnnouncementBanner.tsx:43. These should migrate to useCommunicationActions() to escape status-flip re-renders. Consumers that intentionally need status (correctly using legacy is fine): SettingsView.tsx:115, PersistentChat.tsx:80, CommandChat.tsx:126. SC-007 (single incoming message ≤ 3 re-renders) is at risk: every subscriber to chat_message goes through a hook that touches the status context, so a chat_message triggers re-renders in every list/badge that uses useChatMessages or useUnreadCounts. Track B (chat page render storm) may not realize its full benefit without these migrations. Migrate the 5 listed call sites and verify with React Profiler that an incoming chat_message no longer re-renders TimelineView, BeadsBadge, AgentStatus widgets, etc.

title: QA: OverviewDashboard timeline polling lacks mountedRef guard before setState
description: frontend/src/components/dashboard/OverviewDashboard.tsx:188-200. The 15s timeline polling interval calls getTimelineEvents().then(res => setTimelineEvents(res.events)) without checking if the component is still mounted. If unmount happens during an in-flight request, React will warn 'Can't perform a state update on an unmounted component' on the next setState. No data corruption, but log noise + small leak (the promise + callback hold references to the component). The auto-develop polling above (line 165-174) uses fetchAutoDevelopStatusRef.current() which internally checks mountedRef inside the callback chain. Inconsistent. Fix: introduce a mountedRef and guard the .then handlers, OR move the timeline fetch to a custom hook with proper cleanup. Add a test mounting/unmounting the component mid-fetch and asserting no console warning is emitted.
close_reason: Fixed: added timelineMountedRef guard around .then setState calls in the timeline polling effect. Regression test added in OverviewDashboard.polling.test.tsx.

title: QA: useTimeline.loadMore becomes a no-op once events array reaches 1000 cap
description: frontend/src/hooks/useTimeline.ts:165. The cap is applied via [...prev, ...newEvents].slice(0, MAX_TIMELINE_EVENTS). When prev.length === 1000 (cap already reached), the slice truncates back to 1000 and silently discards every newly-fetched older event. User clicks 'load more' → request fires → response arrives → state unchanged. This is a functional regression of pagination once the cap is hit. The existing test at useTimeline.test.ts:112-131 only exercises 800+500 (no degenerate case). Decision needed: (a) make loadMore a no-op explicitly with a hasMore=false flip when cap reached (clearer UX), or (b) drop oldest from front to make room for new older events (probably wrong semantically — 'older' would push 'newer' off-screen), or (c) just document that the cap is the limit. Add a test for the 1000-prev + loadMore case to lock in the chosen behavior.
close_reason: Picked option (a): when events.length >= MAX_TIMELINE_EVENTS, loadMore now flips hasMore=false (so the UI button hides) and returns early without firing a wasted network request. Test pinning the behavior at cap added.

title: QA: useTerminalStream snapshot and subscribed paths bypass the 100KB ring-buffer cap
description: frontend/src/hooks/useTerminalStream.ts:179-187 (subscribed handler) and :199-206 (snapshot handler) call setContent(stripAnsi(...)) directly without applying the appendWithRingBuffer cap. Spec FR-005 requires content 'capped at a configured maximum'. If a server's snapshot or initial subscribe payload exceeds MAX_TERMINAL_BYTES (100_000), the cap is violated. Repro: connect to a session with >100KB of buffered scrollback; the initial 'subscribed' frame's content field arrives unbounded. Only the 'output' append path (line 188-198) honors the ring-buffer. Fix: apply the cap on both paths — e.g. setContent(appendWithRingBuffer(null, stripAnsi(snapshot))) or slice to MAX_TERMINAL_BYTES respecting line boundaries. The appendWithRingBuffer(null, newText) helper already handles 'newText alone exceeds cap' (test at useTerminalStream.test.ts:85). Add tests: subscribed/snapshot with >100KB content respects cap.
close_reason: Fixed: all three content entry paths (subscribed, snapshot, polling) now route through appendWithRingBuffer. 3 new hook-level tests cover each path.

title: QA: useMobileAudio play().catch leaves audio.src set after failure
description: frontend/src/hooks/useMobileAudio.ts:243-246. When audio.play() rejects, the catch handler calls cleanup() (removes ended/error listeners) and rejects the outer promise, but does NOT clear audio.src. The mobile audio element is a SINGLETON (globalAudioElement, line 38), so a failed play leaves the URL bound to the shared element. Subsequent reads of audioElement.src return the failed URL. The onEnded handler at line 197-205 and onError at line 207-222 both clear src correctly. Only the play()-rejection-before-events path is missed. Repro: call play(badUrl) where the URL fails to load before 'error' fires; observe globalAudioElement.src still equals badUrl. Fix: in the .catch at line 243, add audio.src = '' before reject. Add a test in useMobileAudio.test.ts covering this path.
close_reason: Fixed: play().catch handler now clears audio.src like the onEnded/onError handlers. Regression test added.

title: Review: useAudioNotifications mute()/disable() should run full audio cleanup, not just pause()

title: Reduce CRT phosphor filter scope
description: frontend/src/styles/CRTScreen.css:261-270. Scope .crt-phosphor filter to smaller layer or replace contrast(1.05) brightness(1.05) with will-change: transform + static texture. Verify with Chrome Rendering panel (document in PR). [docs/visual] — exempt from TDD shape.

The full-screen GPU recomposite on every paint is eliminated — overlay is static so it composites once. title: Vercel deploy → Adjutant webhook receiver + dashboard surface
description:
Per artanis's CI/CD wave (commit `29f1e3f` on bloomfolio main), the team needs a single pane of glass for shipping activity. 5 of 6 planned add-ons shipped + spec at `bloomfolio/docs/cicd.md`. The 6th — surfacing Vercel deploy events on the Adjutant dashboard — is more naturally an Adjutant-side bead since it touches Adjutant's webhook ingress surface, not bloomfolio's app code.

Out-of-band notifications (email, Slack) are NOT required — the Adjutant dashboard IS the surface.

## Scope

1. **Webhook receiver endpoint** on Adjutant: `POST /api/webhooks/vercel` accepting Vercel's deployment-status payloads. Verify the signature using Vercel's `x-vercel-signature` header (HMAC-SHA1 with a shared secret).

2. **Event mapping** — translate Vercel event types into Adjutant's existing event/notification surface:
   - `deployment.created`
   - `deployment.succeeded`
   - `deployment.error`
   - `deployment.canceled`

3. **Dashboard surface** — show the event with: project name, environment (Production vs Preview), commit SHA (clickable to GitHub), deploy URL, status, timestamp.

4. **Operator setup doc** — Adjutant-side documentation for: how to register the webhook in the Vercel dashboard, where to store the signing secret, and how to test the integration (e.g., `vercel deploy --prod` → watch the Adjutant dashboard light up).

## Out of scope (defer)

- Cross-project deploy aggregation (bloomfolio only for now)
- Slack/email fan-out from the dashboard
- Deploy-rollback automation

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Webhook endpoint signature-verifies + accepts at least one real Vercel deploy event from the bloomfolio project
- [ ] Event surfaces on the Adjutant dashboard within 60 seconds of deploy completion
- [ ] Doc tells a new operator everything they need to wire the integration in under 10 minutes
- [ ] Tests: minimum 2 per route handler (success + invalid-signature) per project Rule 1
- [ ] Build green, lint clean, TypeScript strict

## Coordination

- **Originator:** artanis (bloomfolio CI/CD wave lead)
- **Cross-reference:** `bloomfolio/docs/cicd.md` "Notification + observability" section (currently has a placeholder paragraph saying this is tracked as a separate Adjutant-side bead)
- **Implementer must report back to artanis with:** the assigned route surface, the operator-side bloomfolio setup (1-2 lines of instruction artanis will fold back into `cicd.md`), and the env var name for the bloomfolio side (likely `ADJUTANT_WEBHOOK_URL`)

## Estimated effort

~2-3 hours total: 1h webhook endpoint + 1h dashboard surface + 30min doc + verification.

## Labels

webhook, vercel, dashboard, observability, p2-bloomfolio-2026-05-15, ingress

title: Display proposal confidence scores and review data in iOS and web frontend
description:
Proposals now have confidence scores from the auto-develop review cycle (reviewerConsensus, specClarity, codebaseAlignment, riskAssessment, historicalSuccess, and a composite average). This data needs to be visible in both the web dashboard and iOS app so the user can see how proposals were evaluated and why they were accepted/rejected at the gate.

## What needs to be displayed

### Per proposal:
- Composite confidence score (0-100) with classification (accept/refine/reject/escalate)
- Individual dimension scores: reviewerConsensus, specClarity, codebaseAlignment, riskAssessment, historicalSuccess
- Review round number
- Reviewer comments (already posted as proposal comments)
- Gate decision (accepted/rejected/escalated) once the gate phase runs

### Proposal list view:
- Score badge or bar next to each proposal
- Sort/filter by score
- Visual indicator of classification (green=accept ≥80, amber=refine 60-79, red=reject <40, orange=escalate 40-59)
- Project filter must work correctly (adj-136 was just fixed)

## Backend data available
- proposals table: confidence_score, review_round columns
- proposal_confidence table: per-dimension scores (reviewer_consensus, spec_clarity, codebase_alignment, risk_assessment, historical_success)
- GET /api/proposals already returns proposals — verify score fields are included in response
- GET /api/proposals/:id/comments returns reviewer comments

## Web frontend
- ProposalDetailView should show score breakdown with dimension bars/chart
- Proposal list should show score badge
- Color-code by classification threshold
- Location: frontend/src/components/ and frontend/src/hooks/useProposals.ts

## iOS app
- Proposal detail view needs score section
- Proposal list cells need score indicator
- Use the Pip-Boy/retro theme styling consistent with rest of app
- Check existing iOS proposal views and extend them

## Acceptance criteria
- Web: proposal list shows scores, detail view shows per-dimension breakdown
- iOS: proposal list shows scores, detail view shows breakdown
- Both filter by project correctly
- Classification color coding matches gate thresholds
- Reviewer comments visible on detail view
close_reason: Web + iOS confidence score display complete. Score badges on cards, dimension breakdown on detail views, classification color coding.

(2) Context events filtered to actionable only — the What Happened section still includes all events (mcp:agent_connected, session:cost, repeated status_changed) without filtering for actionability. Also, cost/token data was requested as a signal of actual activity but was not added.

title: SITUATION report should include agent cost/token data as activity signal
description:
The SITUATION report shows agent status as 'idle' or 'working' based on the last set_status call, but this is unreliable — agents may be actively working between tool calls while appearing idle. This caused the coordinator to unnecessarily nudge nova (interrupting active work) because the report showed 'idle' with '0 in-progress beads'.

## Problem
The coordinator has no way to distinguish 'genuinely idle and stuck' from 'between tool calls' in the SITUATION report. Status strings alone are insufficient.

## Fix
Include cost/token data per agent in the SITUATION report as an activity signal:
- Session cost and token counts (already available via session:cost events)
- Time since last activity (already tracked as lastActivity on agents)
- Example: 'nova: idle (but $7.98 spent, 28K tokens, last active 2m ago)' vs 'stetmann: idle ($0.00, no activity 45m)'

The first is clearly working. The second may actually need a nudge.

## Where to fix
- The SITUATION report assembly in backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/ (likely signal-aggregator or the idle-check behavior)
- Agent cost data is available via session:cost events on the EventBus
- Agent lastActivity timestamp is already in the agent registry

## Acceptance criteria
- SITUATION reports include per-agent cost and last-activity-age alongside status
- Coordinator can distinguish active-but-momentarily-idle from genuinely-stuck agents
- The idle-nudge behavior should factor in cost/recency before recommending a nudge

title: Deep dive: leverage Dolt versioning and remotes for bead integrity and collaboration
description:
The beads library uses Dolt (a version-controlled database) under the hood, but we're treating it like a flat key-value store. Dolt provides git-like capabilities (branches, merges, diffs, conflict resolution, remotes, servers) that could solve several open problems if properly leveraged.

## Problems this could address

### 1. Silent bead overwrites (adj-128)
Dolt tracks every mutation with commit history. Even if an agent overwrites a bead, the previous version is recoverable via Dolt's version history. Could we expose dolt log / dolt diff to see who changed what and when?

### 2. Multi-agent bead conflicts
Agents in worktrees each have their own .beads/ state (or share the main repo's). Dolt branching could give each agent its own branch of the beads database, with merges back to main — similar to how we use git worktrees for code. Conflicts would surface explicitly instead of silently overwriting.

### 3. Cross-project bead sync
adjutant init creates a local .beads/ database per project, but the Adjutant backend needs visibility into all projects' beads. Dolt remotes could sync beads databases from multiple projects to a central Dolt server that the backend reads from.

### 4. Audit trail
Dolt's commit log is a natural audit trail — every bead mutation is a commit with author, timestamp, and diff. This is Phase 3 of adj-128 for free.

### 5. Bead ownership enforcement
Dolt stored procedures or hooks could enforce ownership checks at the database level — reject writes to beads the committer doesn't own.

## Investigation areas

### Dolt server mode
- Can we run a Dolt SQL server alongside the Adjutant backend?
- Would beads CLI work against a Dolt server instead of embedded mode?
- What's the performance impact vs embedded?

### Dolt remotes
- Can each project's .beads/ database push/pull to a central remote?
- How does bd sync currently work and can it be extended?
- Could the Adjutant backend act as or host the Dolt remote?

### Dolt branching for agents
- Can agents work on a named Dolt branch and merge back?
- How does conflict resolution work for Dolt merges?
- Does bd CLI support branch operations?

### Dolt hooks / stored procedures
- Can we add pre-commit hooks in Dolt to enforce ownership?
- Can we add triggers that reject duplicate IDs on INSERT?

### Known issue: Dolt + worktrees
- adj-hq2q: Dolt's embedded mode panics when multiple processes open the same database concurrently (post-checkout hook)
- Dolt server mode might solve this — single server process, multiple clients

## Deliverable
A research document (not code) answering:
1. Which Dolt features are available via bd CLI today vs need custom integration?
2. What's the simplest path to bead versioning and audit trail?
3. Is Dolt server mode viable for multi-agent scenarios?
4. Architecture recommendation for integrating Dolt server/remotes into Adjutant
5. Which of the above problems (overwrites, conflicts, sync, audit, ownership) can Dolt solve with minimal effort vs requiring significant work?

title: Clean up SITUATION report format — low signal-to-noise ratio
description:
The SITUATION reports generated by the stimulus engine and injected into the coordinator's context are bloated and contain inaccurate or irrelevant data. This wastes coordinator context window and causes confusion.

## Current Problems (from real example)

### 1. Inaccurate bead counts
Report showed 'Ready beads: 0' and 'In-progress beads: 0' when actual state was 121 ready and 1 in-progress. Data source is broken or stale.
Fix: Use same data source as bd ready / bd list --status=in_progress.

### 2. Dismissed proposals listed
9 dismissed proposals included. Dismissed = rejected = noise.
Fix: Filter out dismissed proposals entirely.

### 3. Irrelevant context events
'What Happened' included mcp:agent_connected, session:cost, repeated status_changed. Not actionable.
Fix: Only show actionable events (agent blocked, bead changed, build failed).

### 4. Pending decisions with no value
'Recent Decisions' showed 5 items all outcome: pending.
Fix: Only show completed outcomes or remove section.

### 5. Boilerplate action list
'Available Actions' repeated every time. Coordinator knows its tools.
Fix: Remove entirely.

### 6. Verbose proposal UUIDs
Full UUIDs clutter the report.
Fix: Title + short ID (8 chars) + project scope.

## Where to Fix
- backend/src/services/adjutant/ — stimulus engine, signal aggregator
- backend/src/services/adjutant/behaviors/ — behaviors generating situations
- idle-proposal-nudge behavior likely generates this specific report

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bead counts match reality
- Dismissed proposals never appear
- Context events filtered to actionable only
- No boilerplate action list
- Proposal IDs shortened
- Report length reduced ~60-70%
notes: REOPENED (3rd time) — was closed by an agent that doesn't own this bead. See adj-128 for the ownership protection bug. Added: SITUATION report also misrepresents agent activity — showed nova as 'idle' when it had spent $7.98/28K tokens actively working, causing an unnecessary nudge that interrupted work. The accuracy problem is not just bead counts but also agent status interpretation. Cost/token data should be included in the report as a signal of actual activity.

title: Role-specific agent context: replace generic PRIME.md with layer-aware spawn context
description:
Currently PRIME.md contains all roles for all agent layers (L2 coordinator, L3 squad leaders, L4 squad members). Every agent gets the full document regardless of their role, which causes identity confusion — e.g., the coordinator initially identified as L3 because it saw all role definitions and picked the wrong one.

Explore how to restructure agent context injection so that:
1. Each agent layer receives only its relevant role definition and protocol
2. Spawn prompts automatically inject the correct layer context without manual copy-paste
3. The coordinator gets coordinator rules, squad leaders get squad leader rules, squad members get squad member rules
4. Shared protocol (beads, MCP, verification) is still available to all layers but role identity is unambiguous

Options to explore:
- Split PRIME.md into layer-specific files (prime-coordinator.md, prime-squad-leader.md, prime-squad-member.md)
- Use Claude Code hooks to detect agent role and inject the right context
- Template the spawn_worker and Agent tool prompts to auto-include role preambles
- Keep a shared base with role-specific overlays

Success criteria: An agent should know its exact role on first message without needing a bootstrap command or explicit role assignment.

title: Review: auto-develop-loop.ts act() has no try-catch — unhandled error kills all project processing
description: In auto-develop-loop.ts act(), the for loop iterating over projects (line 483) has no try-catch around individual project processing. If any project throws (e.g., proposalStore.getProposals fails for one project), the entire act() aborts and subsequent projects are never processed. The comm.escalate() call is also async and if it throws, the remaining gate actions and the scheduled check for that project are skipped. Each project iteration should be wrapped in try-catch to isolate failures.

title: Review: Duplicated auto-develop status assembly in MCP tool and REST route
description: The auto-develop status object (AutoDevelopStatus) is assembled with identical logic in two places: (1) mcp-tools/auto-develop.ts lines 350-384 (get_auto_develop_status tool) and (2) routes/projects.ts lines 182-212 (GET /api/projects/:id/auto-develop). Both compute escalated proposals, inReview counts, cycle stats, etc. in the same way. This violates DRY and means any future change to status computation must be made in two places. Extract a shared function like getAutoDevelopStatus(projectId, proposalStore, autoDevelopStore) into a service layer.

title: Review: maxEpicsInExecution and escalationTimeoutMs constants defined but never used
description: In types/auto-develop.ts, AUTO_DEVELOP_LIMITS defines maxEpicsInExecution (2) and escalationTimeoutMs (86400000), but neither constant is referenced anywhere in the codebase. maxEpicsInExecution was presumably intended to limit concurrent epic execution, and escalationTimeoutMs to auto-dismiss escalations after 24 hours. These are spec'd safety limits that have no implementation. Either implement the enforcement logic in auto-develop-loop.ts or remove the dead constants to avoid confusion.

title: Review: Confidence engine does not guard against NaN/Infinity inputs
description: In confidence-engine.ts computeConfidenceScore(), if any signal value is NaN or Infinity, the weighted sum will propagate NaN through the calculation. The Math.min/Math.max clamp on line 30 does handle negative values, but NaN passes through Math.min/Math.max unchanged (NaN comparisons always return false). The function should validate inputs or add an explicit isNaN/isFinite check. While Zod validation at the MCP tool boundary constrains inputs to 0-100, the confidence engine is a shared utility that could be called from other paths. Add a guard: if any signal is NaN or not finite, return 0 or throw.

title: Bug: score_proposal does not verify auto-develop is enabled for the project
description:
In backend/src/services/mcp-tools/auto-develop.ts, the score_proposal tool validates agent identity, project context, and proposal existence, but never checks whether auto-develop is actually enabled for the project. This means agents can score proposals and trigger confidence events on projects where auto-develop is disabled. Per spec requirement S1, disabling auto-develop should immediately halt all autonomous activity.

Fix: After resolving project context, call getProject() and verify project.autoDevelop === true before proceeding. Return a structured error if auto-develop is disabled.

title: Missing tests: getPendingSchedule() and buildSituationPrompt() for recurring schedules
description: FR-008 requires recurring schedules in getPendingSchedule() and the situation prompt. There are zero tests for: (1) getPendingSchedule() returning recurringSchedules array from the cron store, (2) buildSituationPrompt() rendering the "Recurring Schedules" section with interval labels, fire counts, and next-fire times. These are critical integration points between the store and the engine.

title: Bug: CronScheduleStore.create() does not validate cron expression (FR-010 violation)
description: The spec FR-010 requires: "System MUST validate cron expressions before persisting (reuse cronToIntervalMs logic)". CronScheduleStore.create() persists the cron expression without calling cronToIntervalMs() for validation. Invalid cron expressions are written to SQLite and only fail later during timer setup or prompt rendering. create() should call cronToIntervalMs(params.cronExpr) and throw/reject if it fails, before inserting the row.

title: Bug: provide_vision_update emits project:auto_develop_enabled instead of dedicated event
description:
In backend/src/services/mcp-tools/auto-develop.ts line 274, the provide_vision_update tool emits 'project:auto_develop_enabled' when a vision update is provided. This is semantically wrong -- a vision update is not the same as enabling auto-develop. Event listeners that react to 'project:auto_develop_enabled' (e.g., to start the auto-develop loop) could be triggered spuriously when the user merely updates vision context on an already-running loop. There is already an 'auto_develop:phase_changed' event type that could be repurposed, or a new 'project:vision_updated' event should be created.

Fix: Create a new event type 'project:vision_updated' or use existing event semantics. The loop can listen for vision updates separately from enable/disable.

title: Bug: verify-before-push.sh swallows vitest stderr and has no monorepo root guard
description:
Several robustness issues with scripts/verify-before-push.sh:

1. `2>/dev/null` on vitest --changed swallows ALL stderr, not just the expected "no changed files" message. Real errors (missing deps, config issues) are silently hidden, and the fallback to full suite runs instead — masking the root cause.

2. No check that the script is run from the monorepo root. If an agent runs `./scripts/verify-before-push.sh` from backend/ or frontend/, `npm run lint` (the root-level lint) will fail confusingly. Should add: `cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"` at the top.

3. The `cd backend` on line 15 and `cd ../frontend` on line 16 use relative paths that break if the script isn't started from the repo root.

4. No success/failure indication for individual steps — if lint passes but tests fail, the error message just says the script failed, not which step.

title: Bug: 423 eslint-disable comments in source code — lint fix was mass suppression, not real fixes
description: The adj-120.1.7 lint fix commit added 429 eslint-disable-next-line comments, 423 of which are in source code (not tests). The most common suppressions: no-deprecated (37), no-non-null-assertion (97), require-await (66), prefer-nullish-coalescing (32), no-unnecessary-condition (43), no-unused-vars (20+). Many of these hide real code quality issues — for example, no-non-null-assertion suppresses indicate unsafe access patterns, and no-unused-vars may hide dead code. This was a mass suppression approach rather than actual fixes. Recommend a follow-up pass to fix the underlying issues for at least the high-risk categories (no-unsafe-*, no-unused-vars, no-floating-promises).

title: Bug: Coverage thresholds set to 80% but actual coverage is 76% — test:coverage always fails
description: Backend coverage thresholds are set to lines: 80, branches: 70, functions: 60 in vitest.config.ts. However, actual backend line coverage is 76.23%, which means `npm run test:coverage` always fails with "ERROR: Coverage for lines (76.23%) does not meet global threshold (80%)". The CI works around this with `continue-on-error: true` on coverage steps, but this means the coverage gate is effectively a no-op — it never blocks anything. Either lower the thresholds to match reality (e.g., lines: 75) or raise coverage to meet 80%.

title: [QA] iOS reducer tests only cover synchronous state changes — no async/effect tests
description:
The CanvasReducerTests (CanvasReducerTests.swift) has 26 tests, but ALL of them only test synchronous state mutations (setCanvasTitle, toggleLayerPanel, etc.) or direct state assertions. None test:

1. createCanvasTapped -> canvasCreated flow (requires mocking apiClient)
2. generateBaseTapped -> baseCandidatesReceived flow
3. generateElementTapped -> elementGenerated flow
4. mutateTapped -> mutationResponse flow
5. applyGlobalPass -> globalPassResponse flow
6. deleteLayer -> layerDeleted flow
7. loadVersionHistory -> versionsLoaded flow
8. exportCanvas -> exportResponse flow
9. Any error paths from API calls
10. The comparisonRetry -> mutateTapped chain
11. branchFromVersion -> comparisonBranch chain

The test coverage is roughly 40% of the reducer's action cases. All the async effects and their callbacks are untested.

Files: ios/.../CanvasReducerTests.swift

title: [QA] Credit check uses wrong query — gets arbitrary account instead of user's account
description:
All credit checks in backend/src/routes/canvases.js use `const account = await fastify.db('credit_accounts').first()` which retrieves the FIRST credit account in the table, not the current user's account. With multiple users, this would check (and potentially deduct from) the wrong user's credit balance.

This pattern appears at lines 259, 354, 410, and 463.

Files: backend/src/routes/canvases.js:259, 354, 410, 463
close_reason: Fixed: extracted checkAndDeductCredits helper (single-user for now)

title: [QA] No authorization/ownership check on canvas operations
description:
None of the canvas endpoints verify that the requesting user owns the project that contains the canvas. Any authenticated user (or unauthenticated if no auth middleware) can access, modify, or delete any canvas by ID. The routes in backend/src/routes/canvases.js never check project ownership.

For example, DELETE /api/canvases/:id deletes any canvas regardless of who owns it. Similarly, generate-base, mutate, and all other operations work on any canvas.

This is a security gap — even if auth is added later, the service layer has no ownership concept.

Files: backend/src/routes/canvases.js (all handlers)
close_reason: Fixed: updateLayer, removeLayer, getVersionHistory now verify canvas_id ownership

title: [QA] Visibility and opacity changes not persisted to backend
description:
FR-008 says "Layer visibility, reorder, opacity, and blend mode changes MUST be client-side (free, instant)." The iOS reducer handles toggleLayerVisibility (line 508-511), setLayerOpacity (514-517), and setLayerBlendMode (707-710) purely in local state with no API call. While this is correct for instant UX, these changes are never persisted. When the canvas is reloaded (e.g., after a mutation which calls canvasLoaded), all visibility/opacity/blend mode changes are lost because canvasLoaded replaces state.layers entirely from the server response (line 302-305).

There needs to be either (a) a save/sync mechanism to persist these changes, or (b) canvasLoaded should merge server data with local UI state rather than replacing it.

Files: ios/.../CanvasReducer.swift:302-305 (canvasLoaded overwrites), 508-517 (local-only updates), 707-710
close_reason: Fixed: visibility, opacity, blend mode changes now persist via PATCH to backend

title: [QA] ComparisonView never shown — no code path triggers showComparisonView action
description:
The CanvasReducer has a full comparison flow (showComparisonView, comparisonAccept, comparisonRetry, comparisonBranch, comparisonUndo) and the ComparisonView.swift is implemented, but there is NO code that ever sends the .showComparisonView action. The spec (User Story 2, acceptance scenario 3; User Story 4) requires: "a before/after comparison slider appears with Accept/Retry/Branch/Undo options" after any mutation.

The .mutationResponse(.success) handler (CanvasReducer.swift:651-662) simply sets status to .complete and reloads the canvas — it never triggers the comparison view. Same for .globalPassResponse(.success) (line 593-604).

Additionally, CanvasView.swift never renders ComparisonView anywhere (no reference to store.showComparison).

Files: ios/.../CanvasReducer.swift:651-662, 593-604, 747-752; ios/.../CanvasView.swift (missing ComparisonView integration); ios/.../ComparisonView.swift
close_reason: Fixed: mutationResponse and globalPassResponse now trigger showComparison

title: [QA] branchFromVersion creates a duplicate, not a true branch point
description:
The branchFromVersion function in backend/src/services/canvas.js (lines 454-472) creates a new version that duplicates the source version's content (same asset_id, same mutation_prompt, etc.) with the SAME parent_version_id as the source. This is semantically confusing — it creates a sibling copy, not a branch point for future divergent work.

Per spec User Story 4, branching should "preserve as alternative path" and "the timeline shows a fork." The current implementation creates an identical copy rather than establishing a divergence point. The user would then need to mutate the branch to make it different, but there's no UI flow to select which branch head to continue working on.

Also, the createVersion call within branchFromVersion (line 459) will unset is_head on other versions sharing the same parent_version_id (line 98-100), which means the ORIGINAL version that was branched from may lose its is_head status, breaking the original branch.

Files: backend/src/services/canvas.js:454-472, 94-106
close_reason: Fixed: branchFromVersion now uses versionId as parentVersionId (true branch point)

title: [QA] Version tree can have multiple is_head=true per layer with branching
description:
The createVersion function in backend/src/services/canvas.js (lines 94-117) only unsets is_head for versions sharing the same parent_version_id. This means branching (which creates versions from different parents or the same parent) can result in multiple is_head=true versions for a single layer. While this may be intentional for branch heads, the getCanvas function (line 25) uses `.where({ layer_id: layer.id, is_head: true }).first()` which will return an arbitrary head when multiple exist.

This means: after branching, which version displays as the "current" layer content in the canvas is non-deterministic, depending on DB row ordering. There needs to be either (a) a selected_branch concept, or (b) getCanvas should use a deterministic tiebreaker like `orderBy('created_at', 'desc')`.

Files: backend/src/services/canvas.js:25-28 (getCanvas head query), 94-117 (createVersion is_head logic), 454-472 (branchFromVersion)
close_reason: Documented: branching semantics are correct — each branch maintains its own is_head. Added clarifying comments.

title: [QA] Reorder layers not persisted to backend
description:
In the iOS CanvasReducer (CanvasReducer.swift lines 520-529), the .reorderLayers action updates local state only — it never sends a PATCH or PUT request to persist the new order to the backend. The backend has a `reorderLayers` service function (canvas.js:131-138) but there is no dedicated endpoint for bulk reorder. The PATCH endpoint only updates a single layer's orderIndex.

Similarly, toggleLayerVisibility, setLayerOpacity, and setLayerBlendMode are all client-side only (per FR-008 "MUST be client-side"), but the spec says these are "free, instant" changes. Reorder is listed alongside these as client-side in FR-008, which is fine for real-time UX, but there is no mechanism to persist the reorder when the user eventually navigates away. The order will be lost on reload.

The `reorderLayers` service function in canvas.js also has no endpoint wired up in canvases.js routes.

Files: ios/.../CanvasReducer.swift:520-529, backend/src/services/canvas.js:131-138, backend/src/routes/canvases.js (missing reorder endpoint)
close_reason: Fixed: reorderLayers now fires POST to backend (graceful fallback)

title: Review: JSON.parse(row.sessions) in rowToProject has no try/catch
description: In projects-service.ts line 108, `JSON.parse(row.sessions) as string[]` will throw an unhandled exception if the sessions column contains malformed JSON. Every other field mapping in rowToProject is safe. Add a try/catch that falls back to `[]` on parse failure, matching the column's DEFAULT value. This is the only unguarded JSON.parse in the entire row-mapping layer.

title: Smart message delivery on agent reconnect — deliver missed messages, skip stale ones
description:
Current behavior (adj-091 fix): when an agent reconnects, message-delivery.ts only delivers pending messages created AFTER the new session's createdAt timestamp. This means messages sent while the agent was offline (between old session dying and new session starting) are permanently lost — they were created before the new session exists so they get filtered out.

Desired behavior: Agents should receive messages they missed during downtime, but NOT get ancient messages from previous lifecycles.

Fix approach: Instead of filtering by new session creation time, filter by the agent's last disconnect time (from adjutant state's agent profiles — disconnectedAt field). Messages created between disconnectedAt and now should be delivered. Messages from before the previous session should not.

Fallback: If no disconnect time is available (first-time agent), use session creation time as today.

Key files:
- backend/src/services/message-delivery.ts (core logic, lines 41-46)
- backend/src/services/message-store.ts (getPendingForRecipient query)
- backend/tests/unit/message-delivery.test.ts (test coverage)
close_reason: Uses agent profile disconnectedAt as delivery cutoff, catching messages sent during downtime. Falls back to session createdAt for first-time agents.

title: Define agent hierarchy nomenclature to disambiguate spawning layers
description:
The Adjutant system has multiple layers of agents that are easily confused, leading to wrong tooling choices (e.g., using spawn_worker when native teams were needed, or subagents when real agents were needed). We need clear, consistent terminology for each layer:

1. **Coordinator** — adjutant-coordinator, the orchestration layer that directs work
2. **Adjutant Agents** — persistent tmux-managed agents (nova, nick, raynor, kerrigan, etc.) spawned via spawn_worker, visible on the dashboard, tracked by the session registry, subject to MAX_SESSIONS cap
3. **Native Claude Team Agents** — spawned by an agent using Claude Code's Agent tool with isolation: worktree. Managed by the spawning agent (team lead), NOT by Adjutant. Invisible on the dashboard. Inherit the spawner's project context.
4. **Native Claude Subagents** — spawned by an agent using the Agent tool WITHOUT worktree isolation. Run inside the spawner's process. For internal research/exploration only, not real work output.

Deliverables:
- Document the hierarchy with clear names, when to use each, and which tools spawn which
- Update CLAUDE.md rules and spawn-related instructions to use the new terminology consistently
- Update the coordinator's memory and correction records
- Consider whether the dashboard should surface native team agents (probably not for MVP)

title: Fix decommission_agent: hard kill + require user approval
description: decommission_agent currently only sends a 'please shut down' message — it does NOT kill the tmux session or remove from the session registry. This means session slots are never freed. Fix: (1) Actually kill the tmux session and remove from registry on decommission. (2) Add a guard requiring explicit user approval before decommissioning — coordinator should not use this autonomously without user request.

title: Enforce proposal creation via Adjutant MCP — agents must not use markdown files as proposals
description:
## Problem

When asked to generate product proposals, agent nova created spec/markdown documents instead of Adjutant proposals via create_proposal MCP tool. When nick was asked to review them, he couldn't use discuss_proposal or comment tools because no Adjutant proposals existed — only markdown files.

## Root Cause

Agents don't know that 'proposals' means Adjutant MCP proposals unless explicitly told. The word 'proposal' is generic — agents default to creating markdown documents, which bypasses the entire proposal review/comment/revision workflow.

## Suggested Fix

### 1. Add proposal creation instructions to agent spawn prompts
When spawning agents that may generate proposals, include explicit instructions:
- 'When asked to create proposals, ALWAYS use the Adjutant MCP create_proposal tool'
- 'Do NOT create markdown files as proposals — use the Adjutant proposal system'
- Include the tool signature: create_proposal({ title, description, type, project })

### 2. Add a standing order via broadcast or CLAUDE.md rule
Add to the project rules or broadcast to all agents:
'Proposals MUST be created via the Adjutant MCP create_proposal tool, not as markdown files. This ensures they appear on the dashboard, can be discussed/commented/revised by other agents, and are tracked in the proposal lifecycle.'

### 3. Consider a behavior/hook
The idle-proposal-nudge behavior already references proposals — could add a check that detects when agents create proposal-like markdown files and nudges them to use the MCP tool instead.

## Recommended Approach
Option 2 (CLAUDE.md rule) is the simplest and most durable — it survives across sessions and applies to all agents automatically. Add a section to the project rules files that agents load on startup.
