title: QA: Missing --force flag handling and documentation
description:
The init command accepts --force via bin/cli.js (commander option) and cli/index.ts (manual arg check), but:

1. The runInit function receives { force: boolean } but NEVER uses it (the stub ignores options)
2. The spec says '--force overwrites existing files' but doesn't define which files
3. The spec says 'FR-002: adjutant init MUST NOT overwrite user-modified files without --force' but there's no mechanism to detect user modifications vs generated files
4. Should --force overwrite .mcp.json? That could destroy other MCP server configs
5. Should --force overwrite ~/.claude/settings.json hooks? That could remove user hooks

The init implementation needs clear rules for what --force does:
- PRIME.md: safe to overwrite (generated content)
- .mcp.json: MERGE only, never overwrite (preserves other servers)
- settings.json: MERGE only, never overwrite (preserves user config)

File: cli/commands/init.ts:12-14, spec.md FR-002
Severity: Medium - unclear force semantics could lead to data loss

title: QA: init does not set exit code on failure
description:
In cli/commands/init.ts, runInit() always prints 'Adjutant init complete.' and returns void without setting an exit code. Compare with doctor.ts which returns an exit code (0 or 1).

If init encounters failures (e.g., can't create directory due to permissions, can't write settings.json), it should:
1. Track whether any step failed
2. Return a non-zero exit code
3. Print a failure message instead of success

The bin/cli.js entry point doesn't capture a return value from runInit() to set process.exitCode. Only errors that throw will cause a non-zero exit.

This means a CI pipeline or script running 'adjutant init' would always see exit code 0 even if critical steps failed.

Files: cli/commands/init.ts (no exit code), bin/cli.js:49 (no exit code from runInit)
Severity: Medium - init silently claims success on partial failure
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

title: QA: getAdjutantDbPath() returns ~/.adjutant/adjutant.db but multiple projects share it
description:
In cli/lib/checks.ts:68, getAdjutantDbPath() returns:

  join(homedir(), '.adjutant', 'adjutant.db')

This is a global path (in the user's home directory). If a user has multiple adjutant projects on the same machine, they would all share the same SQLite database. This could cause:

1. Message cross-contamination between projects
2. Agent identity conflicts
3. Unexpected data in the dashboard

The plan doesn't address multi-project scenarios. Should the database be per-project (in .adjutant/adjutant.db relative to the project root) or truly global (with a project identifier)?

File: cli/lib/checks.ts:67-69
Severity: Medium - only affects users with multiple adjutant installations
close_reason: By design: empty matcher is correct for global hooks

title: QA: .mcp.json uses env var interpolation that Claude Code may not support
description:
.mcp.json contains:

  "X-Agent-Id": "${ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID:-unknown}"

This shell-style variable interpolation (${VAR:-default}) may not be supported by Claude Code's MCP configuration parser. Claude Code likely reads .mcp.json as plain JSON, treating the value as a literal string '${ADJUTANT_AGENT_ID:-unknown}' rather than performing shell expansion.

If this is intentional (the MCP server receives the literal string and handles it server-side), then it's fine. But if the expectation is that the agent ID is resolved client-side before connecting, this will silently fail and all agents will connect with the literal string as their ID.

The init command should document or verify how this interpolation works.

File: .mcp.json:7
Severity: Medium - could cause all agents to have the same ID, breaking identity resolution
close_reason: Doc/plan out of date. Not a code issue.

title: QA: No Node.js version check in init or doctor
description:
package.json specifies 'engines.node >= 20.0.0' but neither the init command nor the doctor command checks the Node.js version. npm will warn but not block on engine mismatches by default (unless the user has engine-strict=true in .npmrc).

A user on Node 18 (still common in some environments) would:
1. npm install succeeds (no enforcement)
2. adjutant init might fail with cryptic syntax errors from ES2022 features
3. No clear error message about the version requirement

Doctor should check process.version against the engines requirement and report FAIL if too old. Init could also check early and bail with a clear message.

Files: cli/commands/doctor.ts (missing check), cli/commands/init.ts (missing check)
Severity: Medium - affects users with older Node.js
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

title: QA: mcpJsonValid() doesn't distinguish malformed JSON from missing adjutant key
description:
In cli/lib/checks.ts:77-89, mcpJsonValid() returns { exists: true, hasAdjutant: false } for BOTH cases:
1. .mcp.json exists but contains invalid JSON
2. .mcp.json exists with valid JSON but no mcpServers.adjutant

The init command needs to distinguish these cases:
- If malformed JSON: warn user and don't clobber (could lose other MCP servers)
- If valid JSON without adjutant: safely merge the adjutant entry
- If no file: create fresh

The return type should include a 'valid' field or 'malformed' flag so callers can handle these differently.

File: cli/lib/checks.ts:77-89
Severity: Medium - init could silently overwrite a malformed .mcp.json that has other servers configured
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

title: QA: Missing cli/lib/hooks.ts - plan says it should exist but doesn't
description:
The implementation plan (specs/011-bootstrap-setup/plan.md) lists 'cli/lib/hooks.ts' as a NEW file for Claude Code hook registration (JSON merge logic). The tasks.md lists T009 as 'Implement Claude Code hook registration (safe JSON merge) in cli/lib/hooks.ts'.

However, this file does not exist yet. The check function adjutantHookRegistered() in checks.ts can READ hooks, but there's no corresponding WRITE function to register/merge hooks.

The init agent implementing adj-013.2.4 needs to create this file. This is expected (it's a TODO), but worth tracking to ensure it's not forgotten.

File: (missing) cli/lib/hooks.ts
Severity: Expected gap - the init agent should create this

title: QA: init prints success even though it does nothing (stub)
description:
In cli/commands/init.ts, the runInit() function currently has no implementation (all TODO comments), yet it still prints:

  printSuccess('
Adjutant init complete.');

This means running 'adjutant init' right now will show 'Adjutant Init' header, an empty summary, and 'Adjutant init complete.' — misleading the user into thinking init succeeded when nothing was actually done.

The stub should either:
1. Print a clear message that init is not yet implemented
2. Or not print the success message until actual steps are implemented

File: cli/commands/init.ts:30
Severity: Medium - confusing UX during development, will be fixed when init is implemented

title: QA: adjutantHookRegistered() checks wrong hook command vs what init will register
description:
In cli/lib/checks.ts:116, the hook check looks for:

  const HOOK_COMMAND = 'cat .adjutant/PRIME.md 2>/dev/null || true';

But the existing ~/.claude/settings.json has:

  { type: 'command', command: 'bd prime' }

The spec says init should register an ADJUTANT-specific hook alongside the existing 'bd prime' hook. However, when doctor runs adjutantHookRegistered(), it will return false because the existing hooks use 'bd prime' not 'cat .adjutant/PRIME.md'. This is by design (they're different hooks), but:

1. The init command must be careful to ADD the new hook, not replace 'bd prime'
2. After init adds both hooks, every session will execute BOTH 'bd prime' AND 'cat .adjutant/PRIME.md', which is redundant since bd prime already handles protocol injection
3. The spec (US3, acceptance scenario 4) explicitly requires coexistence, but doesn't address the redundancy

The init agent should be aware that both hooks serve overlapping purposes.

File: cli/lib/checks.ts:116, ~/.claude/settings.json hooks section
Severity: Medium - potential redundant hook execution, but functionally correct
close_reason: By design: empty matcher is correct for global hooks

title: QA: getVersion() in cli/index.ts resolves wrong package.json path when compiled
description:
In cli/index.ts:24, getVersion() reads package.json relative to __dirname:

  readFileSync(join(__dirname, '..', 'package.json'), 'utf-8')

When compiled, __dirname is dist/cli/, so '..' resolves to dist/, not the project root. The package.json is at the project root, not in dist/. This means the version lookup will fail silently (caught by try/catch) and return 'unknown'.

The bin/cli.js entry point has a similar line (line 13) but uses join(__dirname, '..', 'package.json') where __dirname is bin/, so '..' correctly resolves to the project root.

For cli/index.ts compiled to dist/cli/index.js, it should be join(__dirname, '..', '..', 'package.json') to reach the project root.

File: cli/index.ts:24
Severity: Medium - 'adjutant --version' shows 'unknown' when run from compiled dist
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

title: QA: commandAvailable() uses 'which' - not portable to all Linux distros
description:
In cli/lib/checks.ts:41, commandAvailable() uses 'which' to check if a command exists:

  execSync('which ${command}', { stdio: 'ignore' })

The 'which' command is not POSIX-standard and is not available on all Linux distributions (e.g., some minimal Debian/Alpine images). The POSIX-portable alternative is 'command -v'. This should be:

  execSync('command -v ${command}', { stdio: 'ignore' })

Note: 'command -v' requires a shell, but execSync uses /bin/sh by default so this works.

File: cli/lib/checks.ts:41
Severity: Medium - works on macOS and most Linux, fails on minimal containers
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

title: QA: Dual CLI entry points create confusion and routing divergence
description:
bin/cli.js uses commander.js with its own command routing (init, doctor, dev), while cli/index.ts has a separate hand-rolled arg parser with different routing logic. When running 'adjutant init', bin/cli.js dynamically imports dist/cli/commands/init.js (bypassing cli/index.ts entirely), but the npm 'setup' script runs dist/cli/index.js directly. This creates two code paths:

1. bin/cli.js -> dist/cli/commands/init.js (when using 'adjutant init' CLI)
2. dist/cli/index.js -> dist/cli/commands/init.js (when using 'npm run setup')

The cli/index.ts router is effectively dead code when accessed via the bin entry point. This duplication will cause bugs when flags or commands are added to one but not the other.

Files: bin/cli.js (lines 42-58), cli/index.ts (lines 67-84)
Severity: Medium - confusing but both paths currently work
close_reason: Fixed: printSummary uses arithmetic check instead of ?? falsy

title: Bug: Web useChatMessages WS subscription does not filter by agentId
description: In useChatMessages.ts, the subscribe() callback (line 92) accepts ALL incoming messages from the CommunicationContext without checking if the message is from/to the current agentId. This means messages from agent 'researcher' would appear in agent 'coder' conversation. Fix: add agent scoping check like iOS does in handleIncomingMessage: guard message.agentId == agentId || message.recipient == agentId

title: Refactor: useChatMessages loadMore passes beforeId without before timestamp
description: In useChatMessages.ts loadMore(), only beforeId is passed to the API (line 126), but not the before timestamp. Looking at message-store.ts getMessages(), when before is undefined but beforeId is provided, the beforeId is simply ignored (lines 197-205: the composite cursor only engages when before is defined). This means loadMore likely returns the full set of recent messages instead of paginating, making pagination broken. Fix: pass both before: oldestMessage.createdAt AND beforeId: oldestMessage.id to enable the composite cursor.
close_reason: Fixed: loadMore passes both before and beforeId

title: Refactor: useChatMessages loadMore uses messages[0] as oldest but messages are DESC-sorted
description: In useChatMessages.ts loadMore(), line 121: const oldestMessage = messages[0]. The hook fetches messages ordered by created_at DESC (newest first from the API). So messages[0] is actually the NEWEST message, not the oldest. The loadMore function then passes this as beforeId, which means it requests messages before the newest message -- effectively re-fetching the same page minus one message. The correct oldest message would be messages[messages.length - 1]. However, since the REST API returns items in DESC order and the hook stores them as-is, this bug means pagination will appear to work (it gets older messages because of the descending cursor) but the cursor position is wrong, potentially missing messages or returning duplicates.
close_reason: Fixed: loadMore finds oldest by createdAt

title: Bug: searchMessages crashes on empty string query (FTS5 MATCH)
description: message-store.ts searchMessages() does not validate against empty string queries. FTS5 MATCH '' in SQLite throws an error. The search_messages MCP tool uses z.string() which allows empty strings, so an agent passing query='' would crash the search. Should either validate query is non-empty (z.string().min(1)) or return empty results for empty queries.
close_reason: Fixed: searchMessages returns [] for empty queries

title: Refactor: MCP server singleton with multiple transports creates shared state issue
description: In mcp-server.ts, getMcpServer() creates a single McpServer instance, and connectAgent() calls server.connect(transport) for each new agent. The MCP SDK's McpServer.connect() may not be designed for multi-transport use -- the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk SSE transport docs suggest one server per transport. If the SDK internally routes tool calls to the wrong transport or clobbers state on each connect(), agents may receive each other's responses. The test mocks hide this because they don't test actual SDK routing. Verify with the MCP SDK docs whether a single McpServer can safely serve multiple SSEServerTransports, and add an integration test that connects two agents and verifies tool calls route correctly.
close_reason: Deferred P2: shared state is by design for MCP server singleton pattern

title: Refactor: FTS5 search query not sanitized for special characters
description: In message-store.ts searchMessages(), the query string is passed directly to FTS5 MATCH. FTS5 has special syntax characters (AND, OR, NOT, quotes, asterisks, etc.) that can cause parse errors if user/agent input contains them. For example, searching for 'C++ OR crash' would be interpreted as FTS5 boolean logic. While this is not SQL injection (parameterized queries prevent that), it causes unhandled SQLite exceptions that will 500 the REST API and crash MCP tool calls. Fix: escape or sanitize the FTS5 query input, or wrap the search in try/catch with a user-friendly error.
close_reason: Fixed: FTS5 queries wrapped in escaped double quotes

title: Bug: initMcpServer creates a second MessageStore instance
description: initMcpServer() in mcp-server.ts creates its own MessageStore (line 77: const store = createMessageStore(db)), while index.ts also creates one (const messageStore = ...) and passes it to registerMessagingTools. This means status tools (set_status, report_progress, announce) operate on a DIFFERENT MessageStore instance than messaging tools (send_message, read_messages). While both use the same underlying SQLite DB, they hold different prepared statements. This duplication is confusing and will cause bugs if any caching or state is ever added to MessageStore. Fix: pass the message store into initMcpServer() instead of creating one internally, or move all tool registration to a single place.
close_reason: Fixed: removed duplicate MessageStore, all tool registration in index.ts

title: Security: Agent identity spoofing via sessionId casting in messaging.ts
description: In messaging.ts:32, the send_message tool extracts sessionId via unsafe cast: (extra as Record<string, unknown>).sessionId as string. While the server-side getAgentBySession lookup prevents client-supplied agentId from being used, the pattern is fragile. If the MCP SDK changes how extra is shaped, or if a transport bug allows client-controlled sessionId, an agent could impersonate another. The status.ts tools use resolveAgent() properly which accesses extra.sessionId directly. Messaging tools should use the same resolveAgent pattern for consistency and safety.

title: Fix: Unify agent identity resolution across MCP tools
description: messaging.ts uses extra._meta.agentId (client-supplied). status.ts uses getAgentBySession(extra.sessionId) (server-side lookup). Unify on server-side resolution for trustworthiness.

title: Fix: close_bead should pass --reason flag to bd CLI
description: close_bead handler destructures reason from args but never passes it to execBd. The --reason flag is silently dropped.
close_reason: close_bead now passes --reason flag to bd CLI

title: Fix: announce tool missing APNS push notification (spec US2-AC2)
description: Spec User Story 2, Acceptance Criteria 2 says: 'When [agent] calls announce(type: completion), Then iOS sends a push.' The announce tool in status.ts stores the message and broadcasts via WebSocket, but does NOT call sendNotificationToAll from apns-service. Compare with messaging.ts which correctly sends APNS for messages to 'user'. The announce tool should also send APNS push with title=announcement type and body=announcement title.
close_reason: Fixed: announce tool now sends APNS push notifications

title: Fix: messaging resolveAgentFromExtra uses _meta pattern instead of getAgentBySession
description: messaging.ts uses a different agent identity resolution pattern than status.ts. Status tools use getAgentBySession(extra.sessionId) which looks up the connection map in mcp-server.ts. Messaging tools use extra._meta.agentId which depends on a client-supplied _meta field. This is inconsistent and _meta may not be present in all MCP SDK versions. Should use the same getAgentBySession pattern as status tools for consistent, server-side identity resolution.
close_reason: Fixed: messaging uses getAgentBySession instead of _meta

title: Fix deprecated dolt_server_port metadata-pin for bd 1.0.4 (port-file primary; avoid cross-project leakage)
description: RE-SCOPE / LIKELY WON'T-FIX (karax finding 2026-06-06, from the BUG-2 diagnosis adj-182.3.8). Do NOT naively drop the dolt_server_port metadata pin. On bd 1.0.4: (1) the pin IS the externally-managed trigger (IsAutoStartDisabled) — 'bd dolt --help' exposes no alternative attach/external/auto-start flag, the pin is the mechanism; (2) externally-managed mode is EXACTLY the BUG-2 fix — it is what keeps adjutant at 0.2s/call with 0 jsonl re-imports; dropping the pin reverts adjutant to bd-managed = degraded per-call re-import (BUG2) on the proof case; (3) NO deprecation warning is actually emitted on 1.0.4 'bd list' — the cosmetic-warning premise is moot. RECOMMENDATION: keep the pin (load-bearing). Close as won't-fix, OR re-scope to 'standardize the externally-managed pin across ALL projects' which aligns with the BUG-2 fleet rollout (adj-182.3.8). Original intent ('port-file-authoritative, drop deprecated pin') is superseded.

title: doctor port-collision check uses short-id vs UUID — align to path-based resolution (follow-up to 1.4.1)
description: raynor-fix2 flagged: cli/commands/doctor.ts collision check compares allocation projectId (short id from registry p.id) against opts.projectId (beads UUID), so cross-project collision reporting is dead against the real ~/.adjutant/projects.json (same id-space mismatch fixed for allocation in adj-182.1.4.1). Align the collision check to path-based resolution. Non-blocking (allocation works; this is detection-only).

title: Review: supervisorLabel() lockstep duplication (backend service vs cli/lib) enforced only by comment — add a cross-import equality test + drop dead lastGoodPort state
description:
TWO low-severity craft items in adj-182.2.5 / 2.4.

(1) backend/src/services/dolt-supervisor.ts supervisorLabel() is hand-duplicated from cli/lib/dolt-supervisor.ts supervisorLabel() because backend/src cannot import the repo-root cli/ tree (rootDir:./src). Both return `com.adjutant.dolt.${projectId}` today, but the contract ("MUST produce the identical launchd label or the kickstart target will miss") is enforced ONLY by a code comment. If either side later adds projectId sanitization (launchd rejects some chars) they silently diverge and self-heal kickstart misses its target. FIX: add a lockstep test that imports BOTH functions and asserts equality across a sample of projectIds (incl. edge chars). The functions can't be merged across the build boundary, so a contract test is the right guard.

(2) bd-client.ts DoltConnectionState.lastGoodPort is written (line 529) but never read anywhere — dead state. The actual breaker reset is _resetDoltConnectionState() clearing clearedPorts. Remove lastGoodPort (and its doc comment) or wire it into a real decision. Minor cleanup; do alongside the adj-182.2.4.1 fix since both touch doltConnectionState.
close_reason: Fixed both items: (1) removed dead lastGoodPort state from DoltConnectionState (written, never read) — real breaker reset is clearedPorts clearing. (2) Added dolt-supervisor-label-lockstep.test.ts importing BOTH supervisorLabel() impls (backend service + cli/lib) and asserting byte-identical labels across projectIds incl. edge chars — the kickstart-target contract is now test-enforced, not comment-only. Merged to main 357a4859.

title: Review: duplicated dolt production-seam boilerplate across doctor.ts + init.ts (+ 3rd SQL-probe copy in backend service) — extract cli/lib/dolt-seams.ts
description: adj-182.2.2 (cli/commands/doctor.ts) and adj-182.2.3 (cli/commands/init.ts) each define byte-identical realExec(), resolveDoltBin(), realSqlProbe(), and the same installOptions production-seam block (doltBin/plistPath/logPath/uid/exec/pinPort/writePlist/sqlProbe). A THIRD copy of the loopback TCP SQL probe lives in backend/src/services/dolt-supervisor.ts realSqlProbe(). This is copy-paste that will drift (e.g. someone tunes the 1000ms probe timeout in one place only). ARCHITECTURE: the two CLI commands CAN share a new cli/lib/dolt-seams.ts (same build tree) exporting realExec/resolveDoltBin/realSqlProbe + a buildProductionInstallOptions helper. The backend service copy genuinely cannot import cli/ (rootDir:./src build boundary) so it stays separate, but should at least share the probe-timeout constant via documentation. FIX: extract cli/lib/dolt-seams.ts, have doctor.ts and init.ts import from it; add a unit test for the shared seams. Net reduction ~80 LOC and a single source of truth for the install seam contract.

title: Review: doctor --fix realClearCircuitFiles wipes ALL projects' breaker files, not just the repaired port
description: cli/commands/doctor.ts realClearCircuitFiles() deletes every /tmp/beads-dolt-circuit-*.json via the glob regex /^beads-dolt-circuit-.*\.json$/, so a single-project `adjutant doctor --fix` clears the in-process circuit-breaker state of EVERY other beads project/port on the host (confirmed bd 0.60.0 names them per-port: `/tmp/beads-dolt-circuit-%d.json`, and a real box has dozens — e.g. -49820.json, -50760.json, etc.). A healthy concurrent project on another port loses its breaker cooldown state as collateral. Contrast bd-client.ts clearCircuitFileReal() which is correctly PORT-SCOPED. FIX: scope the doctor clear to the repaired project's pinned port (the `port` already resolved in fixDolt) — clear `beads-dolt-circuit-<port>.json` only — or explicitly document the broad clear as an intentional "clean slate" operator action. Add a test asserting only the repaired port's file is removed. Suite: backend/tests/unit/cli-doctor-fix.test.ts (the existing :159 clear test passes a stubbed seam, so the broad-glob blast radius is untested).
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main (9c33c477). Tests GREEN, CLI+backend typecheck clean.

title: Gap: boot entry points startDoltSupervisorFromEnv/OnBoot (the index.ts no-op gate) have no unit tests
description: The 'merging must NOT adopt the supervisor / trigger cutover' guarantee actually depends on startDoltSupervisorFromEnv() and startDoltSupervisorOnBoot() (backend/src/services/dolt-supervisor.ts) short-circuiting on the REAL ADJUTANT_DOLT_SUPERVISOR env BEFORE reading ADJUTANT_DOLT_PROJECT_ID / BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_PORT or building any seam (lines 303-305, 327-329) — that is the function index.ts:269 calls. The code is correct on inspection, but dolt-supervisor-service.test.ts only exercises ensureDoltSupervisor() with a fake isFlagEnabled; the boot wrappers are UNTESTED. A future edit reordering the flag check (e.g. computing the label or reading projectId first) would silently re-enable on-merge work with no failing test. ADD tests (mutate process.env[ADJUTANT_DOLT_SUPERVISOR] in a try/finally): (1) unset/0 -> enabled:false AND no project-id/port env read, no seam build; (2) flag on but projectId missing -> disabled, warns; (3) flag on but port invalid -> disabled, warns. Suite: backend/tests/unit/dolt-supervisor-service.test.ts.
close_reason: Fixed two items. (1) Added boot entry-point no-op tests for startDoltSupervisorFromEnv/OnBoot (the index.ts callers): flag unset/0 -> disabled AND no projectId/port env read (proxy-tracked) -> genuine no-op/no seam build; flag-on-but-missing-projectId / invalid-port -> disabled+warn, never touches launchctl. (2) Upgraded realSqlProbe from a bare TCP connect to a real MySQL-wire handshake check via new pure isMysqlHandshakePacket() (v10 proto byte, seq-0, ERR-packet + absurd-length rejection) so a port-squatter cannot false-pass 'reachable' (adj-182.2.1.r3); dependency-free, socket stays seam-injected. Merged to main db91a5dd.

title: Review (suggestion): DoltProcess.port gathered by realScanDoltProcesses but never used by checkDolt — drop the extra lsof -iTCP
description: Suggestion from review of adj-182.2.1. realScanDoltProcesses() runs a SECOND lsof per pid (`lsof -anP -p <pid> -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN`) purely to fill DoltProcess.port, but checkDolt() keys rogue detection on cwd + pid only and never reads .port. That is one extra subprocess per discovered dolt process for dead data. Either drop the port lookup (and the field) to halve the lsof calls in the scan, or wire .port into a check (e.g. flag a dolt under our data-dir listening on a port != pinned). Pick one; don't leave gathered-but-unused data.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main (9c33c477). Tests GREEN, CLI+backend typecheck clean.

title: Review (suggestion): autocommit insert hardcodes 2-space indent + ignores flow-style behavior: block
description: Suggestions from review of adj-182.2.6 (cli/lib/dolt-pin.ts applyBehaviorAutocommit). Two low-priority robustness gaps vs the template-only happy path (which is well covered by 8 passing tests): (1) When an active block-style `behavior:` exists but has no active autocommit line, the insert path hardcodes `  autocommit: true` (2 spaces). The REWRITE path correctly infers indent from the existing line; the INSERT path should likewise infer child indent from the block's existing children (fall back to 2) so a 4-space hand-edited block doesn't get a mis-indented sibling that misparses. (2) The block-header match `/^behavior:\s*$/` only matches block style; a flow-style active `behavior: {autocommit: false}` is missed and a second `behavior:` block is appended (duplicate key; last-wins happens to be ours so functionally OK but produces a duplicate key). This is asymmetric with the port-pin flow-style listener fix (commit 6762d909). Both are edge cases not seen in the shipped template — fix for consistency/robustness or document as out-of-scope. Add tests for each if fixed.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main (9c33c477). Tests GREEN, CLI+backend typecheck clean.

title: Review (suggestion): TCP-connect probe can't tell a wedged-but-listening Dolt from a healthy one — epic-wide
description: Suggestion from review of adj-182.2.1. The seam is named/documented as a "SQL probe (NOT the PID — #2670)", but realSqlProbe() (doctor.ts:456) and the Phase-1 entrypoint (cli/lib/install-dolt-supervisor-cli.ts:73) both implement it as a bare TCP createConnection() that resolves true on `connect`. A bare port-open is NOT a SQL handshake: a hung/wedged dolt that still holds the listening socket, or any unrelated process bound to the port, reads as "reachable" — a softer recurrence of the #2670 false-up the epic set out to kill. This is consistent across Phase 1 and 2 (2.1 faithfully mirrors the existing seam), so it is an epic-wide design note, not a 2.1 regression. Consider upgrading realSqlProbe to a minimal MySQL-protocol handshake read (read the server greeting packet / do a `SELECT 1`) so "reachable" means "actually serving SQL". Defer if the supervisor's KeepAlive+kickstart already covers wedged-process recovery; capture the decision either way.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main (9c33c477). Tests GREEN, CLI+backend typecheck clean.

title: Bug: applyBehaviorAutocommit ignores flow-style behavior block, emits duplicate behavior key
description:
adj-182.2.6 (cli/lib/dolt-pin.ts applyBehaviorAutocommit) handles only BLOCK-style `behavior:` headers (regex /^behavior:\s*$/). A flow-style `behavior: {autocommit: false}` is not matched, so the function APPENDS a second active `behavior:\n  autocommit: true` block. Result is a config.yaml with TWO active `behavior:` keys:

```
behavior: {autocommit: false}
listener:
  host: 127.0.0.1
  port: 17005

behavior:
  autocommit: true
```

Duplicate YAML keys are parser-undefined; Dolt may honor `autocommit: false` (last/first wins varies), silently defeating the 2.6 fix for any flow-style config. The sibling port-pin code (applyListenerPort) ALREADY defends against flow style (flowIdx branch, lines 96-111) — applyBehaviorAutocommit should have parity.

Severity P3: the SHIPPED Dolt template is block-style+commented (the default path is safe and well-tested), so this bites only operator-hand-edited or future flow-style configs. But it is a real correctness asymmetry the team explicitly guarded against on the port path.

REPRO (verified live, temp .beads dir): seed config.yaml=`behavior: {autocommit: false}\nlistener:\n  host: 127.0.0.1\n  port: 49599\n`, call pinDoltPort(beadsDir,17005) -> two behavior keys. The existing dolt-autocommit.test.ts helper activeBehaviorKeys only counts block-style headers (/^behavior:\s*$/) so it returns 1 and MASKS the dup — add a flow-style regression test.

SECONDARY (note, not separate bead): a malformed input with a COMMENTED `# behavior:` header followed by an indented active `autocommit: false` orphan leaves TWO active autocommit lines after the append. This is garbage-in (invalid YAML) and the real template never produces it, so lower concern — but a defensive guard or a comment documenting the assumption would harden it.

FIX: add a flow-style branch to applyBehaviorAutocommit mirroring applyListenerPort's flowIdx handling (edit autocommit inside the braces, inject if absent), plus a flow-style regression test and a helper that counts BOTH flow and block behavior keys.
close_reason: dup of adj-182.2.6.r1 (flow-style behavior block) — fixed there

title: Coverage gap: dolt-pin emits duplicate listener block for flow-style listener: {..}
description: applyListenerPort()'s listener detector regex is /^listener:\s*$/, which only matches a block-style header on its own line. A flow-style 'listener: {host: 127.0.0.1, port: 49599}' is NOT matched, so a SECOND 'listener:' block is appended. YAML with two top-level 'listener:' keys is ambiguous/non-strict-valid (last-wins in most parsers, so it functionally pins, but leaves a stale conflicting block). Real beads templates use block style so severity is low, but tests cover neither flow-style nor the no-listener-append-then-re-pin (idempotency) case. Add tests: flow-style listener, and re-pin after an append must not stack multiple appended blocks.
close_reason: Fixed: flow-style listener detected and edited in place (no duplicate block); idempotent. Regression tests RED->GREEN. Merged f26444a7.

title: Coverage gap: bd-doctor supervised-PID derived from stale pidfile can mis-trust a rogue
description: Under externally-managed mode bd-doctor derives SUPERVISED_PID from .beads/dolt-server.pid when BD_DOCTOR_SUPERVISED_PID is unset (script lines ~135-138). If launchd is NOT actually loaded and the pidfile is stale, and a rogue dolt happens to reuse that exact PID, the rogue is classified as the legitimate supervised server (NOT killed, reported 'alive'). Reproduced: metadata externally-managed, pidfile=88888, override '88888 33333 <beadsDir>' with no SUPERVISED_PID seam -> 'Adjutant dolt server is alive: pid=88888'. Spec US2 intent is to probe the supervised PID from the launchd agent (launchctl print) rather than trust the pidfile. This is acceptable for 1.5's documented pidfile-derivation scope but should be hardened in Phase 2 checkDolt(); flagging as a coverage/robustness gap with a test for stale-pidfile/PID-reuse.
close_reason: Re-scoped to adj-182.2.7 (harden in Phase 2 checkDolt per QA recommendation)

title: Review: bd-doctor must guard empty PROJECT_ID before building launchctl kickstart label
description: scripts/bd-doctor.sh: when metadata.json has dolt_server_port but is MISSING project_id, EXTERNALLY_MANAGED=1 (line 96-100) yet PROJECT_ID stays empty. The --restart kickstart path (line 231) then builds 'launchctl kickstart -k gui/<uid>/com.adjutant.dolt.' with a trailing dot and no id, which fails at runtime with a confusing launchctl error instead of a clear diagnosis. Verified empirically. Fix: if EXTERNALLY_MANAGED=1 but PROJECT_ID is empty, print a clear red error ('externally-managed but metadata.json has no project_id — cannot derive supervisor label; re-pin or fix metadata') and exit non-zero BEFORE attempting kickstart. Add a regression test (metadata with dolt_server_port but no project_id + --restart -> clear error, no launchctl invocation). Low likelihood (live metadata always carries project_id) — defensive hardening aligned with the epic's malformed-config focus.
close_reason: Fixed: empty PROJECT_ID guard before launchctl kickstart; clear error + non-zero exit. Regression test RED->GREEN. Merged to main 8f69c49f.

title: UX: no keyboard navigation between question rows — tab/arrow flow not implemented
description: The spec (US3) states 'Keyboard-navigable'. The current implementation has no keyboard navigation scheme for moving between question rows. Each QuestionRow is a div (not a focusable element with tabIndex), so keyboard users can only tab through every interactive element sequentially — textarea, submit, dismiss, option buttons — with no way to jump between questions. For a triage session with many questions, the General needs arrow-key or j/k navigation to move row-to-row. Fix: (a) add tabIndex={0} to each QuestionRow div with an onKeyDown handler for ArrowUp/ArrowDown/j/k to move focus between rows, and (b) add an 'Enter to expand context' affordance when a row is focused. This is consistent with the terminal aesthetic (vim-like navigation). File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx — QuestionRow + OpenQuestionsView list rendering.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: category chip uses cyan (#00ccff) — off-palette, inconsistent with Pip-Boy green theme
description: The categoryChip style uses color: '#00ccff' and border: '1px solid #00ccff' — a bright cyan that is not in the Pip-Boy color palette (--pipboy-green, --pipboy-green-dim, --pipboy-bg, --pipboy-bg-panel per rules/05-ui-theme.md). Cyan is used nowhere else in the theme. This creates a visually jarring element in an otherwise monochrome-green terminal aesthetic. The intent may have been to differentiate category from urgency, which is valid, but the mechanism breaks theme cohesion. Fix: use a consistent in-palette approach for differentiation — for example use --pipboy-green-dim for the chip text/border, or reserve a secondary accent color that is explicitly part of the theme design system. If an accent color is needed, add it as a CSS variable to the theme palette rather than hardcoding. File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx — categoryChip style.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: timeAgo color (#555) fails contrast against dark bg — nearly invisible on Pip-Boy theme
description: The timeAgo style uses color: '#555' (a mid-grey) against the --pipboy-bg-panel (#111111) background. This is approximately 2.5:1 contrast ratio, well below WCAG AA minimum of 4.5:1 for small text (10px). In a dark room or on a dim display this timestamp is essentially invisible. The rest of the row uses --pipboy-green-dim (#00aa00) for de-emphasized text which is still readable. Fix: change timeAgo color to --pipboy-green-dim (#00aa00) or at minimum a lighter grey (#888) that achieves acceptable contrast on the dark panel background. File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx — timeAgo style and dismissBtn color (also #555).
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: context block collapsed by default — agent's key framing buried under a click
description: The spec intent for 'context' is that it is 'rich framing authored by the agent (what it's doing, what it tried, the tradeoff / what it needs done) so the General can answer or act fast and accurately'. Collapsing it by default means the General must click [+] CONTEXT on every question before they can meaningfully answer. For a triage session with 10 questions, that's 10 extra clicks, and the framing that enables a fast, accurate answer is hidden until requested. Fix: for high/blocking urgency questions, default contextExpanded to true (the context is precisely most critical here). For normal/low, the collapsed default is fine. File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx — QuestionRow initial state for contextExpanded.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: project field missing from question row — multi-project Generals cannot identify origin
description: The spec (US3) says each row shows 'asking agent, project, urgency, age, body'. The AgentQuestion type includes projectId. However the rendered QuestionRow only shows agentId, urgency badge, category chip, and timeAgo — there is no project field rendered. In a multi-project Adjutant setup the General may have questions from agents on different projects and needs context to answer correctly. Fix: render question.projectId (or a resolved projectName if available) in the row header meta. Even a short projectId prefix disambiguates. File: frontend/src/components/questions/OpenQuestionsView.tsx — rowMeta in QuestionRow.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: UX: QUESTIONS tab has no unread badge — General gets no ambient signal of pending questions
description: Every other time-sensitive tab (CHAT) has a ChatBadge component that shows an unread count in the nav tab, giving ambient awareness without navigating to it. The QUESTIONS tab renders as a bare text label with no count/badge. The General must proactively click the tab to discover there are 3 blocking questions waiting. The APNS push handles mobile, but on web there is no ambient indicator. Fix: add a QuestionsBadge component (mirroring ChatBadge) that shows the count of open questions, and especially a distinct 'blocking' indicator (e.g. red dot or '!' prefix) when any question has urgency=blocking. Subscribe to question:new/answered/dismissed WS events or poll GET /api/questions?status=open. File: frontend/src/App.tsx + new frontend/src/components/questions/QuestionsBadge.tsx.
close_reason: Fixed + merged to main; frontend suite green

title: Recover ~926 stranded pre-migration DM messages (reversible backfill) — deferred from adj-pgwa4
description:
DEFERRED P3 (General's call): the live DM view is restored by the iOS NUL-byte fix (c36b2d76) + the cross-platform contract test (8d6552e4). No recent messages are stranded; the ~926 below are all OLDER/pre-migration. No live-data write was performed. This bead captures everything needed for a one-command future restore.

## What's stranded (backend conversation_id NULL or thread-id orphan → invisible to all clients)
From ~/.adjutant/adjutant.db triage (2026-05-30):
- 541 rows with NULL/empty conversation_id:
  - 37 agent rows where recipient == agent_id (self) → belong in dm(user, agent)
  - 497 agent-to-agent rows (e.g. coordinator↔raynor) → NOT user DMs; would map to dm(agentA, agentB) if desired
  - 7 user rows: 5 bare (recipient='user', no thread, ambiguous target — unrecoverable) + 2 proposal-thread rows (not DMs)
- 923 rows with conversation_id == thread_id (adj-hq8p4 earlier backfill keyed on thread_id), all orphan ids:
  - 882 self-recipient + 7 mayor agent rows → belong in dm(user, agent)
  - 34 user rows in proposal threads (not DMs)
- Net recoverable into the user's DM view: ~926 (37 + 882 + 7). Earliest ~2026-02-21.

## Backup (full consistent snapshot, taken before any work)
~/.adjutant/adjutant.db.bak-adj-repair-20260530-144803  (19M, sqlite .backup incl. WAL)

## Resolver fix (apply to backend/src/services/conversation-backfill.ts resolveAgentCounterpart — backward-compatible, backend suite stays 16/16 green). Adds the self-recipient case:
    if (row.role === "agent" || row.role === "announcement" || row.role === "system") {
      if (
        row.agent_id &&
        row.agent_id !== "user" &&
        (row.recipient === "user" || row.recipient === "mayor/" || row.recipient === row.agent_id)
      ) {
        return row.agent_id;
      }
      return null;
    }
(Null/empty recipient stays a broadcast → unscoped; agent-to-agent left null unless we choose dm(agentA,agentB).)

## Repair procedure (reversible)
1. Backup live DB again (sqlite .backup).
2. Clear the 923 thread-id orphans: for rows where conversation_id == thread_id AND conversation_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM conversations using dm_/chan) AND message_id NOT IN conversation_backfill_log → record (message_id, old conversation_id) into a conversation_repair_log, then set conversation_id = NULL.
3. Apply the resolver fix above; re-run backfillConversations(db) (idempotent; only touches NULL rows; logs to conversation_backfill_log).
4. Verify: getMessages({conversationId: dmConversationId('user', <agent>)}) shows the recovered older history; spot-check Feb-21 messages appear.

## Reverse (one-command restore)
- reverseBackfill(db) undoes the re-run (uses conversation_backfill_log), and restore conversation_id from conversation_repair_log for the cleared orphans. OR hard-restore from the backup file above.

## TDD already in place
- Resolver self-recipient behavior + thread-id orphan clearing should be unit-tested in conversation-backfill.test.ts with REAL row shapes (adj-067) before any live run. Existing 11 backfill tests pass; the resolver change keeps them green.

Note: 497 agent-to-agent + ~41 proposal/ambiguous rows are intentionally NOT user-DM content; only the ~926 are in scope for the user's DM view.

title: Fix keystroke-latency perf harness selector for reworked CommandChat (target message input, not search)
description: adj-164.7.5: the gated Puppeteer keystroke-latency test (tests/perf/keystroke-latency.test.ts) times out against the reworked CommandChat. Root cause: it grabs the FIRST input[type=text] which is now the search bar (and the coordinator-DM default view adds a load path). It should target the message input explicitly (.chat-input) and wait for that element's value. formatter-cache + leak-overview budgets PASS; this is a test-harness maintenance gap, not a measured latency regression (keystroke path + adj-139 virtualization unchanged, 682 frontend unit tests green). Verified manually 2026-05-29.

title: iOS: make AdjutantUI macOS-compilable so chat ViewModels are swift-testable via SPM (extract ViewModels to platform-agnostic target)
description:
Follow-up from adj-164.7.7. The `AdjutantUI` SPM target does NOT compile on macOS (~300 errors across 16 View-layer files using iOS-only SwiftUI/UIKit APIs: navigationBarLeading/navigationBarTrailing/topBarTrailing, navigationBarTitleDisplayMode, fullScreenCover, UIResponder, UIApplication, UIPasteboard, UIImpactFeedbackGenerator). Files: CRTBackButton, CRTTextField, ChatView, ChatBubble, MainTabView, AppCoordinator, AgentDetailView, AgentPickerView, BeadDetailView, EpicDetailView, ProposalDetailView, UnifiedChatView, PersonaDetailSheet, CallsignPickerView, SwarmProjectDetailView, BackgroundTaskService. Because the AdjutantTests target depends on AdjutantUI, chat ViewModel tests can only run via xcodebuild (iOS sim), not `cd ios && swift test`. This is why selendis wired the chat tests into project.pbxproj (adj-164.3 build-file IDs C101-C106) — that pbxproj entry is the ONLY working test path and must remain until this is fixed.

Two viable approaches:
(A) Guard the 16 View files with #if os(iOS) (or #if canImport(UIKit)) around iOS-only toolbar/cover/feedback APIs so AdjutantUI compiles on macOS, then the AdjutantTests target builds under swift test. Lower-effort but spreads platform conditionals through the View layer and risks the iOS app build; needs an iOS-sim regression pass.
(B) PREFERRED (separation of concerns): extract the testable chat ViewModels (ChatViewModel, ChannelViewModel, and their pure dependencies) out of the iOS-only AdjutantUI target into a platform-agnostic SPM library (AdjutantKit or a new AdjutantCore). Then ViewModels are swift-testable on macOS without the View layer, the pbxproj test wiring can be reverted (restoring SPM auto-discovery / MEMORY rule), and `cd ios && swift test` works. Largest change but the correct long-term architecture — testable logic should not live in an iOS-only UI target.

DONE in adj-164.7.7: ChatViewModel's `import UIKit` + UIApplication.didBecomeActiveNotification observer are already guarded with #if canImport(UIKit), so that file already compiles on macOS — a head start for either approach.

Acceptance: `cd ios && swift test` builds + runs the chat ViewModel tests on macOS; pbxproj test-target entries C101-C106 reverted (SPM auto-discovery restored); iOS app still builds + the chat tests still pass via xcodebuild; CI gates the tests.

title: QA: WS membership gate relies on client-supplied identity + REST channel senderId is unauthenticated (defense-in-depth)
description:
EPIC adj-164. WARNING (defense-in-depth; v1 channels are public per spec section 3 so not a v1 blocker, but the boundary is weaker than the comments claim).

1) WS identity spoofing: handleAuth (backend/src/services/ws-server.ts:215-217) sets client.identity directly from msg.identity with NO binding to the validated API key. A client holding any valid (shared) API key can declare identity='<any channel member>' and then receive that member's live room fan-out (wsBroadcastToConversation gates only on memberIds.has(client.identity)). The membership gate is therefore only as strong as the honesty of the self-declared identity.

2) REST channel post senderId: POST /api/channels/:id/messages (backend/src/routes/channels.ts:104) takes senderId from the request body unauthenticated; any caller can post as any member (membership is checked against the supplied senderId, but the caller chooses it).

3) REST channel/conversation reads are unauthorized: GET /api/conversations/:id/messages (routes/conversations.ts:53-83) and channel message reads do NOT verify the requester is a member — any caller who knows a conversation id can read full history. The route comment claims store-scoping is 'the root-cause fix for bleed', conflating scoping correctness with authorization.

These are acceptable for v1 (public channels, single-operator dashboard) but should be tracked so the future private-channel/ACL work (explicitly out of v1 scope) closes them. At minimum, bind WS identity to the authenticated principal and derive REST senderId from auth rather than the request body.

FILES: ws-server.ts:215-217,788-800; routes/channels.ts:104; routes/conversations.ts:53-83.

title: Review: AGENTS/coordinator chat view (undefined agentId) now always empty
description:
adj-164 code review (mohandar). frontend/src/hooks/useChatMessages.ts lines 73-82 + components/chat/CommandChat.tsx.

When agentId is undefined, useChatMessages short-circuits: setConversationId(null), setMessages([]) and never calls getDm — so the view is permanently empty and the WS subscription drops everything (incoming guard: if (!openConversationId) return). CommandChat still renders a "AGENTS DIRECT LINE" header and "Send a message to AGENTS" empty hint, and handleSend posts to to: 'user' (default), which lands in dmConversationId("user","user") — never displayed.

Pre-epic, the coordinator/AGENTS view aggregated coordinator messages. This is a behavioral regression with no replacement conversation. Confirmed intentional-but-unreplaced by tests/unit/hooks/useChatMessages.test.ts:316 ("should drop all WS messages when no agentId is set").

Decide product intent: either route the no-agent view to a dedicated coordinator conversation id, or remove/relabel the AGENTS view so it isn't a dead surface that silently swallows sends.
close_reason: Undefined agentId maps to adjutant-coordinator DM (no dead empty view); labeled COORDINATOR. 4 tests. By lasarra.

title: Review: useMobileAudio.play() rejection path leaves audio.src attached
close_reason: Fixed alongside adj-fwywd: play()-rejection path now clears audio.src.

title: Review: defensive Array.isArray check on sync_response.missed
description:
frontend/src/contexts/CommunicationContext.tsx:384 — 'const missed = msg.missed ?? [];' then 'for (const entry of missed)'. If the server (or a malicious/buggy proxy) emits a sync_response where missed is not an array (e.g., '{}', 42, 'string'), the for-of loop will either throw a TypeError or iterate unintended values. The error would propagate up through ws.onmessage and is not caught — leaving the WS handler in an inconsistent state.

Suggested fix:
  const missed = Array.isArray(msg.missed) ? msg.missed : [];

Add a test case in frontend/tests/unit/CommunicationContext.sync.test.tsx that injects 'missed: 42' or 'missed: {}' and asserts the hook does not throw and delivers no messages.

title: Update CLAUDE.md and memory with new patterns
description: Update project identity docs to reflect removal of active project concept.

title: Grep audit for remaining active-project references
description: rg audit for activeProject, getActiveProjectName, activateProject, .active across entire codebase.

title: End-to-end verification across web and iOS
description: Start server, test overview, beads, proposals, project switching on web and iOS.

title: Update CLAUDE.md and memory with new patterns
description: Update project identity docs to reflect removal of active project concept. Update memory entries.

title: Grep audit for remaining active-project references
description: rg audit for activeProject, getActiveProjectName, activateProject, .active across entire codebase. Fix any remaining references.

title: End-to-end verification across web and iOS
description: Start server, test overview with projectId param, beads, proposals, project switching on web. Test iOS JSON decoding with and without active field.

title: Polish: Cleanup & verification
description: Final audit, verification, and documentation updates.

title: Bug: doctor does not detect outdated quality files (only checks existence)
description: checkQualityFiles only checks if files exist (pass/fail). Per spec US4.3, doctor should also compare content against templates and report 'warn' with 'outdated — run adjutant upgrade' for files that exist but differ from the template.

title: Add architecture rule to 04-architecture.md
description: Document project identity standard: projectId=UUID for backend/API, projectName=display only, projectPath=CWD+beads only.

title: Review: Dynamic SQL in auto-develop-store updateCycle is safe but should use prepared statements
description: In auto-develop-store.ts line 143, updateCycle() builds a dynamic UPDATE query by joining setClauses. While the column names are hardcoded (not user-supplied) and all values use parameterized placeholders, this pattern is fragile and does not benefit from prepared statement caching. All other queries in the store use pre-prepared statements. Consider refactoring to either: (a) always update all columns (simpler), or (b) use a fixed prepared statement with COALESCE/CASE for optional updates.

title: Test gap: No FK constraint test for auto_develop_cycles table
description:
Migration 027 creates auto_develop_cycles with FOREIGN KEY (project_id) REFERENCES projects(id), but no test verifies that inserting a cycle with a nonexistent project_id fails. SQLite FK enforcement depends on PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON. If the database is not configured with foreign keys enabled, orphaned cycles could be created silently. The auto-develop-store.test.ts inserts a test project via insertTestProject() to satisfy the FK, but never tests the negative case.

Fix: Add a test that attempts startCycle() with a nonexistent project ID and verifies it throws a constraint error. Also verify PRAGMA foreign_keys is ON in the test database setup.

title: Test gap: Missing enable_auto_develop failure path test
Missing: No per-signal clamping in computeConfidenceScore
description:
In backend/src/services/confidence-engine.ts, computeConfidenceScore() only clamps the final weighted sum to 0-100, but does not validate or clamp individual signal values. While the Zod schema at the MCP boundary enforces 0-100, internal callers could pass signals > 100 or < 0, producing incorrect weighted sums before the final clamp. For example, a signal of 200 for reviewerConsensus (weight 0.30) adds 60 to the sum instead of the maximum expected 30.

The test at line 75-85 verifies negative inputs clamp to 0, but doesn't test that signals > 100 produce a capped result.

Fix: Clamp each signal to 0-100 before multiplication: `Math.min(100, Math.max(0, signals[key]))`. Add a test for signals > 100.

title: Missing test: two recurring schedules firing within 90s cooldown
description: The spec edge case states: "Two schedules fire within cooldown — Queued like any other wake; latest wins." The cooldown queue logic exists in the engine but there is no test specifically verifying that when two recurring schedule timers fire within the 90s cooldown window, only the latest reason is delivered. Add a test with two schedules whose nextFireAt values are within 90s of each other.

title: Missing test: CronScheduleStore.update() with empty fields
description: CronScheduleStore.update() returns false when called with an empty fields object (setClauses.length === 0). This edge case has no test coverage. Should add a test verifying update(id, {}) returns false and does not modify the row.

title: Review: update() returns false ambiguously for both empty fields and missing row
Review: recurring test uses imprecise assertion (toBeGreaterThanOrEqual) for re-fire count
Review: CronScheduleStore.update() uses dynamic db.prepare on every call instead of pre-compiled stmt
Bug: CI runs lint twice — build scripts include lint, then separate lint steps run again
description:
The CI pipeline (ci.yml) runs: Build backend → Build frontend → Lint backend → Lint frontend → Tests → Coverage.

Both build scripts already include lint: backend `"build": "npm run lint && tsc"`, frontend `"build": "npm run lint && vite build"`. So lint runs in the build step, then again in the dedicated lint step — doubling the lint time for no benefit.

Options: (a) Remove lint from build scripts (risky — local `npm run build` would no longer lint), or (b) remove the separate lint steps from CI (since build already covers it), or (c) accept the redundancy as defense-in-depth.
close_reason: Deferred — minor CI inefficiency, not blocking

title: [QA] CASCADE delete on canvas does not clean up orphaned assets
description:
When a canvas is deleted, CASCADE deletes propagate correctly through canvases -> canvas_layers -> layer_versions and canvases -> global_passes. However, layer_versions.asset_id uses ON DELETE SET NULL (migration line 38), meaning the assets table retains orphaned asset records (and their files on disk) after canvas deletion.

Similarly, global_passes.source_asset_id and result_asset_id use ON DELETE SET NULL (lines 57-58). Deleting a canvas leaves behind all generated image assets with no parent reference, consuming storage indefinitely.

Files: backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js:38, 57-58
close_reason: Deferred: CASCADE delete orphaned assets is a broader concern, not specific to canvas

title: [QA] Backend tests don't verify credit deduction or insufficient credits for element/mutate/pass
description:
The backend generation tests (canvases-generation.test.js) seed a credit account with balance=1000 and never test:

1. What happens when credits are insufficient (no 402 response test for generate-element, mutate, or global pass)
2. Whether credits are actually deducted after generation (they aren't — see separate bug)
3. Race condition where multiple requests exhaust credits simultaneously

The CRUD tests (canvases.test.js) test the 10-layer cap but don't test:
1. Reorder endpoint (doesn't exist)
2. Update layer with invalid blend mode
3. Concurrent layer addition race condition

Files: backend/tests/routes/canvases-generation.test.js, backend/tests/routes/canvases.test.js

title: [QA] Missing index on layer_versions(layer_id, is_head) for head version lookups
description:
The getCanvas function queries `layer_versions WHERE layer_id = ? AND is_head = true` for every layer (backend/src/services/canvas.js:25-27). The migration creates an index on layer_id alone (006_canvases.js:46) but not a composite index on (layer_id, is_head). This query pattern is used on every canvas load and export, and without the composite index it will be slow as version counts grow.

Similarly, exportCanvas queries `layer_versions WHERE layer_id = ? AND is_head = true` repeatedly (lines 374-376, 403-405).

Files: backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js:46 (missing composite index), backend/src/services/canvas.js:25-27
close_reason: Fixed: added composite index on layer_versions(layer_id, is_head)

title: [QA] Missing mutation_type CHECK constraint on layer_versions table
description:
The migration (backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js) adds CHECK constraints for canvas_layers.layer_type, canvas_layers.blend_mode, and global_passes.pass_type, but there is NO CHECK constraint on layer_versions.mutation_type. This column accepts any string value, which could lead to invalid data.

The route schema validates mutation_type with enum ['restyle', 'relight', 'detail', 'transform', 'remove'] (canvases.js:398), but the DB should also enforce this as defense in depth (allowing NULL for initial versions).

Files: backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js (missing constraint), backend/src/routes/canvases.js:398
close_reason: Fixed: added mutation_type CHECK constraint to migration

title: [QA] removeLayer does not re-index remaining layers' order_index
description:
When a layer is deleted via removeLayer (backend/src/services/canvas.js:87-90), only the row is deleted. The remaining layers keep their original order_index values, which may now have gaps (e.g., 0, 2, 3 after deleting index 1). While this won't crash anything, the reorderLayers function expects contiguous indices (0, 1, 2, ...), and the gap could cause confusion or ordering bugs if new layers are added (they always get max+1).

The iOS reducer handles deletion by reloading the full canvas (CanvasReducer.swift:541-549), which is correct, but the backend data will have non-contiguous order indices.

Files: backend/src/services/canvas.js:87-90
close_reason: Fixed: removeLayer now re-indexes remaining layers after deletion

title: [QA] No DB constraint enforcing 10-layer cap (application-only check)
description:
The 10-layer cap (FR-011) is enforced only in application code in addLayer (backend/src/services/canvas.js:50-53). There is no database CHECK constraint or trigger to prevent exceeding 10 layers. This means:

1. Direct DB access or other code paths could bypass the cap.
2. The selectBaseCandidate function (line 176-181) calls addLayer which does check, but concurrent requests could race past the count check since there's no row-level locking or serializable transaction.

Two concurrent addLayer calls when count=9 could both read count=9 and both insert, resulting in 11 layers.

Files: backend/src/services/canvas.js:50-53, backend/src/db/migrations/006_canvases.js (no constraint)
close_reason: Documented: 10-layer cap intentionally in app code, DB trigger would be complex

title: Add agent role/specialization section to PRIME.md
description: Agents currently have no formal role identity — they describe themselves as 'general-purpose engineering agent.' Add a section to PRIME.md where agents can discover their specialization (if any) from their agent profile or memories. E.g.: 'Check your memories and agent profile for role specialization. If none defined, you are a general-purpose engineering agent. Report your role in your first status update.' This is lightweight — does NOT require backend changes, just protocol awareness.

title: Rename 'Workbook' to 'Beads' on iOS beads page
description: The top-left title on the beads page in the iOS app says 'Workbook'. Change it to 'Beads'.

title: Review: hasBeadsDb() called synchronously per row in listProjects
description: rowToProject() calls hasBeadsDb(row.path) which does up to 3 synchronous existsSync() calls per row. listProjects() maps all rows through rowToProject, so for N projects this is O(3N) blocking filesystem checks on the event loop. With 20+ registered projects this causes measurable latency on the GET /api/projects response. Consider: (1) making listProjects async and using fs.promises.access in parallel, (2) computing hasBeads lazily only when the field is accessed, or (3) caching the result with a short TTL since .beads/ directories don't appear/disappear frequently.

title: Review: Dead sessions column in projects table — always empty
description: The `sessions` column in the projects table (migration 021) stores a JSON array that is always written as `'[]'` and never updated with real data. The Project interface has `sessions: string[]` but every code path sets it to `[]`. Meanwhile, migration 022 created a proper `managed_sessions` table with `project_path` for the actual session-to-project relationship. The `sessions` column, the `JSON.parse` in rowToProject, and the `sessions` field on Project are all dead code carrying forward the legacy JSON structure. Consider removing the column in a follow-up migration or documenting it as deprecated if Phase 3 (session-registry migration) will address it.

title: [Product] iOS PromptHistory model uses camelCase but backend returns snake_case — decoding mismatch risk
description:
**UX concern / potential bug**: The iOS `PromptHistory` model uses camelCase properties (`originalPrompt`, `enhancedPrompt`, `stylePresetId`, `assetId`, `createdAt`) and relies on `JSONDecoder.keyDecodingStrategy = .convertFromSnakeCase` in the APIClient. The backend `promptHistorySchema` returns snake_case (`original_prompt`, `enhanced_prompt`, etc.).

This SHOULD work because the APIClient uses `.convertFromSnakeCase`. However, the backend response schema validation allows these fields through as snake_case, and the iOS model expects camelCase conversion. There's a subtle risk: if any field has multiple underscores or non-standard naming (like `thumbnail_url`), the automatic conversion can silently fail.

Verified: The current fields all follow standard single-underscore patterns, so this works. But the `StylePreset` model has `promptModifier` while the backend column is `prompt_modifier` and the POST body expects `promptModifier` (camelCase). The backend response returns `prompt_modifier` (snake_case from the DB). This creates a mismatch: the create request sends camelCase but the response returns snake_case. The APIClient encoder uses `.convertToSnakeCase` for requests but the decoder uses `.convertFromSnakeCase` for responses, so this should work symmetrically.

**Status**: No bug currently, but this is fragile. Recommend adding explicit `CodingKeys` to both Swift models to make the mapping explicit rather than relying on automatic conversion.

title: [Product] Style presets missing "Social Media" and "3D Render" categories — gaps for content creators
description:
**Enhancement**: The 32 curated presets cover 6 categories (cinematic, photography, illustration, digital-art, retro, abstract) and are generally well-crafted with effective prompt modifiers. However, for content creators specifically, two significant gaps exist:

1. **Social Media / Content Creator** category — Missing styles like:
   - "YouTube Thumbnail" (bold text-ready, high saturation, dramatic expressions)
   - "Instagram Flat Lay" (top-down, organized arrangement, clean background)
   - "TikTok Hook Frame" (attention-grabbing, mobile-optimized vertical, text overlay ready)
   - "Podcast Cover Art" (portrait-focused, clean background for text overlay)
   These are the styles content creators actually need most.

2. **3D Render** category — Missing styles like:
   - "Isometric 3D" (common for tech content, product mockups)
   - "Claymation" (trending style for social content)
   - "Unreal Engine Photorealistic" (game/tech content)

The current presets lean heavily toward artistic/photography — they're beautiful but underserve the "content creator making things for social platforms" persona that C4 targets.

**Fix**: Add 6-8 social media/content creator presets and 3-4 3D render presets in a future seed migration.

title: [Bug] iOS StylePreset model missing userId field — cannot track custom preset ownership
description:
File: `ios/C4/Packages/CoreKit/Sources/CoreKit/Models/StylePreset.swift` (on branch `003-ai-creative-director-foundation`)

The iOS `StylePreset` model does not include a `userId` property, but the backend response includes `user_id` in the JSON. While Swift's `Codable` silently ignores extra keys during decoding (so this won't crash), it means:

1. The iOS app cannot determine who owns a custom preset
2. The app cannot filter "my custom presets" vs "other users' custom presets"  
3. Any future UI that shows ownership info will lack the data

The backend response schema explicitly includes `user_id`:
```js
user_id: { type: ['string', 'null'] },
```

**Fix:** Add `public let userId: String?` to the StylePreset struct and its init.

title: [Bug] Style preset delete/update has TOCTOU race — no transaction wrapping
[Bug] No maxLength on prompt_modifier in POST/PUT style routes — unbounded text input
description:
File: `backend/src/routes/styles.js` (on branch `003-ai-creative-director-foundation`)

The POST and PUT body schemas for `promptModifier` only specify `minLength: 1` but no `maxLength`. The database column is `TEXT` (unbounded). A client could submit an extremely long prompt modifier (megabytes), which would:

1. Consume excessive database storage
2. Cause issues when the modifier is appended to generation prompts (exceeding LLM token limits)
3. Potentially cause memory issues when loading many presets

Other text fields in the same schema have limits: `name` has `maxLength: 100`, `description` has `maxLength: 2000`, `category` has `maxLength: 50`.

**Fix:** Add `maxLength: 4000` (matching the existing prompt limit in generate.js) to the `promptModifier` property in both POST and PUT body schemas.

title: [Bug] POST /api/styles never sets user_id — custom presets have no ownership
description:
File: `backend/src/routes/styles.js` (on branch `003-ai-creative-director-foundation`)

The create custom style handler inserts with `is_custom: true` but never sets `user_id`. The column exists in the migration and schema but is never populated. This means:

1. Custom presets have no owner — any user can edit or delete any custom preset
2. The GET /api/styles list query filters `is_custom = false OR user_id IS NULL`, so custom presets with null user_id are always visible to everyone
3. PUT and DELETE only check `is_custom` flag, not ownership

While there's no auth system yet, this is a design gap that will cause authorization issues when auth is added. The user_id should at minimum be accepted in the request or derived from auth context.

**Fix:** Either:
- Accept user_id in the POST body and set it on insert
- Add a TODO/comment documenting this as an auth-dependent feature
- Add ownership checks to PUT/DELETE when user_id is present

title: [Bug] LIKE wildcard characters not escaped in prompt history search
description:
File: `backend/src/routes/prompts.js` (on branch `003-ai-creative-director-foundation`)

The search handler builds the ILIKE pattern as:
```js
this.where('original_prompt', 'ilike', `%${search}%`)
  .orWhere('enhanced_prompt', 'ilike', `%${search}%`);
```

While Knex parameterizes the value (safe from SQL injection), the LIKE metacharacters `%` and `_` within user input are not escaped. A user searching for `%` or `_` would match far more rows than intended, and crafted patterns like `%_%_%_%` could cause performance issues on large tables.

**Fix:** Escape LIKE metacharacters before interpolation:
```js
const escaped = search.replace(/[%_\\]/g, '\\$&');
// then use escaped in the ilike pattern
```

title: [Bug] Missing index on prompt_history.created_at — full table scan on every list query
description:
File: `backend/src/db/migrations/003_creative_director.js` (on branch `003-ai-creative-director-foundation`)

The `prompt_history` table is always queried with `.orderBy('created_at', 'desc')` in the list history endpoint (prompts.js), but there is no index on `created_at`. The migration only indexes `style_preset_id` and `asset_id`.

This will cause a full table scan + filesort on every paginated list request, degrading as history grows.

**Fix:** Add `t.index('created_at');` to the `prompt_history` table creation in the migration.

title: Bug: iOS Storyboard model missing metadata field present in a3e3efc6 migration
description:
The a3e3efc6 migration includes a `metadata` JSONB column on the storyboards table, but the iOS Storyboard.swift model has no `metadata` property. If the a3e3efc6 migration schema is adopted, the backend will return a `metadata` field that iOS will silently ignore (since Codable skips unknown keys by default). However, if any code path tries to read/write metadata on the iOS side, it won't be available.

This is lower priority since the a5b2e2e9 migration (which matches the routes) doesn't include metadata on the storyboards table. But if the migrations are reconciled to include metadata, the iOS model needs updating.

Files: ios/C4/Packages/CoreKit/Sources/CoreKit/Models/Storyboard.swift

title: Bug: Assembly service has no validation for empty clips array
description:
The assembleVideo function in assembly.js does not check if the clips array is empty. If called with an empty array, it will create a Creatomate render with no elements, which will either fail with an unhelpful error or produce a zero-length video.

The assemble route does validate minItems: 1 on clipAssetIds, but if assembleVideo is called from a storyboard assembly flow (future C4-002 feature), the empty-array case may not be guarded.

Files: backend/src/services/assembly.js (assembleVideo function)

title: Bug: No token/length limit enforcement before sending script to LLM
description:
The split endpoint allows script_text up to 50,000 characters (maxLength in JSON schema). A 50K character script could be ~12,000+ tokens. Combined with the system prompt and the expected JSON response, this could exceed the context window of gpt-4o-mini (128K tokens for input but the response may be very long with many scenes).

There's no check on estimated token count before calling the LLM, and no handling for token limit errors specifically. Very long scripts will either produce truncated JSON responses (causing parse failures) or hit API rate/cost limits.

Consider: (1) warning when script exceeds ~20K characters, (2) handling OpenAI context_length_exceeded errors gracefully, (3) splitting very long scripts into chunks.

Files: backend/src/services/scene-splitter.js, backend/src/routes/storyboards.js

title: Bug: Storyboard update allows setting arbitrary fields via spread — potential for column injection
description:
In the PUT /api/storyboards/:id handler, the update object is built by spreading the entire request body:

    const updates = { ...request.body, updated_at: fastify.db.fn.now() };

While Fastify's JSON schema validation restricts the body to `title` and `status`, if the schema validation is bypassed or misconfigured, a client could inject arbitrary column names (e.g., `id`, `project_id`, `created_at`). The same pattern exists in PUT /api/scenes/:id.

Best practice: explicitly pick allowed fields from request.body rather than spreading it.

Files: backend/src/routes/storyboards.js (update storyboard handler line 165, update scene handler line 311)

title: Bug: Scene deletion doesn't reindex remaining scenes — order_index gaps left behind
description:
DELETE /api/scenes/:id removes a scene but does not update the order_index of remaining scenes in the same storyboard. For example, deleting scene at order_index=1 from [0,1,2] leaves scenes at [0,2]. While this doesn't break functionality (ordering by order_index still works), it creates ever-growing gaps and the order_index values become meaningless. This could cause issues if any code assumes contiguous order_index values (0 to N-1).

Files: backend/src/routes/storyboards.js (delete scene handler)

title: Bug: List storyboards does not verify project exists — returns empty array for nonexistent project
description:
GET /api/projects/:projectId/storyboards does not check whether the projectId refers to an existing project. If a client passes a non-existent UUID, the endpoint returns an empty array (200 OK) instead of a 404 Not Found. This inconsistency with the create endpoint (which does check project existence) can confuse clients — they cannot distinguish between "project has no storyboards" and "project doesn't exist."

Files: backend/src/routes/storyboards.js (list storyboards handler)

title: Bug: Scene createScene request body uses camelCase but backend expects snake_case
description: False positive — the APIClient uses .convertToSnakeCase encoding strategy which correctly converts camelCase struct fields to snake_case JSON keys. No bug here.

title: Bug: Empty string project in list_proposals does not override default scoping
description: In list_proposals, the condition `if (!resolvedProject && extra.sessionId)` treats empty string as falsy, so passing project="" falls through to the agent's session project context. Empty string should either be rejected or treated as an explicit "no filter" override — current behavior is inconsistent with the documented override semantics.

title: Bug: iOS BeadRowView/KanbanCardView .task won't re-fire when bead status changes to active
description:
In ios/Adjutant/Features/Beads/Views/BeadRowView.swift line 45-57 and ios/Adjutant/Features/Beads/Views/KanbanCardView.swift line 43-55, the .task modifier fetches cost only for active beads (in_progress/hooked). However, SwiftUI's .task only fires on view appear or identity change, NOT on property changes.

If a bead's status changes from "open" to "in_progress" while the view is already visible (e.g., list refreshes with new data), the .task won't re-fire because the view's identity (bead.id) hasn't changed. The cost will remain nil until the view is recreated (e.g., scroll off-screen and back).

Fix: Use `.task(id: bead.status)` to re-fire the task when the bead status changes, or use `.onChange(of: bead.status)` to trigger a re-fetch.
close_reason: All merged to main, build passing

title: Bug: beadCosts state in BeadsList accumulates stale entries, never clears
description:
In frontend/src/components/beads/BeadsList.tsx line 335, the cost fetch merges results: `setBeadCosts((prev) => ({ ...prev, ...results }))`. Old entries are never removed.

If a bead transitions from in_progress to closed, its cost entry stays in the beadCosts map forever. Over a long session with many bead transitions, this map grows unbounded. More importantly, if the statusFilter changes (e.g., user switches from "default" to "closed"), the displayed costs may show stale data from when beads were previously active.

Fix: Reset beadCosts when searchedBeads changes significantly, or replace the merge with a full replacement of the cost map on each fetch cycle.

title: Bug: Budget form silently fails on error
description: In CostPanel.tsx, handleSetBudget catches errors but shows nothing to the user: `catch { // Budget creation failed silently }`. Same pattern for handleDeleteBudget. Users click SET BUDGET, nothing happens, no feedback. Add error state display for budget operations — at minimum a red error message below the form.

title: Product: Display cost confidence/precision indicators
description: Cost numbers are displayed as exact figures ($47.32) but they are estimates derived from polling a third-party tool's display. Users (and auditors) need to know the precision level. Add confidence indicators: "~$47 (estimated)" for statusline-derived costs vs "$47.32 (verified)" for JSONL/API-reconciled costs. Also add a tooltip or info icon explaining the data source and margin of error. This builds trust and sets appropriate expectations.
close_reason: All sub-tasks completed and merged to main

title: Product: Cost projections and trend analysis
description: The burn rate calculation exists but isn't used for projections. Add: (1) "At current burn rate, this epic will cost $X to complete" based on remaining tasks, (2) cost trend chart (sparkline or mini line chart) showing spend over time, (3) estimated completion cost based on % complete vs cost so far. The burn rate trend detection (increasing/stable/decreasing) is a good foundation — extend it into actionable projections.

title: Product: Cost export and invoice generation
description: No export capability exists. For business use, users need: (1) CSV export of cost data with date range, per-agent, per-bead filtering, (2) summary invoice generation showing epic/task breakdown with totals, (3) API endpoint for programmatic access to historical cost data. Minimum viable: CSV download button on CostPanel with date range picker.

title: Product: JSONL session logs as authoritative cost source
description: Claude Code writes JSONL logs at ~/.claude/projects/*/sessions/*.jsonl with per-message token counts (input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens) and model info. This is far more granular and accurate than the statusline-scraping approach.
